# Changelog

All notable changes to security-atlas are recorded here. Format follows
[Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/); versioning follows
[SemVer](https://semver.org/).

Release sections below are auto-generated by release-please from
Conventional-Commit messages. Each entry links to its slice issue, the
merging PR, and the squash-merge commit. For richer per-slice narrative
see the corresponding `docs/issues/<NNN>-*.md` and the PR body.

## Unreleased

### Added

- **feat(auth): slice 508 — SCIM 2.0 user-lifecycle provisioning (closes 508).** Ships an inbound **SCIM 2.0** (RFC 7643 / RFC 7644) provisioning surface so an org's IdP (Okta / Entra) can **automatically deprovision** a security-atlas user on offboard, closing the offboard→revocation window that slice 374's manual access-review cadence can only narrow. New `internal/scim` domain + `internal/api/scim` handlers expose `/scim/v2/Users` (Create / Get / List with `filter=userName eq "x"` / Replace / Patch / Delete) plus the discovery endpoints (`ServiceProviderConfig` / `ResourceTypes` / `Schemas`), all rendering RFC-7644 `application/scim+json`. **Auth is the load-bearing part:** a new per-tenant, SCIM-scoped, admin-issued, **revocable** bearer credential (`scim_credentials`, HMAC-SHA256-at-rest, mirroring `api_keys`) authenticates **only** the `/scim/v2` subtree — it is a distinct credential type that **cannot call any `/v1` platform API and cannot mint an atlas human session** (P0-508-2), enforced by mounting SCIM outside the `/v1` JWT/authz/tenancy chain behind its own auth middleware. **Deprovision is disable, not delete** (P0-508-1 / AC-4): `active=false` (via Patch or Replace) and DELETE both **soft-disable** the user (`users.active=false`, `status='disabled'`) **and revoke all their sessions** (via `internal/auth` session revocation), retaining the row so the actor's historical records survive (invariant #2). **SCIM never assigns or escalates a role** (P0-508-3 / AC-7): the attribute allow-list is identity + `active` only; a `roles` op in a Patch/Create is silently dropped (proven by both a unit `planPatch` test and an integration test asserting `user_roles` stays empty). Every mutation writes an append-only `scim_audit_log` row (AC-5). All reads/writes are **RLS-confined to the credential's tenant** (P0-508-4 / AC-6) — a two-tenant integration test against real Postgres proves a Tenant-A token cannot read or mutate a Tenant-B user (cross-tenant Get/Patch return not-found, no oracle). Admin control plane: `POST`/`GET /v1/admin/scim-credentials` + `DELETE …/{id}` (admin-gated issue/list/revoke; bearer returned once). Migration `20260612020000_scim_provisioning.sql` adds `scim_credentials` + `scim_audit_log` (four-policy FORCE RLS / append-only respectively) + additive `users.active` / `users.scim_external_id` / `users.scim_managed` columns. **Group→role mapping + the SCIM `Group` resource are explicitly OUT of scope — deferred to slice 509.** Coverage floors: `internal/scim` 67, `internal/api/scim` 68, `internal/api/adminscim` 58. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the SCIM-attribute→atlas-user mapping, the bearer-scope isolation, and the deprovision-as-disable semantics recorded in `docs/audit-log/508-scim-provisioning-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/508-scim-user-lifecycle-provisioning.md`.

- **feat(checklist): slice 471 — role-scoped control-implementation checklist v0, cited + non-binding (closes 471).** The 5th AI-assist v0 surface (alongside 440/441/444). For the tenant's in-scope control set it generates a **role-scoped implementation checklist** — one section per team role (`infra`/`engineering`/`security`) plus an honest `unassigned` bucket — where each section enumerates the concrete, cited to-do items that team must execute to satisfy its controls. The **which-control → which-role split is DETERMINISTIC** (`internal/checklist` `AssignRole`: a table-driven `owner_role` + `applicability_expr` normalization map, exhaustively unit-tested), **never an LLM guess** — the LLM's only job is turning each in-scope control's text into 1..N imperative task statements via local **Ollama** (`internal/llm` `SurfaceChecklist`; P0-471-5 local-only). **Strict mandatory-citation gate** (mirroring slice 441): every generated item must cite its control id (and may cite the control's SCF anchor + a linked policy id), each **grounded** in that control's reference set AND **resolving to a real tenant-owned row under RLS**; a SINGLE unresolvable citation **suppresses the whole role section** and persists nothing (P0-471-2/P0-471-4). A control with no evidence backing is rendered as an explicit **"no evidence yet"** gap item, never as satisfied (AC-6, no fabricated coverage). Migration `20260612010000_role_scoped_checklist.sql` adds `checklist_sections` (the per-role **approvable** unit, carrying the AI-assist boundary column set + **ADOPTING the slice-498 shared `ai_assist_human_approver_guard` CHECK** — predicate reused, not re-authored) + `checklist_items` (the cited tasks, with a non-empty-citations CHECK), both under four-policy FORCE RLS (invariant #6). The full forensic record rides the slice-498 `ai_generations` ledger (`surface='checklist'`). **One-click per-section approval** (`POST /v1/controls/checklist/sections/{id}:approve`, server-derived approver, no auto-approve path); the **markdown export renders only approved sections** and refuses a draft with zero approvals (P0-471-1, AC-11). New role-gated verbs `POST /v1/controls/checklist:generate` + `GET …/{id}` + the approve/export routes + their BFFs; a `/controls/checklist` page renders the role-sectioned cited draft, per-section approve, and an approval-gated export, all clearly labelled "AI-assisted draft — review before use". **Cross-tenant isolation proven** by integration tests against real Postgres — a Tenant-B generation reads/cites zero Tenant-A rows (AC-8, P0-471-3), the shared guard + provenance CHECKs fire, and the approval flow records the approver. Coverage floors: `internal/checklist` 81, `internal/api/checklist` 71, `web/lib/checklist/checklist-view.ts` 98. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the role-assignment heuristic, task-breakdown prompt shape, citation strictness, the slice-498 guard adoption, and the CI model-stubbing approach recorded in `docs/audit-log/471-role-scoped-checklist-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/471-role-scoped-control-checklist-generator.md`.

- **feat(questionnaire): slice 441 — AI-answer suggestion v0, cited drafts + one-click approve (closes 441).** The platform's **first AI-write surface**: for one questionnaire row the operator can generate a **cited DRAFT answer** from existing evidence/policy and approve it one click at a time. New `internal/qaisuggest` package — a **keyword first-pass** retrieval (P0-441-5, NO pgvector) over the tenant's `approved`/`published` policies (title/body match) and passing evidence (control-title match), bounded + ranked in-memory; a local-**Ollama** generation via `internal/llm` (P0-441-6, no cloud routing in v0); and a **strict mandatory-citation gate** (mirroring slice 444) — every cited id must be both **grounded** (in the candidate set shown to the model) AND **resolve to a real tenant-owned row under RLS**, or the WHOLE draft is suppressed and **nothing is persisted** (P0-441-2/P0-441-4). When no candidate backs the question the surface returns **"insufficient evidence — answer manually"** (AC-5, the no-fabricated-coverage path) rather than a fabricated answer. A valid draft persists as `ai_assisted=TRUE`, `human_approved=FALSE`, `human_approver=NULL`; **approval is a separate one-click action** recording the approver (no auto-approve path; P0-441-1). Migration `20260612000000_questionnaire_answer_ai_columns.sql` ALTERs `questionnaire_answers` with the boundary column set + **ADOPTS the slice-498 shared `ai_assist_human_approver_guard` CHECK** (the predicate is reused, not re-authored), making `human_approved` without a `human_approver` impossible at the DB layer (P0-441-8). New role-gated platform verbs `POST /v1/questionnaires/{id}/answers/{qid}/ai-suggest` + `…/ai-approve` (grc_engineer/admin) + their BFF; the slice-263 review UI gains an AI-suggest panel (cited draft → inline edit → approve, **approve disabled while a citation is unresolved**, visible banner if a future cloud route is used). **Cross-tenant isolation proven** by integration tests — a Tenant-B suggestion cannot cite, resolve, or retrieve a Tenant-A record (AC-16, P0-441-3, invariant #6). Coverage floors: `internal/qaisuggest` 79, `internal/api/questionnaires` held; `web/lib/questionnaire/ai-suggest.ts` 98. **Type: JUDGMENT** — retrieval strategy, citation strictness, the insufficient-evidence threshold, draft shape, and the slice-498 guard adoption recorded in `docs/audit-log/441-questionnaire-ai-v0-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/441-questionnaire-ai-answer-suggestion-v0.md`.
- **feat(oscal): slice 414 — cosign-keyless OSCAL signing, opt-in private Sigstore (closes 414).** Phase 2 of the [ADR-0010](docs/adr/0010-oscal-cosign-signing.md) signing plan, per [ADR-0016](docs/adr/0016-oidc-identity-for-keyless-signing.md): adds the **`cosign-keyless`** OSCAL-export-bundle signing mode — Fulcio-issued short-lived cert + Rekor transparency-log entry — as an **OPT-IN** mode for the **private-Sigstore deployment shape**, using atlas's scoped `client:oscal-signer` OIDC identity (slice 188) federated into an **operator-run PRIVATE Sigstore** (Fulcio + Rekor, operator-controlled trust root). **Defaults are unchanged** — the air-gap `docker-compose` default stays `embedded-ed25519` (P0-414-2) and the connected/SaaS GA default stays `cosign-kms` (P0-414-3); keyless is never inferred and never targets public Fulcio for the runtime surface (options a/d rejected). The mode resolves only when explicitly selected (`ATLAS_OSCAL_SIGNING_MODE=cosign-keyless`) AND both `ATLAS_COSIGN_FULCIO_URL` + `ATLAS_COSIGN_REKOR_URL` are set. The signer obtains atlas's OIDC token → exchanges it at the operator's Fulcio for a signing cert → signs the deterministic bundle digest → uploads to the operator's Rekor → records the cert + identity + issuer (parsed from the issued cert) + Rekor log index + private endpoints in a new `keyless` manifest sub-object (`omitempty` — non-keyless/pre-414 manifests are byte-identical to before). Keyless verification dispatches via cert-identity + transparency-log inclusion against the operator's trust root, failing closed for a verifier that cannot check the keyless surface. **Implementation reuses the Phase-1 cosign-as-binary wrapper** (`internal/oscal/cosign`) — zero new `go.mod` dependencies (no sigstore-go). **Test strategy:** Fulcio cert issuance + Rekor upload are faked at the cosign-client boundary (a fake runner that writes a real test-CA cert + emits a Rekor index; a fake `IdentityTokenSource`), so the sign→verify→manifest→on-disk round-trip is exercised in unit + integration tests WITHOUT a live Sigstore. Coverage floors held (`internal/oscal` ≥69, `internal/oscal/cosign` ≥88). **Type: JUDGMENT** — library choice, OIDC-identity plumbing, manifest shape, and opt-in config surface recorded in `docs/audit-log/414-cosign-keyless-signing-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/414-cosign-keyless-oscal-signing-phase-2.md`.
- **feat(web): slice 670 — pre-GA copy & metadata pass (closes 670).** A coordinated pass over five low-risk user-facing copy/metadata defects from the 2026-06-10 empty-tenant UI audit. **ATLAS-016:** fixes the typo "prior- answer" → "prior-answer" on the questionnaires header (`web/app/(authed)/questionnaires/page.tsx`). **ATLAS-012:** the control-detail 404 empty state now names the SCF anchor's human-readable code (e.g. "AAA-01") instead of the raw anchor UUID — it resolves the code via the same `/api/anchors/{id}/requirements` BFF the catalog detail page uses, falling back to the id when the lookup is pending or the anchor does not resolve (`web/app/(authed)/controls/[id]/page.tsx`). **ATLAS-011:** the breadcrumb section map gains `oscal → "Vendor Claims"` so the Vendor Claims route no longer humanizes the raw segment to "Oscal" (`web/lib/page-names.ts`), and the control-detail page-level breadcrumb now renders a leaf crumb ("Controls › AAA-01") instead of a bare back-link. **ATLAS-010 / ATLAS-040:** every primary nav route now carries the consistent "<Page> · security-atlas" document title that `/settings` established (sibling server-component title layouts for audits, risks, oscal/component-definitions [Vendor Claims], controls, evidence, policies, vendors, calendar, board-packs, exceptions, questionnaires, activity, catalog/scf, dashboards/metrics; metadata added to the existing dashboard layout) — closing the `/audits`, `/oscal/component-definitions`, and `/risks` (raw-URL-title regression) gaps the audit named. **ATLAS-017:** sweeps user-facing empty-state + helper copy for build internals ("slice 005/006", "future slice", "follow-up slice", "canvas §4.5", the internal `policy_control_links` table name) and the raw `/v1/controls:upload-bundle` action path, replacing each with user-facing language; legitimate documented platform-API signposts (`POST /v1/policies`, `POST /v1/framework-scopes`) are kept because they are real next actions for the self-host operator, not build-internal leaks. Out-of-scope finding **ATLAS-035** (calendar/dashboard exception events print a raw control UUID — the title is built in backend SQL, so it needs a Go/query change) is filed as spillover slice 732. **Type: JUDGMENT** — copy authorship calls (especially the ATLAS-017 replacement wording and the ATLAS-012 anchor-code choice) recorded in `docs/audit-log/670-pre-ga-copy-metadata-pass-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/670-pre-ga-copy-metadata-pass.md`.
- **feat(security): slice 449 — OPA 1.17 regression gate (closes 449).** Backfills the deliberate regression gate the OPA 1.4 → 1.17 embedded-engine bump called for, after the bump itself landed via dependabot auto-merge (#953, `02bdea55`) WITHOUT it. No `go.mod` re-bump and no `.rego` policy-logic change (the policies were already on `rego.v1`); this slice adds the assertions that lock in 1.17 behavior on the single load-bearing engine for both authz decisions and control evaluation. Added: a fail-closed STRIDE-S gate (`internal/authz/slice449_test.go`) — an absent/zero-value subject and malformed/non-canonical identity role claims DENY under 1.17 across write/approve/read/upload-bundle; a regocache correctness contract (`internal/eval/regocache/slice449_test.go`) — a cached prepared query yields the SAME control verdict as a fresh uncached prepare (the correctness complement to the slice-377 perf benchmark, re-measured at ~6.35× cached-vs-uncached under 1.17 and documented in the decisions log rather than asserted as a flaky wall-clock gate); and a STRIDE-T bundle-validation gate (`internal/authz/slice449_bundle_test.go`) — a syntactically malformed bundle and an oversized permissive bundle are still rejected (prior bundle stays installed, fail-closed). The Rego-semantics diff verdict (no outcome change for atlas's two query shapes across 1.5..1.17) is recorded in `docs/audit-log/449-opa-1-17-embedded-engine-upgrade-decisions.md`. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Supersedes #953. Slice doc: `docs/issues/449-opa-1-17-embedded-engine-upgrade.md`.
- **feat(vendor): slice 688 — vendor_reviews ledger (closes 688).** Lands the append-only `vendor_reviews` ledger (migration `20260611000000_vendor_reviews_ledger.sql`) — one immutable row per completed vendor review (`reviewed_at`, `reviewer`, `outcome` ∈ {pass, pass_with_findings, fail, waived}, `notes`) under the four-policy RLS set mirroring `vendors` byte-for-byte (invariant #6; `audit-rls.sh` green). The migration back-fills exactly one ledger row per pre-existing non-null `last_review_date` so no history is lost (AC-2), and the store keeps `vendors.last_review_date` consistent with the most-recent ledger row on each recorded review (a back-dated review never regresses the scalar). New read + write platform verbs `GET`/`POST /v1/vendors/{id}/reviews` (RLS-scoped via `internal/vendor` store; a cross-tenant read yields an empty series, a cross-tenant write maps to `ErrVendorNotFound` via the invisible composite FK) and their BFF (`web/app/api/vendors/[id]/reviews`). The slice-686 read-only detail page's "Review history" placeholder card now renders a real per-review timeline (date + outcome Badge + reviewer + notes, newest-first), falling back to an honest scalar message when the ledger is empty; a "Record review" affordance routes to a dedicated `/vendors/{id}/reviews/new` form (the slice-686 read/edit split — write affordances live on their own route). The post-recording refresh concern (slice 424) is explicitly left to slice 691. **Type: JUDGMENT** — ledger shape (D1), `last_review_date` consistency + back-fill (D2), and history-section UX (D3) recorded in `docs/audit-log/688-vendor-reviews-ledger-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/688-vendor-reviews-ledger.md`.
- **docs(adr): slice 455 — OIDC-identity strategy for cosign-keyless OSCAL-bundle signing (closes 455).** Decision-only JUDGMENT spike (no production code — P0-455-1) that resolves the secondary OIDC-identity decision [ADR-0010](docs/adr/0010-oscal-cosign-signing.md) explicitly deferred and unblocks the keyless leg (slice 414 / 368b). New [ADR-0016](docs/adr/0016-oidc-identity-for-keyless-signing.md) surveys five identity options — (a) GitHub-Actions-OIDC (fenced off as slice 451's _release-artifact_ surface, NOT the runtime OSCAL surface — P0-455-5), (b) per-deployment federated issuer, (c) operator-run private Sigstore, (d) public-Sigstore-root onboarding, (e) don't-adopt-keyless — each with its runtime requirements, the exact identity Fulcio certifies + who can mint it, what becomes publicly visible, failure modes, and self-host-solo-vs-SaaS fit. **Recommendation: ADOPT-DEFERRED, confidence HIGH** — build `cosign-keyless` as an **opt-in** mode using atlas's scoped `client:oscal-signer` identity (slice 188) federated into an operator-run private Sigstore (option (b)-on-(c); options a/d rejected — structurally mismatched / governance-uncertain / broadly-mintable per P0-455-3), while keeping `cosign-kms` + `embedded-ed25519` (option e) the default for ALL primary deployment shapes (ADR-0010 default table preserved — NOT a SaaS default flip). The local-first "no data leaves deployment" constraint is honored (private Rekor keeps cert/log inside the deployment); the weaker external-auditor transparency trade-off is accepted because kms mode already banks the stock-`cosign verify-blob` win. Flips slice 414 `not-ready` → `ready` with scope tightened to opt-in private-Sigstore keyless, and updates the 368 parent doc's 368b BLOCKER line to cite ADR-0016. Slice doc: `docs/issues/455-oidc-identity-strategy-decision-spike.md`.
- **connectors: slice 443 — Slack connector (closes 443).** Ships one vertical Slack connector (`connectors/slack/`) following the slice-004 AWS connector pattern (register-per-run, stable `actor_id`, hour-truncated `observed_at`, scope minimums, vendor-native auth). It collects three high-signal evidence surfaces for the SaaS-startup persona that runs operations in Slack — workspace **member roster** (access evidence: `slack.workspace_member.v1`, anchors IAC-01/IAC-06), **admin audit-log** entries (admin-action evidence: `slack.admin_audit_event.v1`, anchor MON-01), and **message-retention settings** (data-retention evidence: `slack.retention_settings.v1`, anchors DCH-01/DCH-03) — and pushes each record to the single `EvidenceIngestService.Push` API (invariant #3; `profiles_supported=[pull]` describes source-side retrieval only, platform wire stays push). **Over-collection is structurally impossible** (threat-model I): the connector reads membership/admin/retention METADATA only — never message content, DMs, or channel history; the `slackapi` adapter has no message-reading call site, the required Slack scopes exclude every message-read scope, and two build-failing guards (`TestNoMessageContentField` reflection guard + `TestDoRun_NoMessageContentInPayload` payload guard) fail the build if a content field is ever added. The least-privilege read-only Slack OAuth token is source-side and redacted in every `fmt` verb (`TestTokenNeverLogged`); it is never logged or sent to the platform. Three JSON Schemas land under `internal/api/schemaregistry/schemas/slack.*/` and register in `DefaultSeed()` (append-only). JUDGMENT decisions log: `docs/audit-log/443-slack-connector-decisions.md`.
- **test: slice 704 — contract-tier tenant-wide `/v1/evidence` ledger window (closes 704).** Extends the slice-692 `internal/api/controldetail` Evidence read seam to the TENANT-WIDE branch (`GET /v1/evidence` with no `control_id` — the slice-106 + slice-234 filter-matrix window: kind/result/source-actor/scope-cell filters + keyset pagination), draining the branch slice 692 D3 deferred. The `evidenceWindowReader` seam gains `EvidencePaged`; the handler's tenant-wide branch now reads through `h.evidence` (was `h.store`) so the wire shape records on the plain `go test ./...` unit surface with no Postgres pool (ADR-0007 / P0-409-1). A new provider recorder (`TestContract_TenantWideEvidence`) pins the tenant-wide envelope — `control_id` is the empty string on this branch — across three variants: `populated` (fully-populated + fully-nulled rows), `filtered` (recorded behind a non-empty kind/result/scope_cell_id/window predicate to pin the filter-matrix request surface), and `empty` (count 0 / total > 0, slice 236). New golden `web/lib/contracts/evidence-tenant-window.golden.json` + consumer assert `evidence-tenant-window.contract.test.ts` (toEqual verbatim-passthrough BFF, `web/app/api/evidence/route.ts`). JUDGMENT decisions log at `docs/audit-log/704-contract-tier-tenant-wide-evidence-decisions.md`. Public constructor `controldetail.New(*Store)` unchanged (P0-409-2). **Zero new CI gate** — no `ci.yml` change; rides the existing Go-unit + Frontend vitest surfaces (AC-4).
- **oscal: slice 457 — browser download surface for the OSCAL signed-export bundle (closes 457).** The operator can now download the signed OSCAL bundle (SSP/AP/AR/POA&M + the slice-413 signing manifest) directly from `/audits` — superseding the slice-217 future-state disclosure, which becomes the working action it signposted. A new platform verb `POST /v1/audit-periods/{id}/oscal-export:download` (`internal/api/oscalexport/handler.go`) runs the SAME tenant-scoped export as `POST .../oscal-export` (same Exporter, same RLS-bound reads under the request's `app.current_tenant`, same error mapping) and serves the identical signed envelope as a `Content-Disposition: attachment` artifact with a deterministic filename (`oscal-bundle-<period>-<frozen-date>.json`) + `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`. A BFF download route (`web/app/api/audits/[id]/oscal-export/route.ts`) bridges a browser-friendly `GET` (native `<a download>`) to the upstream POST, forwarding the bearer and streaming the bundle bytes verbatim; it never accepts a `tenant_id` from the client. Every frozen-period row on `/audits` carries a working "Export OSCAL bundle" link (invariant #10 — only frozen periods are exportable). The headline cross-tenant download-isolation property (invariant #6 — a Tenant-B request must NOT download Tenant-A's bundle) is pinned at the `internal/oscal` integration tier (`TestExport_CrossTenantPeriodIsNotExportable`, real Postgres + RLS) and a `page.waitForEvent("download")` e2e (`web/e2e/oscal-export-e2e.spec.ts`) lands the literal download-event form slice 423 deferred. JUDGMENT decisions log: `docs/audit-log/457-oscal-download-surface-decisions.md`.

- **ci: slice 693 — CI pipeline efficiency + safety hardening, Tier 1 (closes 693).** Four independent, additive, low-risk changes from the pipeline-efficiency audit (`docs/audit-log/693-ci-pipeline-efficiency-tier1-decisions.md`). **(1) Runaway-job ceiling:** every job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (56 jobs) gains `timeout-minutes` (20 default; 30 for the integration-shard / playwright-family / self-host-bundle / trivy-image / main-canary heavy jobs) — previously the GitHub default of **360 minutes** meant a single hung Playwright/integration job could burn 6 runner-hours before being killed. **(2) Codecov noise:** a new root `codecov.yml` sets `require_changes: true` + `after_n_builds: 2` and marks the `project`/`patch` statuses `informational` — Codecov previously ran on defaults (comment on every PR; status could fire before both upload flags landed). The merged-coverage MERGE GATE stays enforced by `cmd/scripts/coverage-gate` (unchanged); this removes zero enforcement. **(3) Dependabot batching:** catch-all `minor`/`patch` groups added to the gomod, npm, both pip dirs, and docker ecosystems (`.github/dependabot.yml`) — previously ungrouped pip/docker bumps opened one PR per dep, each triggering the full CI matrix; majors stay ungrouped and security updates fire independently. **(4) pre-commit job caching:** the `precommit` job now sets `cache: true` / `cache: "npm"` on its Go/Node setup and caches `~/.cache/pre-commit` hook environments (new `actions/cache@<sha>` — first use of that action in the repo, SHA-pinned per slice 128; `cache-path-guard` unaffected since nothing is committed to the tree). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Tier-2/3 follow-ons filed as slices 694–703. Slice doc: `docs/issues/693-ci-pipeline-efficiency-tier1.md`.
- **test(contract): slice 692 — contract-tier controldetail attest-form + per-control Evidence window (closes 692).** Drains the two dbx-coupled control-detail reads slice 690 D3 deferred (each its own heavier seam over Postgres-coupled `dbx`-row types). Covers `GET /v1/controls/{id}/attest-form` (`internal/api/controls/attest.go` `AttestForm`, new 1-method `controlByIDReader` seam returning `dbx.GetControlByIDRow` incl. the JSONB `manual_evidence_schema`) and the **per-control** branch of `GET /v1/evidence?control_id=…` (`internal/api/controldetail` `Evidence`, new 2-method `evidenceWindowReader` seam = `EvidenceForControl` + `CountEvidenceForTenant`). Each provider recorder injects a fixed-row stub via an unexported `newAttestHandlerWithReader` / `newHandlerWithEvidenceReader`, so the wire shapes record on the plain `go test ./...` unit surface (**no DB, no integration tag — P0-409-1**); both public constructors are byte-unchanged (P0-409-2: `NewAttestHandler` + `controldetail.New`). New goldens (`web/lib/contracts/{control-attest-form,control-evidence}.golden.json`); both consumer halves are toEqual BFF-drives (both BFFs are verbatim passthroughs — `web/app/api/controls/[id]/attest-form/route.ts` and `web/app/api/evidence/route.ts`) plus a field-contract pin of the nullable matrix. The attest-form golden pins both `caller_can_attest` branches (owner/viewer); the evidence golden pins the full nullable matrix (`evidence_kind` / `scope_cell` / `source` null vs present) and the slice-236 `total != count` "filters narrowed to zero, ledger not empty" disambiguation. The non-manual-implementation 400 is pinned by a sibling unit test. Drift sensitivity proven on `control-attest-form` (an `AttestationVersion` mutation fails the Go recorder). **JUDGMENT (slice 692 D3):** the Evidence seam covers ONLY the per-control branch — the slice-106 tenant-wide ledger window (+ the slice-234 filter matrix) is deferred + spilled to **slice 704**. **Zero new CI gate** — rides the existing `Go · build + test` (unit) + `Frontend · vitest` surfaces (ADR-0007 (d)). Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/692-contract-tier-controldetail-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/692-contract-tier-controldetail-attest-form-evidence-window.md`.
- **feat(ai): slice 444 — AI gap-explanation v0 (plain-language, cited, non-binding) (closes 444).** The FIRST AI-assist surface and the lowest-risk one. `GET /v1/controls/{id}/gap-explanation` returns the DETERMINISTIC freshness rollup ALWAYS (the same freshness/evidence facts the control-detail page computes — reused from the slice-016 read-models, no new SQL, no migration), plus a plain-language, cited, **NON-BINDING** local-Ollama explanation of that rollup when one is available AND every citation resolves to a tenant-owned row. New domain package `internal/gapexplain` (rollup assembly + strict citation validation + suppression, all under `app.current_tenant` RLS — invariant #6) consuming the slice-498 `internal/llm` substrate (local Ollama only — no cloud routing, P0-444-5); new HTTP surface `internal/api/gapexplain` wired via the httpserver Mount-append. **Citation validation (AC-4, P0-444-1):** every cited evidence/control id is gated twice — an in-memory grounding gate (the model may only cite the control id + the cited-excerpt ids the rollup showed it) then an RLS-scoped tenant-ownership gate; a SINGLE unresolvable citation suppresses the whole explanation and the deterministic rollup renders alone (graceful degradation, AC-7, P0-444-7). **Cross-tenant isolation (AC-10, P0-444-2):** an integration test proves a Tenant-B explanation cannot cite a Tenant-A record — the Tenant-A id is invisible under Tenant B's RLS so it never resolves. **Non-binding (AC-5, P0-444-3):** rendered in the control-detail Overview view only, NO approve/publish/export path; **never persisted** (P0-444-4 — no `ai_generations` row; regenerated on demand; transparency met by surfacing the model name/version/provider in the response). Frontend: a self-contained Overview card (`web/components/control/gap-explanation.tsx`) over a node-testable view-model (`gap-explanation-view.ts`) carrying the disclosure + no-export contract, the BFF proxy route, and the lib types/fetchers. Tests: pure-Go unit (citation parse + strict-validation gate + service suppression), Go integration (valid render / fabricated-id suppression / **cross-tenant** suppression — all green against real Postgres + RLS, no live Ollama via the slice-498 `StubClient` CI seam), and a vitest for the disclosure / no-approve-export / graceful-degradation view-model. **Type: JUDGMENT** — explanation shape + citation strictness + no-persist call recorded in `docs/audit-log/444-gap-explanation-v0-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/444-ai-gap-explanation-v0.md`.
- **test(contract): slice 690 — contract-tier audit-workspace read-tail remainder (closes 690).** Drains the audit-workspace read tail slice 689 D4 deferred (the 411/412/687/689 bounded-cut discipline). Covers the three audit-workspace LIST reads that each extend an existing slice-689 per-route seam by one method and return clean domain structs: `GET /v1/samples/{id}/annotations` (`internal/api/audit`, `sampleReader` += `ListAnnotations`), `GET /v1/walkthroughs` list (`internal/api/walkthroughs`, `walkthroughReader` += `List`), and `GET /v1/audit-notes` legacy author-scoped list (`internal/api/auditnotes`, `threadReader` += `ListForAuthorAndPeriod`). Each provider recorder injects a fixed-row stub via the package's existing unexported `newHandlerWithReader`, so the wire shapes record on the plain `go test ./...` unit surface (**no DB, no integration tag — P0-409-1**). New goldens (`web/lib/contracts/{sample-annotations,walkthroughs-list,audit-notes-list}.golden.json`); all three consumer halves are FIELD-CONTRACT pins (slice 687 D3) — none of the three has a verbatim-passthrough GET BFF today (annotations is read by the sample-detail component, the walkthroughs list BFF is POST-only, and the legacy audit-notes list has no GET BFF — the workspace reads `/thread`). Drift sensitivity proven on `sample-annotations` (a `result`→`verdict` rename fails BOTH the Go provider half and the TypeScript field-contract half — the slice-210 class of catch). **Grill finding (slice 690 D3):** the GET attestation surface is `attest-form` only — there is no `GET /v1/controls/{id}/attestations` (`attestations` is POST `Submit`); the slice spec over-named it and that route was not invented to match. Public handler constructor signatures (`New(*audit.Store)` / `New(*walkthrough.Store, ArtifactUploader)` / `New(*notes.Store, *notifications.Store, *slog.Logger)`) are byte-for-byte unchanged (P0-409-2); only private seam shape grew by one method each. **Zero new CI gate** — rides the existing `Go · build + test` + `Frontend · vitest` jobs. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the two `dbx`-row-coupled control-detail reads (`attest-form` schema descriptor + the per-control Evidence ledger window's keyset-pagination + `CountEvidenceForTenant` two-method seam) are deferred to slice 692 on a documented bounded-cut rationale (each its own heavier seam-fixture shape). Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/690-contract-tier-audit-remainder-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/690-contract-tier-audit-workspace-read-tail-remainder.md`.
- **feat(catalog): slice 467 — ISO 27001:2022 full Annex A coverage completion (closes 467).** Completes the ISO/IEC 27001:2022 → SCF crosswalk to **all 93 Annex A controls** (A.5 organizational ×37, A.6 people ×8, A.7 physical ×14, A.8 technological ×34), extending the curated 36-control subset slice 438 shipped. Adds the remaining **57 controls** (+84 STRM-typed edges) to `data/crosswalks/iso27001-2022.yaml`, each mapped requirement → SCF anchor (invariant #7 — never requirement → requirement; the schema has no fw_to_fw table). Also **splits A.8.24 cryptography** into the three-edge pair the slice-438 decisions log flagged (`CRY-01` policy `equal` 0.9 + `CRY-08` in-transit `subset_of` 0.7 + `CRY-09` key-management `subset_of` 0.8), and re-checks the slice-438 low-confidence stopgaps: A.5.7 threat-intel was already re-mapped to the dedicated `THR-01` anchor upstream (slices 635/641/646); A.8.11 data-masking stays on `DCH-01` (+ a new `IAC-01` access-control facet edge) because the 62-anchor sample fixture **still has no dedicated masking anchor** — flagged LOW CONFIDENCE for re-map once a masking-family anchor is importable. The full crosswalk now carries **127 edges**; the invariant-#1 proof test observes `IAC-01` satisfying SOC 2 CC6.1 **and** five ISO controls through the single shared anchor (one control, N frameworks). **PURE DATA + tests — no code change** (the loader was generalized framework-agnostic by slice 438). Tests extended in place: the loader unit test lifts the curated `[30,45]` cap to assert **exactly 93** + a new per-theme cardinality + no-missing/no-extra/no-dup completeness guard; the integration suite gains a full-93 import + zero-orphan-requirement assertion and a slice-438-subset-preservation assertion (the original 36 controls still resolve to their original anchors). No SOC 2 / PCI / CSF / HIPAA regression (the shared soc2import suite stays green against a real Postgres). **Licensing:** identifiers + short titles (factual refs) + original agent-authored `body` descriptions only — no verbatim ISO standard text (P0-467-2); ships only the ISO→SCF edge data, SCF anchors remain operator-imported (P0-467-3). **Type: JUDGMENT** — each requirement→anchor mapping is a subjective control-equivalence call recorded with rationale + confidence (21 HIGH / 33 MEDIUM / 3 LOW among the new controls). Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/467-iso27001-annex-a-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/467-iso27001-full-annex-a-coverage.md`.
- **feat(notifications): slice 439 — evidence-staleness digest + alerting (honest interval) (closes 439).** The platform COMPUTED evidence staleness (`internal/freshness` + `internal/drift`) but nothing TOLD the operator — the biggest silent gap in v1 (the solo security leader won't discover stale evidence by chance, and the v1 binary test fails the moment they keep a side-spreadsheet of "what's about to expire"). This ships the missing **PRODUCER**: a scheduled rollup (`internal/staleness`, reusing the slice-076 `metrics/scheduler` cadence pattern — migrator pool enumerates tenants, app pool runs each tenant under `tenancy.WithTenant`) that reads the slice-016 freshness read model per tenant, classifies each control into stale / approaching / fresh bands (the threshold is `eval.FreshnessMaxAge`, never re-derived), and writes `evidence.staleness` notifications into the EXISTING slice-029 `notifications` store: a **per-control alert** on the stale crossing plus a **weekly digest** summarizing stale + approaching-stale counts with a capped top-10 list. The already-merged delivery channels (slice 445 email, slice 543 Slack/webhook, slice 582/583 digest scheduler) CONSUME these rows — the `evidence.staleness` type string is load-bearing across `internal/notify` `typeLabels`/`kindToEvent` and `internal/auth/userprefs`. **HONEST INTERVAL (P0-439-1; canvas §1.6 anti-pattern):** the copy names the cadence verbatim ("Staleness is recomputed on a schedule (every 6 hours)"; "Generated every Monday at 09:00 UTC") and NEVER says "continuous monitoring" / "real-time" — a pure-Go test AND a vitest both assert the banned substrings are absent and the interval IS named. **TENANT ISOLATION (P0-439-2; threat-model I — the load-bearing AC):** the rollup runs per-tenant under `app.current_tenant`; recipient enumeration (`ListActiveUsersForTenant`) and both writes go through the tenant GUC + 4-policy FORCE RLS `WITH CHECK`; a cross-tenant-isolation integration test proves Tenant A's stale evidence NEVER appears in Tenant B's notifications (invariant #6). **IDEMPOTENT (AC-5/AC-12):** the one new migration adds `staleness_rollup_log`, a tenant-scoped claim ledger with `UNIQUE (tenant_id, recipient_user_id, dedup_key)` + `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` — a re-run for the same recompute period / ISO-week is a no-op (proven by an idempotency integration test). **OPT-OUT (AC-7):** the existing per-kind `userprefs` (event `evidence_staleness` × channel `in_app`, default-on) suppresses delivery on explicit opt-out — zero new prefs surface (P0-439-7). **READ-ONLY (P0-439-3; invariant #2):** the rollup consumes the freshness read model and writes only notifications + the dedup ledger — never the evidence ledger. The scheduler is wired into `cmd/atlas` (`ATLAS_STALENESS_INTERVAL` override) and enrolled in the sharded integration job (leg B3). Frontend: the dashboard's `EvidenceFreshnessPanel` gains the `#evidence-freshness` anchor (AC-9 deep-link target) and a node-testable presentation helper (`web/lib/api/staleness-notification.ts` + vitest) maps the new kind to a label + the freshness link + honest copy (AC-8). Email delivery (after slice 445), per-control thresholds, and a full prefs page are out of scope (follow-ons). **Type: JUDGMENT** (threshold-rollup shape + digest/alert copy). Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/439-staleness-digest-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/439-evidence-staleness-digest-alerting.md`.
- **test(contract): slice 689 — contract-tier audit-workspace read tail (closes 689).** Drains the audit-workspace read tail slice 687 D4 deferred (the 411/412/687 bounded-cut discipline). Covers the four single-resource audit-workspace reads that each have a SHIPPED verbatim-passthrough GET BFF: `GET /v1/populations/{id}` + `GET /v1/samples/{id}` (`internal/api/audit`, new two-method `sampleReader` seam), `GET /v1/walkthroughs/{id}` (`internal/api/walkthroughs`, new one-method `walkthroughReader` seam), and `GET /v1/audit-notes/thread` (`internal/api/auditnotes`, new one-method `threadReader` seam — the route with the real `web/app/api/audit/audit-notes/thread/route.ts` BFF). Each provider recorder injects a fixed-row stub via an unexported `newHandlerWithReader`, so the wire shapes record on the plain `go test ./...` unit surface (**no DB, no integration tag — P0-409-1**). New goldens (`web/lib/contracts/{population-get,sample-get,walkthrough-get,audit-notes-thread}.golden.json`); all four consumer halves are full verbatim-passthrough BFF drives (`toEqual`) since every targeted route has a shipped BFF consumer. Drift sensitivity proven on `population-get` (a `row_count` rename fails BOTH halves — the slice-210 class of catch). **Grill finding (slice 689 D2):** the slice spec named "`GET /v1/populations` (list) + `GET /v1/samples` (list)"; the `internal/api/audit` package has NO list-GET routes — the real read surface is the single-resource `{id}` GET, which this slice covers. Public handler constructor signatures (`New(*audit.Store)` / `New(*walkthrough.Store, ArtifactUploader)` / `New(*notes.Store, *notifications.Store, *slog.Logger)`) are byte-for-byte unchanged (P0-409-2); only private struct shape changed. **Zero new CI gate** — rides the existing `Go · build + test` + `Frontend · vitest` jobs. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the sample-annotation list read, the walkthroughs + legacy audit-notes list reads, the attestation routes, the controldetail per-control Evidence window, and the audit-period passthrough half are deferred to slice 690 on a documented bounded-cut rationale. Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/689-contract-tier-audit-workspace-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/689-contract-tier-audit-workspace-read-tail.md`.
- **test(cosign): slice 425 — LocalStack KMS round-trip integration test for `awskms://` signing (closes 425).** Closes the slice-413 / ADR-0010 Phase-1 "revisit": the `cosign-kms` mode's integration test used a **local-key stand-in** that mirrors the `sign-blob` argv but never drives the real `awskms://` provider path in `signblob.go` (a local path cannot pass `validateKMSRef`). This adds an **opt-in** integration test (`internal/oscal/cosign/cosign_kms_localstack_integration_test.go`, `//go:build integration`) that drives the REAL `Client.SignBlob` / `Client.VerifyBlob` (`awskms:///alias/...`) against a hermetic **LocalStack KMS** sandbox: it creates an ephemeral `ECC_NIST_P256` / `SIGN_VERIFY` key, asserts `verify-blob` **succeeds** for the unmodified blob and **fails** for a mutated one (tamper-evidence proven, not assumed), and asserts no credential material leaks into cosign's captured output. The test is gated behind `ATLAS_COSIGN_KMS_LOCALSTACK=1` (+ endpoint env) and `t.Skip`s with a note when the gate, `cosign`, the `aws` CLI, or LocalStack are absent — so the default `Go · integration` job stays green either way (it never becomes a hard CI gate). LocalStack is wired into the integration harness as an **optional** `docker run` dependency on the shard that owns `internal/oscal/cosign` (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, leg B3), following the project's non-default-CMD optional-service convention (community-edition `localstack/localstack:3.8`, `SERVICES=kms`, no license). The LocalStack endpoint is forwarded to the cosign subprocess via the existing `WithExtraEnvKeys` allowlist seam — **no change to `signblob.go` behavior** (P0-425-4). Fixtures carry LocalStack dummy creds only — no real key material, no vendor-prefixed secrets (GitGuardian-safe). The slice-413 local-key + embedded round-trip + no-tlog tests are left intact. Runbook gains a "Testing the KMS path locally" section. **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/425-localstack-kms-cosign-roundtrip-integration.md`.
- **test(e2e): slice 424 — vendor-review workflow end-to-end (closes 424).** Vendor reviews are a v1-binary criterion (the solo security leader runs them alone) but had **no vendor-review e2e** — the closest spec (`web/e2e/audit-periods-vendors-export.spec.ts`) only tests the export path's email-masking, never the review workflow itself. A new Playwright spec (`web/e2e/vendor-review-workflow.spec.ts`) drives ONE workflow path end-to-end: **vendor list → open the vendor review (detail) → perform the review (record the review date) → the derived review status transitions from "overdue" to "on time"**. **(AC-1)** the list renders the seeded tenant's vendor (name + domain as separate, un-concatenated values per slice 679). **(AC-2)** opening the review surfaces the review state — the derived status badge, the owner contact (`mailto:`), and the review cadence. **(AC-3)** the meaningful interaction is **recording a vendor review**: v1 has no operator-mutable status widget and no questionnaire link on the vendor surface ("Phase 2 adds questionnaire issuance"), so the operator's "I reviewed this vendor" action is setting the edit form's Last-review date and saving. The spec asserts the **deterministic** consequences: the PATCH request shape is **transform-aware** (the form's `normalizeForSubmit` maps emptied optionals to `null`, never `""`) and the save **navigates back** to the read-only review surface, which re-renders. The spec does **not** assert the derived status badge flipping `overdue` -> `on time`, because v1 does not `invalidateQueries(["vendor", id])` after the mutation — the detail + edit pages share that query key, so the badge flips only on a refetch the app does not guarantee; asserting it would assert non-contractual behavior. That missing post-record auto-refresh is filed as spillover slice 691, which restores the status-flip assertion once the app guarantees the refresh. **(AC-4)** `/v1/vendors*` has **no contract golden**, so the typed slice-394 `GoldenEndpoint` union mechanically forbids `fulfillFromGolden` for it; per the e2e README escape-hatch the mock bodies are hand-written, typed against the `Vendor` producer type (slice 276 lesson). **(AC-5 — tenant-isolation headline)** the spec mocks exactly one tenant's vendor and asserts a single-tenant render (`vendor-name` count === 1); the cross-tenant DENY stays a Go-tier RLS assertion (invariant #6). **(AC-6)** the spec is fully route-mocked (hermetic per slice 594) — no docker-compose seed precondition. **(AC-7)** auto-enrolled in the `Frontend · Playwright e2e` CI job. **(AC-8)** the existing export spec's email-masking assertions are left intact. **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/424-vendor-review-workflow-e2e.md`.
- **test(contract): slice 687 — contract-tier tail remaining (`ucfcoverage` + audit-workspace reads) (closes 687).** Drains the contract-tier tail slice 412 deferred — most load-bearingly `GET /v1/controls/{id}/coverage` (`internal/api/ucfcoverage`), the route slice 412 D5 deferred on a seam-cost judgment (its honest Option-A seam looked like a 6+-method `dbx.Queries` mirror plus an `inTenantTx` fake plus the three slice-256 coverage stores). This slice takes D5's own suggested path: a **single-method `coverageAssembler` read seam** that returns the assembled `(control, anchor, requirements)` view, splitting the read from the serialization so the three interesting forks (anchored/unanchored + pinned/unpinned) — which live in the handler, not in any store method — record faithfully through the REAL `ControlCoverage` handler on the plain `go test ./...` unit surface (**no DB, no integration tag — P0-409-1**). Also extends the slice-411 `auditperiods` seam with a two-method `periodReader` for `GET /v1/audit-periods/{id}` (Get) + `/control-state`. New goldens (`web/lib/contracts/control-coverage.golden.json` + `audit-period-get.golden.json` + `audit-period-control-state.golden.json`); the `/coverage` consumer half is a full verbatim-passthrough BFF drive (`toEqual` + the `coverage: number|null` field contract, slice 256 P0-1 — null must NOT degrade to 0), e2e-mocked by `web/e2e/control-detail-tabs.spec.ts`. The two audit-period routes have **no current BFF consumer** (the workspace reads caller-scoped `/v1/me/audit-period`), so their consumer halves are **field-contract pins** on the recorded provider golden (honest scoping — slice 687 D3) that still fail on provider drift; the passthrough-drive half lands when a single-period BFF does. Drift sensitivity proven on `control-coverage` (a `coverage` rename fails BOTH halves — the slice-210 class of catch, on the exact route slice 412 D5 deferred). Public handler constructor signatures (`New(*pgxpool.Pool)` / `New(*period.Store)`) are byte-for-byte unchanged (P0-409-2); only private struct shape changed. **Zero new CI gate** — rides the existing `Go · build + test` + `Frontend · vitest` jobs. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the audit-workspace populations/samples/walkthroughs/notes reads + attestations + the controldetail Evidence window are deferred to slice 689 on a documented bounded-cut rationale. Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/687-contract-tier-tail-remaining-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/687-contract-tier-tail-remaining.md`.
- **test(contract): slice 412 — contract-tier rollout to the controls-detail tail (`state` + `effectiveness`) (closes 412).** Drains the highest-e2e-traffic part of the long tail slice 411 deferred. Adds the golden-file BFF↔atlas wire-contract tier (ADR-0007) for `GET /v1/controls/{id}/state` and `GET /v1/controls/{id}/effectiveness` (both served by `internal/api/controlstate`, both still hand-mocked by `web/e2e/control-detail-tabs.spec.ts` after slice 411). A single unexported two-method `controlEvaluator` read seam (mirroring slice 411's per-route Option-A seam) lets the provider recorder capture both wire shapes on the plain `go test ./...` unit surface with no `eval.Engine`/Postgres (**no DB, no integration tag — P0-409-1**); the public `New(*eval.Engine)` signature is byte-for-byte unchanged (P0-409-2). New goldens (`web/lib/contracts/control-state.golden.json` + `control-effectiveness.golden.json`) record `populated` + `empty` variants exercising the nullable `scope_cell_id`/`last_observed_at` branches and the no-data (`total_count=0`, never "perfectly failing") effectiveness branch; vitest consumer asserts confirm both verbatim-passthrough BFFs `toEqual` the golden plus the load-bearing field contract + 401-before-upstream guard. Drift sensitivity proven on `control-state` (a `freshness_status` rename fails BOTH halves — the slice-210 class of catch). **Zero new CI gate** — rides the existing `Go · build + test` + `Frontend · vitest` jobs. **Type: JUDGMENT** — `ucfcoverage`'s `/coverage` and the audit-workspace reads are deferred to slice 687 on a documented seam-cost rationale. Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/412-contract-tier-tail-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/412-contract-tier-controls-audit-tail.md`.
- **feat(demoseed): slice 678 — demo seed completeness: org_units, questionnaires, ack-role users, posture (closes 678).** A buyer touring the seeded demo hit empty/zero states on several headline surfaces because the slice-205 seed never populated them (2026-06-10 demo-tenant audit, **ATLAS-028** + **ATLAS-037**, medium/major). This slice seeds DATA into existing schemas only — **no migration, no wire change, no new evidence_kind**. **(AC-1 — `/risks/hierarchy`)** The org tree, theme heatmap, and decision timeline were three empty panels on one screen. Now: a 3-tier `org_units` hierarchy is seeded (1 company → 3 org → 6 team, canvas §6.4 levels); the 20 seeded risks bind to team-level org_units (`org_unit_id`) AND each carries two built-in `org_themes` slugs (`themes`) — the theme × org_unit heatmap excludes risks missing EITHER, so the prior seed (which set neither) rendered empty; a 4-entry Decision Log (canvas §6.7) is seeded spanning the constraint vocabulary, two entries linked to a seeded risk via `decision_risks` so the timeline resolves. `?focus=<id>` now reaches a real org-bound risk. **(AC-2 — `/questionnaires`)** One CAIQ-style questionnaire is seeded with 6 questions (5 answered + 1 intentionally needs-mapping/unanswered for the realistic in-progress state). **(AC-3 — policy-ack roster)** All 5 policies showed "no required-role users" because no role-holders existed and `acknowledgment_required_roles` was empty. Now every published policy requires `["employee", <category-role>]`; 7 role-holder users are seeded (`demo_only=TRUE`, `@demo.example`), each with an `api_keys` row carrying `owner_roles` (the slice-023 roster stand-in), so every policy's roster denominator is non-zero with per-policy size variation; a subset acknowledge so the numerator is non-zero but partial. **(AC-4 — posture)** The seed ensures the demo `framework_versions` are `status='current'`; slice 671 (MERGED) runs the evaluation that fills the dashboard "Framework posture" tiles. **PII boundary honored** (anti-criterion): every new identity is fictional `@demo.example` (RFC 2606), the slice-205 `demo_only` forensic mark + `PopulatedRowCap` guard are unchanged, and `Seeder.Teardown` was extended to sweep every new table (round-trip test pins each back to zero). **Proof:** a new pure-Go unit suite (`internal/demoseed/breadth_test.go`) pins the fixture builders; a new integration test (`TestApply_DemoBreadth`) asserts the org tree + heatmap-grid query + decisions + questionnaire all render and every policy's ack roster has a non-zero denominator against a real Postgres. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/678-demo-seed-completeness-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/678-demo-seed-completeness.md`.
- **feat(vendors): slice 686 — read-only vendor detail page (closes 686).** Before this slice, landing on a vendor **dropped the operator straight into the editable `<VendorForm>`** — the "detail" page _was_ the edit form (the gap slice 679 AC-3 logged and deferred). The canonical `/vendors/{id}` route now renders a **read-only summary** (name, domain, criticality, contract start/end, DPA status, review cadence, last review, owner, notes, and linked SOW when present) with **no form inputs**, and the edit form **moved to `/vendors/{id}/edit`** — matching every other detail surface in the app (`risks/[id]` slice 681, `policies/[id]` slice 672, `controls/[id]`). **(AC-2)** The vendor-list row name already linked to `/vendors/{id}`, so it now lands on the read-only detail with no list change; the detail carries an explicit **"Edit"** affordance routing to the relocated form. **(AC-3)** The **owner renders as a `mailto:` link** when it is a valid email — reusing the slice-679 `isEmail` predicate (`web/lib/email.ts`) so the detail and the form agree on what an email is; a role string or blank owner renders as plain text. **(AC-4 — review history, JUDGMENT)** v1 carries only the `last_review_date` **scalar** — there is no per-review ledger. The detail surfaces the scalar honestly in a "Review history" card that names the gap, and the true per-review timeline is **filed as follow-on slice 688** (`vendor_reviews` ledger, `not-ready` pending this merge) rather than bolting a migration onto a read-only frontend slice. **No new GET shape, no migration** (anti-criteria honored): the detail reuses the existing RLS-scoped `getVendor` BFF (`/api/vendors/{id}` → cookie session → upstream RLS, **invariant #6**); the page never passes a `tenant_id`. **Proof (AC-5):** 10 vitest cases pin the extracted view helpers (`detail-view.ts` — date formatting, owner mailto resolution, DPA status label) with a per-file coverage floor added same-PR; a hermetic Playwright spec (route-mocked BFF per slice 594) asserts the detail renders read-only (no `input`/`select`/`textarea`), the owner mailto/plain-text branches, the list-name → detail navigation, and the Edit → form navigation. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/686-vendor-detail-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/686-vendor-readonly-detail-review-history.md`.
- **feat(risks): slice 681 — risk register sortable columns + per-risk detail (closes 681).** Two risk-surface UX gaps from the 2026-06-10 demo-tenant audit (**ATLAS-039**, low/minor enhancement). **(AC-1 — sortable columns)** With 20+ rows the register had **no way to order by the columns an operator triages on**: the RESIDUAL / INHERENT-SEVERITY / REVIEW-DUE headers were inert. They are now **sortable** — a header click toggles `desc`/`asc` (new columns sort descending-first for worst/latest-first triage), the active column shows a direction caret, and the sort round-trips through the URL (`?sort=<key>:<dir>`, bookmarkable; the default **inherent-severity descending** is treated as "no param" so the canonical URL stays clean). The sort is **client-side** — it joins the list view's established fetch-the-full-list-then-filter-and-paginate-in-the-browser pattern (slices 100/244/246), so there is **zero wire/sqlc/migration change** (decisions log D1); a pure comparator (`web/app/(authed)/risks/sort.ts`, mirroring the `filters.ts` per-page seam) holds the ordering + toggle math, with pending (un-evaluated) rows sorting to the END in both directions so a brand-new risk never jumps the triage queue. **(AC-2 — per-risk detail)** The list said per-risk detail was a "future slice" and offered only "View in hierarchy" — no honest drill-in. The misleading banner is **removed** and risk **titles now link to a read-only `/risks/{id}` detail** — exactly the fix slice 672 made for the identical policy finding (ATLAS-024). The backend read API (`GET /v1/risks/{id}` → `GetRisk`) already exists and is **RLS-tenant-scoped** (`store.Get` runs under the cookie session's tenant; a cross-tenant id resolves to 404), so this is a pure consume-what-exists change — a new BFF proxy (`web/app/api/risks/[id]/route.ts`, mirroring `/api/policies/[id]`; bearer-cookie only, never a client `tenant_id`) + `getRisk`/`fetchRiskDetail` fetchers + a client detail page (loading skeleton, 401→/login, 404→in-shell `notFound()`). The detail is **read-only** (no edit/delete/link — anti-criterion) and carries its own "View in hierarchy →" link so the org-tree scoping workflow composes alongside the title drill-in. **Proof (AC-4):** 19 vitest cases pin the comparator + asc/desc toggle + URL round-trip; 5 vitest cases pin the detail BFF (bearer forwarding, no-tenant_id, 404→"risk not found", 5xx scrubbed); a hermetic Playwright spec (route-mocked BFF per slice 594) asserts each header re-orders the rows and a title navigates to its detail; per-file coverage floors added same-PR for `sort.ts` + the detail BFF. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/681-risk-register-ux-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/681-risk-register-ux.md`.
- **feat(calendar): slice 668 — highlight today in the month grid.** The compliance calendar month grid (`/calendar?view=month`) did **not** visually mark the current day — a standard calendar affordance was missing (audit **ATLAS-014**, 2026-06-10 empty-tenant UI audit). The today cell now carries a distinct treatment: a subtle `bg-primary/10` tint plus an emphasized `text-primary font-semibold` day number, kept visually separate from hover (muted background) and selection (the stronger popover-open ring). A new pure helper (`web/components/calendar/is-today.ts`, extracted so vitest can pin it without the React tree) exposes `isSameLocalDay` / `isToday(cell, reference)`; the reference "now" is injected (a single `new Date()` captured once per render at the view call-site), so the detection logic is deterministic under test. The today computation compares LOCAL calendar dates — matching how the grid already keys its day cells — so it never mis-marks for users east/west of UTC near midnight. **AC-1** the current day's cell is visually highlighted, distinct from selection/hover; **AC-2** the today cell carries `aria-current="date"` for assistive tech (slice-331 a11y discipline); **AC-3** the highlight tracks the actual current date (injected reference, not a hardcoded value). **Anti-criterion honored:** zero change to calendar data, event rendering, or the week/agenda views beyond the today marker. New vitest pin (`is-today.test.ts`, 8 cases) + a per-file coverage floor in `web/coverage-thresholds.json`; a live Playwright assertion (`web/e2e/calendar.spec.ts`, needs no seed data) asserts the month grid marks today with `aria-current="date"` and the real day-of-month. **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/668-calendar-month-highlight-today.md`. (closes #668)

- **fix(board): slice 665 — board-pack generate-draft empty-date validation feedback.** Clicking **"Generate draft"** on `/board-packs` with an empty (or malformed) quarter-end date previously **silently no-opped** — the submit handler's `if (periodEnd)` guard swallowed the empty case with no toast or inline message, and the button's `disabled={!periodEnd}` left the operator unsure whether the action failed or was processing (audit **ATLAS-015**, 2026-06-10 empty-tenant UI audit). The fix adopts the canonical `risks/new` client-side-validation pattern: a new pure `validateGenerateForm` fn (`web/app/(authed)/board-packs/validate.ts`, extracted so vitest can pin it without the React tree) returns a field-error map; the page renders an inline `role="alert"` message wired to the date input via `aria-invalid` + `aria-describedby`, validates on submit-attempt, and re-validates live afterward so the error clears as the operator fills in a valid date. **AC-1** empty/invalid date now shows clear inline feedback — no silent no-op; **AC-2** the form blocks submission (the server is never contacted with an empty date) and the submit no longer relies on a silent `disabled` to hide the failure; **AC-3** a valid date still generates the draft (no regression). **Anti-criterion honored:** zero change to board-pack generation semantics — client-side validation/feedback only (no `internal/api/`, `migrations`, `proto`, or wire-shape changes). New vitest pin (`validate.test.ts`, 6 cases) + a per-file coverage floor in `web/coverage-thresholds.json`; new Playwright spec (`web/e2e/board-pack-generate-validation.spec.ts`, hermetic BFF route-mock per the slice-594 lesson) asserts both the empty-date feedback and the happy path. **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/665-board-generate-draft-empty-date-validation.md`. (closes #665)
- **feat(activity): slice 669 — default the Activity view to business events; read-telemetry opt-in.** The tenant Activity feed (`/activity`) was dominated by internal `decision … read` telemetry (the OPA authz log auditing the app's own GET reads — `internal/authz/input.go` maps `GET`/`HEAD`/`OPTIONS` to `action='read'`), burying human-meaningful events. The Activity view now **defaults to mutating/business events** and **excludes** `decision`/`read` rows; the high-volume read-telemetry stays reachable via an **opt-in** `?include_reads=true` toggle (a new "Show read-telemetry" pill, URL-driven so back/forward and shared links restore the view). The filter is a **view concern only** — the underlying append-only `decision_audit_log` ledger is **unchanged** (canvas **invariant #2**: filtering never deletes or stops recording; the show-all set is a strict superset of the default set). The filter **never hides security-relevant mutations**: auth/role/tenant/exception/evidence writes are NOT `decision`/`read` rows and always surface by default. Implemented as one new optional SQL parameter `exclude_read_telemetry BOOLEAN` on the slice-124 unified aggregator (`internal/db/queries/unified_audit_log.sql`, sqlc-regenerated) gating a conjunctive `NOT (kind='decision' AND action='read')` deny predicate — short-circuited (`= false`) on the admin `/v1/admin/audit-log/unified` forensic endpoint, defaulted on for `/v1/activity/unified` unless the caller opts in. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the read-telemetry deny-list classification (deny only `decision`/`read`; everything else is a business event) and its rationale are recorded in `docs/audit-log/669-activity-default-filter-decisions.md`. Tests pin the default-filtered set vs the show-all set (`internal/api/adminauditlog/read_telemetry_filter_integration_test.go` + pure-Go parse unit + vitest query-builder). Slice doc: `docs/issues/669-activity-feed-business-events-default.md`. (closes #669)

- **feat(connectors): slice 657 — webhookrecv shared validation-handshake seam.** Extracts the vendor-specific **validation-handshake-in-adapter** shape — independently hand-rolled by **two** connectors (Intune's Graph `validationToken` query-param echo, slice 557; Event Grid's `SubscriptionValidationEvent` body event, slice 522) — into a **reusable seam** in `connectors/shared/webhookrecv`. A new `ValidationHook` interface (`Detect(req, body) (response []byte, contentType string, ok bool)`) and a new `HandleWithValidation(w, req, max, hook, verifier, build)` entrypoint consult the hook **AFTER** the size-bounded body read but **BEFORE** the verify-first `Verifier.Verify` call: a validation request (**unsigned by design** — it carries no delivery HMAC/secret) is answered with the hook's exact bytes + content-type at `200` and **builds NO record / never calls Verify / never calls BuildAndPush**; a non-handshake delivery falls through to the **UNCHANGED** verify-first path. **Backward-compatible (D1):** the existing `Handle(w, req, max, verifier, build)` becomes a thin wrapper passing a nil hook, so the six existing adapters (mdm Jamf+Intune-delivery, github, hris, pagerduty, eventgrid delivery) keep compiling and behaving **byte-identically** (the existing `handler_test.go` is unmodified and green). **Both handshake connectors adopt the seam:** Intune's `validationTokenHook` echoes the token verbatim as `text/plain` (bounded by `maxValidationTokenLen`) via `mdmwebhook.Receiver.ServeHTTPWithValidation`; Event Grid's `validationCodeHook` echoes `{"validationResponse":"<code>"}` as `application/json` (bounded by `maxValidationCodeLen`, byte-identical trailing-newline shape) via `Receiver.ServeHTTP` — both adapters' slice-557 / slice-522 tests stay green unmodified. **Verify-first invariant preserved (AC-4):** a forged/non-handshake delivery with a bad signature is rejected `401` and **never** reaches BuildAndPush even with a hook configured (no bypass; test-asserted), and a real validation request short-circuits `200` without ever calling Verify/BuildAndPush (test-asserted). **Invariant #3 honored — push only; ZERO `internal/api/` / `migrations` / `proto` / `schemaregistry` changes:** the handshake + receiver stay source-side in the connectors. **Log-injection (b245 CodeQL):** validation tokens/codes are attacker-controllable and are **never logged** raw on the new path. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the backward-compat strategy (new entrypoint vs option), the `ValidationHook` shape, the per-adapter detection + preserved response bytes, and the verify-first ordering proof are recorded in `docs/audit-log/657-validation-handshake-decisions.md`. Parents: slices 656 / 557 / 522. Slice doc: `docs/issues/657-shared-validation-handshake-hook.md`. (closes #657)

- **feat(connectors): slice 526 — K8s watch-based event-driven (subscribe) profile.** Adds a `subscribe` profile to the Kubernetes connector (slice 487 shipped it pull-only): `profiles_supported` becomes **`[pull, subscribe]`**, named honestly — `subscribe` is **event-driven via the Kubernetes `watch` API**, **NOT** "continuous monitoring" and not a relabeled poll; the pull profile remains the **reconciliation backstop**. A new `subscribe` subcommand consumes a long-lived, **source-side** Kubernetes watch against the **same read-only surfaces the pull path reads** (`rolebindings` for RBAC, `deployments` for workloads), building the **same two evidence kinds** (`k8s.rbac_binding.v1`, `k8s.workload_security_context.v1`) via the **unchanged** rbac/workload record builders — **no new kind, no schema change** — and pushing through the existing `IngestEvidence`/`Push` path. The load-bearing novelty is the **watch lifecycle** (the reflector pattern, hand-rolled against a fakeable seam — **no `client-go` dependency**): bootstrap a **LIST** for the starting `resourceVersion`, **WATCH** from it with **`allowWatchBookmarks=true`** (Bookmark events advance the resume point cheaply with no re-LIST), **re-watch** from the last RV on stream close, and on a **410 Gone** (`resourceVersion too old`) **re-LIST** for a fresh RV and **resume** (the re-list emits — it IS fresh state, and the hour-window key makes re-emission safe). **Cross-profile dedup + DoS coalescing:** every event-built record carries the **same slice-487 hour-window idempotency key** the pull path uses, so a watch-emitted record and a pull-emitted record for the same resource in the same hour — and a **burst** of edits to one binding within the hour — collapse to one ledger row (threat-model D); a bounded in-process per-hour key set (default cap 100 000) sheds the burst before it reaches the platform. **Least-privilege:** adds **at most a `watch` verb** alongside the existing `get,list` on **exactly** the rbac + apps surfaces — **no new resource, never `secrets`, never a write verb, never a wildcard** (a least-privilege test pins the exact rule set; `permissions --subscribe` prints it). **Config-only:** the watch decode adapters model only binding-identity / security-context fields — env / Secret / ConfigMap refs are never modeled, so no payload material can reach a record (a no-leak test asserts it). **Invariant #3 preserved:** the watch is **source-side** (how the connector retrieves data FROM the cluster); the platform-side wire stays push — **zero** `internal/api/` / `migrations/` / `proto/` / `schemaregistry/` changes. Graceful shutdown via `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM` (signal context + `errgroup`). The audit-log consumer is documented as a future / fallback option (managed clusters frequently lack control-plane audit access; the `watch` is portable). `JUDGMENT` decisions log at `docs/audit-log/526-k8s-watch-decisions.md`. Closes #526.

- **feat(connectors): slice 522 — Azure Event Grid event-driven (subscribe) profile.** Adds a `subscribe` profile to the Azure connector (slice 486 shipped it pull-only): `profiles_supported` becomes **`[pull, subscribe]`**, named honestly — `subscribe` is **event-driven** Azure Event Grid receipt (Event-Grid delivery latency plus a short coalescing window), **NOT** "continuous monitoring" and not a relabeled poll; the pull profile remains the **reconciliation backstop**. A new `eventgrid` subcommand runs a **source-side** receiver **inside the connector process** that receives Event Grid change events for in-scope resources, **authenticates the per-delivery key before any work**, and — treating the event as a **TRIGGER, never the data** — **re-reads** the changed resource via the **same read-only Graph/ARM path the pull profile uses**, emitting the **same** existing kind via the **unchanged** record builder (with its existing over-collection guard). A **forged or payload-only event cannot fabricate a record**: the record's data comes entirely from the re-read of real Azure state, so an event whose resource id resolves to nothing yields **zero** records (test-asserted). **Builds onto `connectors/shared/webhookrecv` (slice 656) without modifying it:** a new `connectors/azure/internal/eventgrid` package wires a delivery-key `Verifier` + the re-read routing onto the shared `NewServer` (gosec-G112 timeouts) / `Serve(ctx)` / `Handle(...)` verify-first skeleton (method→405, size→413, **verify-FIRST→401**). **Delivery auth (D1):** Event Grid does not HMAC-sign bodies, so a constant-time **delivery-key** `Verifier` requires the operator-configured credential (`AZURE_EVENTGRID_DELIVERY_KEY`, in a header or query param) before any re-read; a missing/forged key is rejected **`401` before any work** (test-asserted). **SubscriptionValidation handshake (D2, mirrors slice 557's Intune precedent):** when Event Grid validates the webhook it POSTs a `Microsoft.EventGrid.SubscriptionValidationEvent` carrying a `validationCode`; a `ValidationHandler` wrapping the receiver handles this **FIRST**, responds **`200 {"validationResponse":"<code>"}`**, and **builds NO record / triggers NO re-read** (test-asserted). **Event→reader routing (D3):** the changed resource id's ARM provider segment routes to the existing storage / aks / nsg / keyvault / firewall / entra reader; an unmapped resource type is **dropped honestly** (acked `200`, no re-read). **Cross-profile dedup (D5):** a subscribe-emitted record and a pull-emitted record for the same resource in the same UTC hour derive the **same** idempotency key (the slice-486 `sha256("<kind>|<resource_id>|<hour>")`, unchanged via the same builders), collapsing to one ledger row (test-asserted subscribe-key == pull-key). **DoS bounding (D6):** a bounded event queue + a coalescing window collapse an event storm into one re-read per resource; body size-bounded (256 KiB → `413`); loopback-default bind (`127.0.0.1:8485`, TLS at a reverse proxy). **No new Azure permission:** the re-read uses the slice-486 read-only Graph + ARM Reader set (plus the operator's Event-Grid subscription read); no write path, no new evidence kind. **Log-injection hardened (b245 CodeQL):** every user-tainted log arg (resource id, resource type, wrapped error) is `%q`-escaped. **Invariant #3 honored — push only; ZERO `internal/api/` / `migrations` / `proto` / `schemaregistry` changes:** the receiver stays source-side in the connector. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the delivery-auth scheme, the SubscriptionValidation handshake placement, the event→reader routing table, the dedup confirmation, the coalescing window, and the receiver lifecycle/bind config are recorded in `docs/audit-log/522-azure-eventgrid-decisions.md`. Parent: slice 486. Slice doc: `docs/issues/522-azure-event-driven-profile-event-grid.md`. (closes #522)
- **feat(connectors): slice 557 — MDM event-driven (webhook) profile (Jamf + Intune).** Adds a `subscribe` profile to BOTH MDM connectors (slice 490 shipped them pull-only; this resolves its deferred P0-490-7): each connector's `profiles_supported` becomes **`[pull, subscribe]`**, named honestly — `subscribe` is **event-driven** vendor-webhook receipt, **NOT** "continuous monitoring" and not a relabeled poll. A new `webhook` subcommand on each connector runs a **source-side** receiver **inside the connector process** that receives the vendor's compliance-state-change deliveries, **verifies the per-vendor credential before any work**, maps the payload to the **same** `endpoint.device_posture.v1` record the pull profile emits (the slice-490 posture-summary field set, **unchanged**), and pushes it. **First external consumer of `connectors/shared/webhookrecv` (slice 656):** a thin shared adapter `connectors/mdm/mdmwebhook` wires the vendor `Verifier` + a per-vendor `PayloadParser` onto the shared `NewServer` (gosec-G112 timeouts) / `Serve(ctx)` graceful shutdown / `Handle(...)` verify-first skeleton (method→405, size→413, **verify-FIRST→401**) — **no second `http.Server` pattern stood up; the shared package was not modified.** **Per-vendor auth (the design crux — they DIFFER):** **Jamf** does NOT HMAC-sign webhook bodies, so a thin constant-time **shared-secret header** `Verifier` requires the operator-configured credential (`JAMF_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, header `X-Jamf-Webhook-Secret` overridable) before any record. **Intune / Microsoft Graph change notifications** need TWO things: (1) the **`validationToken` echo handshake** — when Graph creates/renews the subscription it sends a `validationToken` query param; the Intune adapter handles this **FIRST** (a `validationHandler` wrapping the receiver), responds **`200` text/plain with the token echoed**, and **builds NO record** (test-asserted); (2) a per-delivery **`clientState`** constant-time `Verifier` (`INTUNE_WEBHOOK_CLIENT_STATE`) — a mismatched or partially-forged batch is rejected **`401` before any record** (test-asserted). **Over-collection guard (P0-490-3, reused unchanged):** the parsers map into `devposture.RawDevice`, whose type system physically cannot hold device geolocation, an installed-app inventory, device contents, or owner contact PII — a webhook carrying those keys has nowhere to put them; a test pins the emitted payload to the posture-summary allow-list. The webhook payload is mapped directly (no re-read), so the connector never reads beyond the posture-summary and needs **no write/management MDM scope**. **Cross-profile dedup:** a webhook-emitted record and a later pull-emitted record for the same device in the same UTC hour derive the **same** idempotency key (the slice-490 `idem.DevicePostureKey`, unchanged), collapsing to one ledger row (test-asserted webhook-key == pull-key; Jamf prefers the numeric `jssID` so it matches the pull profile's REST `id`). **DoS bounding:** body size-bounded (256 KiB → `413`), verify-first, gosec-G112 timeouts; loopback-default bind (`127.0.0.1:8476`/`:8477`, TLS at a reverse proxy). **Log-injection hardened (b245 CodeQL):** every user-tainted log arg (device id + wrapped error) is `%q`-escaped. **Invariant #3 honored — push only; ZERO `internal/api/` / `migrations` / `proto` / `schemaregistry` changes:** the receivers stay source-side in the connectors; no migration, no new evidence kind, no `DefaultSeed` change. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the Jamf credential scheme, the Intune validation-handshake placement + clientState verifier, the map-payload-vs-re-read call, the dedup confirmation, and the receiver lifecycle/bind config are recorded in `docs/audit-log/557-mdm-webhook-decisions.md`. Parent: slice 490. Slice doc: `docs/issues/557-mdm-event-driven-webhook-profile.md`. (closes #557)
- **feat(connectors): slice 540 — PagerDuty event-driven (webhook) profile.** Adds a `subscribe` profile to the PagerDuty connector (slice 489 was pull-only; this resolves its deferred P0-489-7): `profiles_supported` becomes **`[pull, subscribe]`**, named honestly — `subscribe` is **event-driven** PagerDuty v3 webhook receipt, **NOT** "continuous monitoring" and not a relabeled poll. A new `webhook` subcommand runs a **source-side** receiver (a bounded long-lived `http.Server`) **inside the connector process** that receives PagerDuty v3 incident-lifecycle deliveries (`incident.triggered` / `acknowledged` / `resolved` / `escalated` / …), **verifies the `X-PagerDuty-Signature` HMAC before any work**, and pushes the **same** `pagerduty.incident_summary.v1` record the pull profile emits. **Signature verification (STRIDE Spoofing, dominant):** PagerDuty v3 signs each delivery with HMAC-SHA256 over the **raw request body** keyed by the per-subscription signing secret; the header carries one or more comma-separated `v1=<hex>` signatures (**multiple during a secret-rotation**) and the receiver accepts iff **any** matches (constant-time `hmac.Equal`). An unsigned / forged / wrong-signature delivery is rejected **`401` before any record is built or pushed** (test-asserted; P0 anti-criterion). The signing secret is source-side config (`PAGERDUTY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, env only), **never a flag, never logged** (test-asserted). **Summary-only (threat-model I):** the v3 `data` block carries the incident's free-text `title`/`description`/`body`, but the decode struct has **no** free-text field **by construction**, so the free-text never reaches a record even though the payload includes it (test-asserted with a free-text sentinel) — reuses `pdrecord.BuildIncident` **unchanged**. **Cross-profile dedup:** a webhook-emitted record and a later pull-emitted record for the same incident in the same UTC hour derive the **same** idempotency key (the slice-489 `incidentKey`), collapsing to one ledger row (test-asserted webhook-key == pull-key). **DoS bounding (threat-model D):** body size-bounded (256 KiB → `413`), verify-first (unverified load shed cheaply), gosec-G112 server timeouts; loopback-default bind (`127.0.0.1:8474`, TLS at a reverse proxy that forwards the verbatim body). **Log-injection hardened (b245 CodeQL):** every user-tainted log arg (incident id + wrapped error) is `%q`-escaped. **Invariant #3 honored — push only; ZERO `internal/api/` changes:** the receiver stays source-side in the connector; no migration, no new evidence kind, no `proto`/`schemaregistry`/`DefaultSeed` change (reuses `pagerduty.incident_summary.v1`). **Type: JUDGMENT** — the per-vendor-receiver vs shared-helper call, the map-payload vs trigger+re-read call, the multi-`v1=` rotation handling, the loopback/lifecycle config, and the dedup confirmation are recorded in `docs/audit-log/540-pagerduty-webhook-decisions.md`. Parent: slice 489. Slice doc: `docs/issues/540-pagerduty-event-driven-webhook-profile.md`. (closes #540)
