This slice does two things. **(1) Generalizes the slice-007 loader IN PLACE** (no directory rename — the lower-risk call that avoids churning `coverage-thresholds.json`, `integration-shards.txt`, the slice-345 enrolment guard, and every caller): the package doc comment now states the importer is framework-agnostic and grounds it in invariants #1 + #7; `Mapping.TSCCode` → `Mapping.RequirementCode` with a custom `UnmarshalYAML` that accepts **both** the generic `requirement_code:` key (preferred) and the legacy `tsc_code:` key (so the shipped `soc2-tsc-2017.yaml` imports **unchanged** — AC-1 behaviour-preserving / AC-10); error prefixes `soc2import:` → `crosswalk:`; CLI `import-soc2` → `import-crosswalk` with `import-soc2` retained as an alias. **(2) Ships a DRAFT ISO 27001:2022 → SCF crosswalk** (`data/crosswalks/iso27001-2022.yaml`, `framework_slug: iso27001` + `framework_version: "2022"`) — a **curated 36-control high-signal subset** of Annex A (NOT full 93-control coverage — scope discipline), covering the SOC 2 overlap zone (25 of 28 referenced SCF anchors are shared with SOC 2) plus ISO-unique controls (threat intelligence A.5.7, cloud A.5.23→CLD-01, ICT readiness A.5.30, privileged access A.8.2→IAC-21, data masking A.8.11). Every edge is requirement → SCF anchor with an STRM relationship_type + strength — **NEVER** requirement → requirement (invariant #7 / P0-438-1). **The invariant-#1 proof (AC-7, the slice's reason to exist):** the single SCF anchor `IAC-01` is shared between SOC 2 `CC6.1` and ISO `A.5.15` — an integration test imports both crosswalks and asserts the one `scf_anchors` row (`count(*) = 1`, no per-framework duplication, P0-438-2) resolves to **both** framework satisfactions through that single anchor (test output: _"invariant #1 proven: single anchor IAC-01 satisfies SOC 2 [CC6.1] AND ISO [A.5.15 A.5.17]"_). **Read path:** AC-6 is satisfied by the **existing** `GET /v1/requirements/{id}/coverage` endpoint, which resolves the `iso27001:2022:A.5.15` natural key to its anchors with STRM type — no new route added (anchors are global catalog data; the controls join stays RLS-scoped, so no tenant control-implementation state leaks into the catalog-reference payload — P0-438-5). **Licensing posture (P0-438-7):** references only ISO Annex A control **identifiers + titles** (factual) paired with **original** agent-authored descriptions — no verbatim ISO standard text; ships only the ISO→SCF **edge data**, not bundled SCF anchors (operator-imported via slice 006). **Tests:** new pure-Go unit branches (`iso_loader_test.go` — ISO parse, legacy/generic key handling, missing/unknown code rejection, cross-framework code namespacing AC-3/AC-9) + a real-Postgres integration suite (`iso_integration_test.go` — ISO import + idempotency AC-4/AC-5, SOC-2-not-disturbed AC-5, requirement→anchors read AC-6, the invariant-#1 proof AC-7, anchor-existence rejection + rollback AC-2/P0-438-4); all 6 slice-007 integration tests + loader unit tests pass **unmodified** (AC-10 / P0-438-3). **Does NOT** ship a crosswalk-review/conflict UI or coverage-strength visualization (P0-438-6 — separate canvas §10.2 slices). **Type: JUDGMENT** — per-control mapping rationale, confidence, low-confidence flags (`A.5.7`→MON-08 @ 0.5, `A.8.11`→DCH-01 @ 0.4), and the "Revisit once in use" list in `docs/audit-log/438-iso27001-crosswalk-decisions.md`. Full Annex A completion filed as spillover slice 467. Unblocks slice 447 (PCI DSS v4.0 — same generic path). Slice doc: `docs/issues/438-iso27001-2022-crosswalk-loader.md`.
- **ci(infra): slice 458 — pre-commit + CI guard against committing machine-local analysis caches.** Slice 433 added `.understand-anything/` to `.gitignore` and untracked the ~9.8M cache, but only after discovering it had **already been committed to main** (commit `a058b09a`) — swept in by a broad `git add .` alongside a status reconcile, _before_ the ignore rule landed. `.gitignore` stops the forward accident only for untracked paths; it does not catch the **next** class of this footgun: a different per-machine cache (a new analysis tool, a `.cache`-style dir, an editor project DB) swept in before anyone adds an ignore rule. The durable fix is a guard at staging time. Adds `scripts/check-staged-cache-paths.sh` — one bash worker, two surfaces (the established slice-128 `check-action-pins.sh` / slice-140 `check-openapi-drift.sh` / slice-345 enrolment-guard pattern): (1) a **`cache-path-guard` local pre-commit hook** (`.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `pass_filenames: true`) that **hard-fails** when a staged path matches a known machine-local-cache shape, with an actionable message naming the path, the matched shape, the `git restore --staged` + `.gitignore` fix, and the denylist escape hatch (AC-1); (2) a **`cache-path-guard` CI job** (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, unconditional + blocking + no stub-twin, mirroring `actions-pin-check`) that re-checks the whole tracked tree via `--all-tracked` so a contributor who skips local hooks is still caught (AC-2 — defense in depth). The denylist (`DENY_DIRS`/`DENY_FILES` in the script) covers `.understand-anything`, `.cache`, `.ruff_cache`, `.mypy_cache`, `.pytest_cache`, `.idea`, `.vscode`, `node_modules`, `.next`, `.turbo`, `.gradle`, and `.DS_Store`. Matching is **directory-SEGMENT-anchored** (not substring), so it does NOT false-positive on legitimately-tracked lookalikes — verified: `--all-tracked` against the real tree exits 0 despite the slice-433 doc filenames `docs/issues/433-...understand-anything-cache.md` and `docs/audit-log/433-...understand-anything-decisions.md` (AC-3). **Fire/no-fire proven:** staging `.understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json` makes the guard exit 1 and name it; the clean tree exits 0; `scripts/check-staged-cache-paths_test.sh` is an 18-assertion smoke harness (10 cases, synthetic git fixtures via `ATLAS_CACHE_GUARD_ROOT`). Does NOT re-add or modify the slice-433 `.understand-anything/` ignore rule (anti-criterion). The CI job ships **non-required** (`branch-protection.json` untouched) — promote after a soak; the local hook runs inside the already-required `pre-commit · all hooks` job meanwhile. **AC-4 history-scrub decision** recorded in the decisions log: leave the inert `a058b09a` blob until public-launch prep (a maintainer-sign-off-gated destructive rewrite), not now. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/458-precommit-cache-guard-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/458-pre-commit-guard-machine-local-caches.md`.
- **docs(ops): slice 432 — operator backup/restore + upgrade runbooks, surfaced in the published docs site.** Two unrelated documents looked like they covered continuity but gave an operator no runnable procedure: the slice-373 BCP/DR plan is a governance artifact (RTO/RPO tiers, restore scenarios) aimed at diligence reviewers, and `docs/SELF_HOSTING.md`'s "Backups" section was one `pg_dump` line with **no restore procedure and no verify-the-restore drill**. Added two published runbooks. **`docs-site/docs/backup-restore.md`:** Postgres `pg_dump` + restore (role-correct — restore as `atlas_migrate`, never superuser-into-app, AC-11), MinIO `mc mirror` both directions, signing-key + bootstrap-token handling (OAuth keystore, `OSCAL_SIGNING_KEY` embedded vs `cosign-kms`, bootstrap-token rotate-after-restore — backed up **separately with stricter access control** than the data dump, AC-3), backup **encryption at rest** (client-side `age` + destination SSE + deny-unencrypted-upload bucket policy, threat-model I / AC-4), and a **tested restore drill** that verifies _integrity, not just presence_ via a signed-OSCAL-export verify (`atlas oscal verify`, threat-model T / AC-5). **`docs-site/docs/upgrade.md`:** pin a version, pre-upgrade backup checkpoint, manual migration apply via the idempotent `atlas-bootstrap` one-shot, verify, rollback-by-restore, plus the major-Postgres dump-and-restore path. **Two-homes drift resolved (AC-8):** `docs-site/` is the canonical published home for backup/restore + upgrade depth; `docs/SELF_HOSTING.md`'s thin Backups + migrations sections now point at the published runbooks. **Drill actually run (AC-14):** dump → wipe → restore against a scratch `postgres:16-alpine` with the shipped `01-roles.sql` + all 66 real migrations — known evidence record survived hash-byte-identical, 237/237 RLS policies intact, restore as `atlas_migrate` zero errors. **Grounding finding:** `atlas evidence verify` (referenced by the spec, `SELF_HOSTING.md`, and the BCP/DR plan) does **not** ship — only `evidence push` exists; the runbook grounds its integrity check in the real `atlas oscal verify` surface and filed slice 464 for the missing ledger-wide verify verb. Docs-only; `mkdocs build --strict` green. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/432-backup-restore-upgrade-runbooks-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/432-backup-restore-upgrade-runbooks.md`.
- **ci(release): slice 451 — SLSA provenance + per-artifact SBOM for the binary / CLI / SDK release surface.** Closed the supply-chain asymmetry the project's "diligence the diligence tool" thesis exposes: `container-publish.yml` already gave images a signed SBOM + SLSA provenance, but `release.yml` cosign-signed **only** `checksums.txt` and emitted no SBOM and no provenance for the downloaded binaries/CLI/SDK archives — exactly the gap a GRC diligence questionnaire asks about by name. Now every released archive carries (a) a **per-archive SPDX SBOM** (`<artifact>.spdx.sbom.json`, emitted by GoReleaser's newly-enabled `sboms:` block driving the already-installed `syft`) and (b) a **SLSA build-provenance attestation** binding each artifact's digest individually to this repo's `release.yml` GitHub Actions OIDC identity (`actions/attest-build-provenance`, same pinned SHA the container job uses), NOT just the aggregate checksums file (P0-451-2). **SBOM is gated, not best-effort (AC-4 / P0-451-4):** the syft step drops `continue-on-error`, and a new "Gate SBOMs" step hard-fails the release if any SBOM is invalid SPDX or has zero components (negative path proven locally: an injected empty SBOM exits 1). **Permissions are job-scoped (AC-3 / P0-451-5):** the only new privilege, `attestations: write`, is added to the GoReleaser job's `permissions:` block — the workflow-level block stays `contents: read`; no workflow-level widening. **Self-verify (AC-7):** a new round-trip step runs `gh attestation verify` asserting the workflow identity, mirroring the existing "Self-verify signed checksums" step (which is unchanged — additive only, AC-8). **Edge-signing JUDGMENT (AC-5):** re-confirmed slice 207 D1 — `:edge`/`:main-<sha7>` images stay deliberately unsigned (provenance + SBOM only; cosign signatures bind to release-cut tag identity); `container-publish.yml`'s header comment updated to record the resolved decision. **Diligence-facing verify guide (AC-6):** `docs/RELEASE_READINESS.md` §12 adds a consolidated, independently-runnable supply-chain verification section covering containers + binaries/CLI/SDK + OSCAL bundles with stock `cosign` / `gh attestation verify` / `slsa-verifier` commands for an external auditor. **Validation:** a local GoReleaser snapshot emitted all 10 SPDX SBOMs (19–118 components each, no secrets/build-host/username leakage — P0-451-3); `goreleaser check` + `actionlint` clean; tooling is license-clean (syft Apache-2.0, attest-build-provenance MIT, slsa-verifier/cosign Apache-2.0). Does NOT touch the runtime OSCAL-bundle signing path (`internal/oscal/sign.go`, ADR-0010 row 2 — P0-451-1). **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/451-slsa-provenance-sbom-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/451-slsa-provenance-sbom-binary-cli-sdk-releases.md`.
- **test(fuzz): slice 421 — native `testing.F` fuzz harnesses for the untrusted-input parsers + a bounded CI fuzz pass.** The platform's thesis is ingesting untrusted files, yet the repo had **zero** Go fuzz targets (`rg "func Fuzz"` returned empty) — every operator-/auditor-supplied parser ran with no test tier guarding the malformed-input frontier, where a panic is a denial-of-service on the ingest path (the slice's headline threat). Adds five native fuzz targets, each seeded from the package's existing golden / synthetic fixtures and asserting the parser returns a clean error OR a deterministic value for every input — never a panic, never a `recover()`-swallowed crash (P0-421-1): `FuzzReadBundle` (`internal/oscal` — OSCAL bundle-manifest read path, incl. the path-traversal guard on member filenames), `FuzzLoad` (`internal/api/scfimport` — SCF catalog JSON load+validate), `FuzzParseSemver` (`internal/api/schemaregistry` — strict X.Y.Z semver, asserts canonical round-trip), `FuzzParse` (`connectors/manual/internal/manualcsv` — RFC-4180 CSV with row/field caps, asserts determinism), and `FuzzParseExcel` (`internal/questionnaire` — excelize-backed `.xlsx`, exercising the malformed-zip boundary below the `MaxUploadBytes` cap). **No crashers found:** each target was run locally for a 20–25s campaign (semver ~6.6M execs, csv ~10M, excel ~900K, plus the heavier file-backed oscal/scfimport targets) and all passed — so there are no `testdata/fuzz/` crasher entries to commit this slice. **CI wiring** adds a `Go · fuzz (bounded)` job (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) that runs `go test -fuzz` at a tight 20s-per-target cap so the harness can never DoS the runner (P0-421-2), path-filtered to Go changes via the existing `changes.outputs.code` gate + a same-name stub-twin (slice-061 pattern) so docs-only PRs resolve the check in seconds. The seed-corpus replay path runs on the existing Go-unit surface (`go test -run=Fuzz…`, no `-fuzz`). Seed corpora are synthetic / golden-derived only — no tenant data, no vendor-prefixed tokens (P0-421-4). Scope discipline honored: harnesses only — no parser rewrites, no coverage-floor changes (P0-421-3 / P0-421-5); a future crasher's fix is a spillover slice, not bolted on. `CONTRIBUTING.md` gains a "Fuzzing" section (target table, local-run commands, crasher/spillover convention). **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/421-parser-fuzz-harnesses-untrusted-input.md`.

### Changed

- **fix(oscal): slice 659 — OSCAL component-definitions list 500: reproduce + diagnose (migration-lag, not a code bug).** The Vendor Claims page (`/oscal/component-definitions`) returned **HTTP 500** in the EMPTY/default tenant on an edge deploy. A DIAGNOSE-THEN-DECIDE reproduce against a **fully-migrated** Postgres (all 83 `migrations/sql/*.sql` applied) proved the platform code is **correct**: an empty tenant returns `200 {"component_definitions":[],"count":0}`, a populated tenant returns the rows, and cross-tenant reads stay RLS-isolated (invariant #6). The edge 500 is **migration-lag** — the edge `imported_catalogs` table is missing the `kind`/`profile_title` columns added by `migrations/sql/20260608000000_oscal_imported_profiles.sql` (slice 511); sqlc bakes `kind` into the generated `ListImportedComponentDefinitions` query (SELECT list + `WHERE kind = 'component_definition'`), so Postgres rejects it at **parse time** (`column "kind" does not exist`) **regardless of row count**, which is why even the empty tenant 500'd. **No code/query/migration change** (`git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- proto/` and `-- migrations/` both empty; no sqlc regen). This slice adds the regression guard `TestList_EmptyTenantReturns200EmptyList` (`internal/api/oscalcomponents/integration_test.go`, `//go:build integration`) that locks in "empty migrated tenant → 200 + empty list" so a future query/migration mismatch is caught in CI, not a deploy. The 500 path uses `httperr.WriteInternal` (slice 367) — verified the client body is a bare `{"error":"internal error","request_id":...}` with no SQL/column/stack leak. The operational fix (apply the missing migration on the edge box + read the `atlas-migrate-edge` logs to find why migrate-on-bringup skipped it) needs maintainer edge access and is filed as deploy-note spillover slice 683 (`not-ready`, BLOCKED on edge-log access). **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/659-oscal-component-definitions-500-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/659-oscal-component-definitions-list-500.md`. (closes #659)

- **refactor(connectors): slice 656 — shared webhook-receiver abstraction.** Factors the vendor-agnostic idioms the three independently-built source-side webhook receivers (github slice 044, hris slices 573/655, pagerduty slice 540) each re-implemented into a new `connectors/shared/webhookrecv` package: the bounded gosec-G112 `http.Server` constructor (ReadHeader/Read/Write/Idle timeouts) + `Serve(ctx)` graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown (squashing `http.ErrServerClosed`→nil), the `MaxBytesReader`→`413` body cap + verify-FIRST handler skeleton (`Handle`: method→`405`, size→`413`, **verify before any record**→`401`, then a vendor `BuildAndPush(req, body) status` callback), and a **parameterizable** constant-time HMAC-SHA256 core (`HMACConfig{Header, Prefix, Encoding, Multi}` covering header name · hex/hex-upper/base64 · prefix `sha256=`/`v1=` · single-vs-comma-separated-multi-signature, all `hmac.Equal`). Each receiver is reduced to its thin vendor adapter: **hris** keeps its `WorkerFetcher`/`PayloadParser`/`ParseWorkerIDs` fan-out (slice 655) as its `BuildAndPush` body — the shared package carries **no** HRIS domain type (no `worker.RawWorker` leak); **pagerduty** keeps its summary-only decode + `v1=` multi-signature rotation; **github** keeps its finer-grained missing/malformed/bad error taxonomy + its `LimitReader`→`400` handler and adopts the shared HMAC compute/compare only. **Pure refactor — byte-identical behavior (P0):** every existing test across all three receiver packages stays green with **no assertion change**; same `401`/`413`/`405` statuses, same verify-first-before-record ordering, same emitted evidence kinds / record shapes / idempotency keys (dedup unchanged), same `%q`-at-log-sink CWE-117 discipline. **Invariant #3 honored — push only:** receivers stay source-side; ZERO `internal/api/` / `migrations/` / `proto/` / `schemaregistry/` diff. New `connectors/shared/webhookrecv` coverage floor (96; measured 98.8%); pagerduty's 90 floor HELD (server-lifecycle code moved out) by adding pure-Go normalizer unit tests, never lowered. **Type: JUDGMENT** — the shared API surface, the seam boundary, github's selective adoption, and the HRIS-fan-out mapping are recorded in `docs/audit-log/656-shared-webhookrecv-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/656-shared-webhook-receiver-refactor.md`. (closes #656)

- **ci: slice 631 — fail-closed `CI · merge-gate` aggregator so a RED or skipped-but-needed required integration shard blocks merge.** Closes the merge-safety hole documented in slice 633 D6: slice 474 merged with its required check `Go · integration (shard A)` a real 10m21s FAIL, and the regression then hid on `main` for ~3 weeks because every subsequent `main` commit either path-filter-SKIPPED the shard legs (docs/status-only) or had its `main` run CONCURRENCY-CANCELLED before the leg completed — so no _completed_ shard-A run on `main` re-caught it (the `CI path-filter as gap-multiplier` pattern). Branch protection reliably blocks a check that ran RED but treats a required check that ended `skipped`/`cancelled`/`neutral` inconsistently. **Mechanism (a) — aggregator (primary, block-before-merge):** a new `merge-gate` job `needs:` every required path-filterable leg (`build-go`, `tests-integration-shard` matrix, `tests-integration` roll-up, `lint-go`, `sqlc-drift`, `proto`, `build-frontend`, `frontend-playwright`, `lint-python`) plus `changes`, runs with `if: always()`, and fails CLOSED: on a Go-affecting PR (`changes.outputs.code == 'true'`) EVERY leg must be `success` — any `failure`/`cancelled`/`skipped` leg fails the gate; on a docs-only PR (`code != 'true'`) the gate short-circuits GREEN so the slice-061 path-filter cost optimization is preserved. The matrix leg's `needs` result is the AGGREGATE (one RED shard ⇒ `failure` ⇒ gate RED), which is exactly the per-shard signal that was absent at the branch-protection layer. **Mechanism (c) — main-branch canary (defense-in-depth, detect-after-merge):** a new `tests-integration-main-canary` matrix job runs the full shard matrix UNCONDITIONALLY on push-to-`main` only, in its own `concurrency` group with `cancel-in-progress: false`, so a `main` run can no longer be cancelled before its shard leg completes (masking mechanism (2)); it reuses `scripts/run-integration-shard.sh` so the slice-417 shard manifest stays the single source of truth. **No existing gate relaxed; no existing required job renamed or removed** (branch protection references them by name) — the aggregator is purely additive. **Maintainer hand-off (cannot be done from a PR):** add `CI · merge-gate` to `.github/branch-protection.json`'s `required_status_checks.contexts` and apply via `bash scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh`; until then the gate runs/reports but is advisory. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/631-ci-red-shard-merge-guard-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/631-ci-guard-block-merge-on-red-required-shard.md`. Parent: slice 633.

- **test(coverage): slice 472 — lift `internal/api/oauth` floor 78 → 87 (measured merged 89.3%, up from 80.8% at slice 456) by covering the device-approval browser flow + the `DBUserResolver` BYPASSRLS authPool path.** The slice-456 spillover: it covers the two security-critical-tier surfaces slice 456 named out-of-scope. (1) The **slice-191 device-approval browser flow** — new `internal/api/oauth/device_approval_integration_test.go` mounts the real `ServeApprove`/`ServeDeny` handlers on the public `Handler` (via `AttachDeviceApprovalEndpoint`, `oauth.go:151`, 0% before) behind a credential-injecting middleware against a real `DeviceCodeStore`, asserting the specific RFC error code (not merely the status) for every arm: approve success (snapshot written + verified via `LookupByUserCode`, 0% before) + the `cred.TenantID != ""` snapshot-build branch + the no-tenant branch; the `Approve` 0-rows error-mapping arms — not-found→404 `invalid_request`, expired→400 `expired_token`, denied/consumed/raced→400 `invalid_grant`; deny success (consumed_at set, snapshot NOT written) + deny not-found→404. (2) The **slice-192 `DBUserResolver` BYPASSRLS authPool path** — new `internal/api/oauth/user_resolver_authpool_integration_test.go` wires `NewDBUserResolverWithAuthPool` (0% before) with the BYPASSRLS admin pool (`DATABASE_URL`) and drives `enumerateMemberships` (cross-tenant: one OIDC subject across two tenants → both in `available_tenants`, per-tenant roles from the correct tenant-scoped user_id) + the session-fallback append branch + `lookupSuperAdmin` both ways (present→`super_admin` true, absent→false; P0-188-4 never synthesized). New pure-Go unit file `device_approval_constructor_test.go` covers the `NewDeviceApprovalEndpoint` nil-codes fail-loud panic + nil-`Now` default (slice-353 Q-2 fast loop). The `90` advisory in `$security_critical_packages` is **unchanged** — hard floor only (monotonic ratchet, slice 069). Residual gap to 90 (AC-3): ~0.7pp of genuinely hard-to-reach mid-transaction-failure + crypto-failure arms + a cross-package initiate/authorize/introspect/revoke error tail outside this slice's named scope. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/472-oauth-coverage-tail-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/472-oauth-device-approval-and-dbuserresolver-coverage.md`. Parent: slice 456 (toward slice-350 advisory).

- **test(coverage): slice 456 — lift `internal/api/oauth` floor 77 → 78 (measured merged 80.8%, up from 79.0% at slice 422) by covering the residual error / signer-failure / audit-write arms.** The residual security-critical tail toward the slice-350 90% advisory that slice 422 deliberately deferred. New unit suite `internal/api/oauth/residual_branches_test.go` drives the pre-DB arms: the **token-exchange signer-failure → `server_error` (500)** arm (via a verify-ok/sign-fail keystore — a non-ES256 P-384 active signing key fails `tokensign.Sign`'s curve check while `Verify` still resolves the real fsstore verification keys, so a self-minted `subject_token` verifies then the exchange's own mint 500s, with a no-internal-detail-leak assertion composing with slice 367); the `tokenBucketLimiter` overflow-cap + `WindowSeconds`/`max` edge arms (via new unexported `export_test.go` seams); the `NewTokenEndpoint` rate≤0 fallback; and the `writeAudit`/`writeAuthCodeAudit` nil-pool early returns. New integration suite `internal/api/oauth/residual_audit_integration_test.go` drives the DB-state-dependent arms: the **best-effort audit-write FAILURE arms** (BeginTx failure via a closed pool; Exec failure via an `AfterConnect` search-path-stripped pool resolving the unqualified `oauth_token_exchanges` to no relation; empty-jti `"unknown"` fallback verified via an RLS-scoped read; jti-truncation; from_tenant non-nil + NULL branches) each asserting the D3 best-effort **non-blocking** outcome; and the **signer-failure `server_error` arms** for `client_credentials` / `authorization_code` / `device_code` — each reaching the JWT mint after a real Verify/redemption and asserting 500 + `server_error`. The `90` advisory in `$security_critical_packages` is **unchanged** — hard floor only (monotonic ratchet, slice 069). Residual gap to 90 (AC-4): the `ApplyTenant`-failure arms are defensive dead code (`SET LOCAL set_config` always succeeds on a healthy tx), and the device-approval browser flow + `DBUserResolver` BYPASSRLS authPool path are out of this slice's named scope and filed as spillover slice 472. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/456-oauth-coverage-residual-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/456-oauth-coverage-residual-audit-and-signer-error-arms.md`. Parent: slice 422 (toward slice-350 advisory).

- **chore(docs): slice 469 — sweep the residual `web/app/**`mockup provenance citations to the archived path.** Finishes the tail of the slice-459 provenance sweep. Slice 437 relocated the iteration-1 mockups`Plans/mockups/`→`Plans/\_archive/mockups/`; slice 459 then repointed every reachable `// per Plans/mockups/<page>.html`-style design-provenance comment (`internal/board`, `internal/catalog`, `web/components`, `web/lib`, `web/e2e`) but **deferred** the ~23 files under `web/app/**`to avoid a same-batch merge collision with the concurrently-in-flight slice 448 (which owned`web/app/`). With 448 merged, that surface is free. This slice applies the identical mechanical update-path convention slice 459 locked in (D1: `s#Plans/mockups/#Plans/\_archive/mockups/#g`, preserving the load-bearing line-anchored citations like `control.html lines 139-152`so the provenance links resolve again) across all 23`web/app/(authed)/**`page/helper files — 36 comment lines, 23 files, **comment-only** (36 insertions / 36 deletions, every changed line a`//`or`*`comment; zero behavior, assertion, or runtime-path-resolver change). The three`.test.ts` hits (`policies/ack-window.test.ts`, `controls/filters.test.ts`, `controls/[id]/tabs.test.ts`) are explanatory `//`provenance comments — distinct from slice 459's deliberately-invalid`web/e2e-audit/lib/manifest.test.ts:47` negative-test *fixture string\*, which lives outside this scope and was already correctly left as-is by 459 (AC-3 satisfied by absence — no such fixture exists under `web/app`). **AC-1 proof:** `grep -rn "Plans/mockups/" web/app | grep -v _archive` now returns zero live-citation hits. The runtime resolver (`mockupsDir()`, slice 437) is untouched. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/469-web-app-mockup-citation-sweep-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/469-web-app-mockup-citation-sweep.md`. Parent: slice 459 (grandparent: slice 437).

- **ci: slice 417 — shard the `-p 1` integration job (Phase A serial + Phase B matrix).** The `Go · integration (Postgres RLS)` tier was a single serial `go test -tags=integration -p 1 ./internal/...` over ~89 packages — the merge-queue wall-clock bottleneck (~9-13 min, re-run on every update-branch) that slice 393's watermark was built to trip on. The fix is **across-runner sharding**, NOT `-p N` within one runner (slice 334 settled `-p N` out of scope; **every leg still runs `-p 1` internally** — P0-4): a `strategy.matrix` over `leg: [A, B1, B2, B3]` puts each leg on its **own runner with its own Postgres + MinIO + NATS + seed**, so two parallel legs cannot race on the shared platform-layer rows (`scf_anchors` [no `tenant_id`], the catalog rows of `evidence_kind_schemas`, the append-only `evidence_records` ledger) the old `-p 1` serialized — they have separate databases. **Leg A** (serial) holds the SCF-catalog seed cluster + the eval/control consumers coupled to the freshly-seeded catalog (P0-2 pins the seeders here); the migration round-trip (slice 297), the slice-461 order-independence guard, the slice-417 shard-coverage guard, and the slice-393 watermark all run on **Leg A only** (AC-12 / AC-10 / AC-6). **Legs B1/B2/B3** hold the tenant-scoped handler families (auth/admin; audit/board/metrics/policy/risk/vendor; dashboard/evidence-read/misc), whose isolation is the transaction-scoped `SET LOCAL app.current_tenant` RLS pattern slice 334 documented as the **real** (non-`-p 1`) isolation mechanism. The package→leg map is `scripts/integration-shards.txt` (source of truth), read by `scripts/run-integration-shard.sh` (always passes `-p 1`). **The load-bearing risk this slice lives or dies on (T-1: a package falls through the split and runs in NO leg) is closed by `scripts/check-integration-shard-coverage.sh`** — it proves the union of all legs == the `//go:build integration`-tagged package set **EXACTLY** (89 == 89; same extraction the slice-345 enrolment guard uses), disjoint (no double-run — T-2), and the catalog-seed cluster pinned to Leg A (P0-2); self-test 14/14 green. The slice-345 enrolment guard now reads the manifest too, so "every tagged package is enrolled somewhere" keeps holding after the list moved out of ci.yml's inline `go test`. **Coverage fan-in (AC-7 / P0-3 / T-3):** each leg uploads a distinct `go-integration-coverage-<leg>` artifact; the fan-in `tests-integration` job downloads the unit profile + every leg's profile, merges them with `gocovmerge`, and runs the slice-279 per-package gate on the **union** — never a single leg's fragment (which would under-count the ratchet); floors are **NOT** lifted (monotonic ratchet preserved). **The required branch-protection check `Go · integration (Postgres RLS)` is UNCHANGED (AC-11) — no maintainer branch-protection update needed:** the fan-in job reports that exact name and is green only when every leg passed (a fail on any shard fails the required check); the per-leg `Go · integration (shard ...)` checks are net-new and intentionally NOT required. The slice-061 docs-only stub-twin under the required name is preserved (AC-13). **Validation:** `actionlint -shellcheck "" -no-color` clean (the project's gate posture); `check-action-pins` 162/162 pinned (AC-16 — no new `uses:`, all reused SHAs); shard-coverage 14/14, enrolment 12/12, watermark 14/14 (AC-15), order-independence 2/2, action-pins 15/15 self-tests green; manifest union proven == the exact 89-package `go test` list, zero double-assignment, legs 16/24/23/26. **Pure CI restructure** — no product code, schema, migration, or test assertion changed (P0-5); no `-p N` (P0-4); no `-retry` (P0-6 / Q-16 unchanged). **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/417-integration-shard-decisions.md` (shard count, package→leg map, coverage-merge approach, billable-minutes trade-off, scheduler-flake-not-a-blocker call, revisit list); reference `docs/ci/integration-shards.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/417-shard-integration-job-phase-a-b.md`. Pairs with slice 418 (composite stack-up action).

- **docs: slice 430 — consolidated configuration / env-var reference page.** Adds one published page `docs-site/docs/configuration.md` — a single reference table over every variable in `deploy/docker/.env.example` (columns: **Variable · Default · Required? · Scope server/web/bootstrap · Description**), grouped into logical sections (Required · Database · Object store + NATS · Cookies & security · Observability · Web · Test-mode · Ports · Tenancy/bootstrap) rather than a flat dump. Replaces the prior grep-the-`.env.example`-comments-or-the-Go-source workflow with a scannable authoritative reference. **Threat model honored (P0-430-1/2):** secret-typed variables (`POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `ATLAS_APP_PASSWORD`, `MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD`, `BEARER_HASH_KEY`, `ATLAS_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN`, `ATLAS_DEFAULT_USER_PASSWORD`, the password segment of `DATABASE_URL_APP`) show a `CHANGE_ME` placeholder + `openssl rand -hex 32` guidance only — never a real or copy-pasteable value — and a dedicated **Security-critical variables** section flags exactly which `CHANGE_ME` values must not be left weak. The three privilege-relevant toggles ship their warnings intact: `ATLAS_TEST_MODE` ("DO NOT set in production — mints admin JWTs"), `ATLAS_METRICS_FALLBACK_ENABLE` ("unauthenticated `/metrics`, must be network-gated, default off"), `ATLAS_SECURE_COOKIES` ("set `true` behind TLS"); the Ports section notes admin/internal ports must not be bound to a public interface. **Drift guard (P0-430-4, AC-9/10/14):** `scripts/check-config-reference-drift.sh` parses `^[A-Z_]+=` (active) and `^# [A-Z_]+=` (commented opt-in) keys from `.env.example` and fails when a template key is missing from the page or a page row documents a variable the template does not define; exercised by `scripts/check-config-reference-drift_test.sh` (7 cases incl. the real in-tree pair) and runnable as `just config-reference-drift-check`. Runs in CI via a new informational `config-drift` job in `.github/workflows/docs-publish.yml` (paths-filter widened to fire on `.env.example` + the guard script). 28 variables documented (25 active + 3 commented opt-in); guard passes clean. Nav entry "Configuration reference" added to `docs-site/mkdocs.yml` after Install; `mkdocs build --strict` passes. Page cross-links to the Install quickstart, `docs/SELF_HOSTING.md`, and `deploy/docker/.env.example` (AC-13). **No variable's name, default, or behavior changed (P0-430-3)** — documentation + sync guard only. **Finding (spillover slice 460):** `NATS_URL` is read by the server (`cmd/atlas/main.go`) but hardcoded inline in `docker-compose.yml` rather than templated — deliberate (fixed compose-network address, not an operator knob), so the page correctly omits it; the spillover records the leave-as-is-vs-promote decision. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/430-configuration-reference-page.md`.

- **deps: slice 454 — bump the go-otel observability group (13 modules, supersedes dependabot #951).** Validated group bump of the OpenTelemetry Go modules — the project's native observability substrate (CLAUDE.md tech-stack "Observability — OTEL native"), on the hot path of every instrumented request via `otelhttp` (HTTP server middleware), the OTLP exporters, and the runtime/metric SDKs. Bumped as a version-coherent group: `go.opentelemetry.io/otel` + `/sdk` + `/sdk/metric` + `/metric` + `/trace` + the `exporters/otlp/...` set (otlptrace grpc+http, otlpmetric grpc+http, otlptrace base) **`1.43.0 → 1.44.0`**, `exporters/prometheus` **`0.65.0 → 0.66.0`**, and `contrib/instrumentation/net/http/otelhttp` + `.../runtime` **`0.68.0 → 0.69.0`**. `otelpgx` (`github.com/exaring/otelpgx`) was **not** re-bumped — it already merged at `0.11.1` via dependabot #656 (`3b7d4a42`); this slice confirms it still composes with the bumped otel core. No instrumentation refactor, no new spans/metrics, no exporter-endpoint config change (P0-454-4). **No breaking API change** surfaced — `go build ./...` clean with zero source edits; transitive `google.golang.org/grpc 1.81.0 → 1.81.1`, `golang.org/x/net 0.54.0 → 0.55.0`, and `grpc-gateway/v2 2.28.0 → 2.29.0` pulled forward by the exporter bump. **Security (AC-5 / P0-454-1/3 — the load-bearing axis):** otelhttp's and otelpgx's redaction defaults are **unchanged** post-bump — the existing redaction-regression tests (`internal/observability/otel/integration_test.go`: `TestAC19_NoBearerTokenInSpanAttributes`, `TestAC13_NoMessageBodyInNATSSpanAttributes`) still pass, confirming no `Authorization` header value, session cookie, DB connection string, or raw SQL literal is newly attached to any emitted span attribute; no telemetry-endpoint credential is logged. **Verification:** `go build ./...` clean; `go test ./...` (unit) green; `go test -tags=integration -p 1 ./internal/observability/... ./internal/api/...` green (57 otel-touching packages, 0 failures); trace + propagation emission confirmed end-to-end (`TestAC21_NATSTracePropagation` — span emits and W3C context propagates parent→child); `golangci-lint run` clean. **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/454-go-otel-observability-group-bump.md`.
- **chore: slice 437 — archive `Plans/mockups/` iteration-1 HTML out of the active tree.** The iteration-1 UI mockups (11 hand-written HTML files + `_shared/shell.css`, ~292K, Tailwind via CDN, no build) lived in the active `Plans/` tree, so every divergence between a mockup and the shipped `web/` page read as drift worth filing — slices 216/220/231/245/258/259 (and others in the slice-204 parity-audit family) were all "mockup stale vs production" findings that cost triage time only to confirm "the mockup is just old." `web/` is canonical; the mockups are a frozen iteration-1 reference. **`git mv` (history-preserving, R100 pure renames)** relocated all 12 files from `Plans/mockups/` to `Plans/_archive/mockups/` (mockups moved, not deleted) and added `Plans/_archive/mockups/README.md` recording the new convention: **per-page mockup-vs-`web/` divergence is expected and is NOT fileable drift.** Updated every **instructional / functional** pointer: CLAUDE.md (reading-order item 4, working-norms item 3, repo-layout diagram, quick-references line — all now carry the "not fileable drift" note), `Plans/ARCHITECTURE_CANVAS.md` Mockups section, the active canvas docs (`12-ui-fill-in-design-decisions.md` path + a dated path-note banner on `13-ui-mockup-audit-2026-05-16.md`), `web/docs/responsive-discipline.md`, and the moved `index.html`'s self-references (including fixing its `../`→`../../` relative links to `ARCHITECTURE_CANVAS.md`/`UCF_GRAPH_MODEL.md`/`EVIDENCE_SDK.md`/`canvas/12` broken by the extra nesting level). **The one load-bearing `web/` touch is the UI-honesty audit harness** (`web/e2e-audit/` — audit tooling, NOT shipped product code): its `mockupsDir()` resolver hardcoded `Plans/mockups`, and `validateManifest`'s P0-178-11 file-existence check (the `Frontend · UI honesty (advisory)` CI job) would break if left dangling, so the resolver constant + the emitted suggestedAction/report path strings + the `mockup-spec.schema.json` descriptions were repointed to the archive; verified all 11 manifest-referenced mockups resolve at the new path and the old `Plans/mockups/` is gone. **AC-5** secrets spot-check: the mockups are static CDN-Tailwind pages with no real credentials (keyword hits are benign UI labels like "API tokens"). **AC-6** honored — no `web/` production code (`web/app/`, `web/components/`, `web/lib/`) modified. **AC-4** conscious-leaves (per the slice's point-in-time-fact clause): `CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/audit-log/**`, `docs/audits/**`, `docs/issues/**`, `docs/design/**`, and ~50 inline `// per Plans/mockups/...html` design-provenance code comments under `web/`+`internal/` are dated historical records left verbatim; a one-time mechanical comment-path sweep is filed as **spillover slice 455**. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/437-archive-iteration-1-mockups.md`.
- **chore: slice 433 — gitignore the `.understand-anything/` local-analysis cache.** The `understand-anything` tool writes a ~9.8M per-machine cache (`knowledge-graph.json`, `fingerprints.json`, `meta.json`, `.understandignore`) to the repo root; `meta.json` pins one developer's working-tree commit hash and a ~1699-file inventory — a machine-local snapshot, not a shared artifact. Adds the directory-anchored trailing-slash rule `.understand-anything/` under the `### Tooling caches` block of the root `.gitignore` (trailing slash = directory-only, cannot match a tracked file outside the cache — the inverse-tampering guard from the slice threat model). **Deviation from the slice's stated premise (documented in the decisions log):** the slice was filed assuming the cache was merely _untracked_, but it had since been swept into history by commit `a058b09a` ("chore(status): batch 164") — the exact `git add .` footgun the slice predicted, already fired. A `.gitignore` rule is inert against tracked files, so the slice also `git rm --cached`s the four cache files (index-only; the local working copy is left on disk per the P0 — not deleted) so the ignore rule takes effect and the 9.8M blob stops churning on every analysis re-run. Verification: `git check-ignore .understand-anything/meta.json` now resolves to the new rule; `git ls-files -i -c --exclude-standard` is empty (no _remaining_ tracked file is newly ignored — AC-4). **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/433-gitignore-understand-anything-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/433-gitignore-understand-anything-cache.md`.
- **test(oauth): slice 422 — lift `internal/api/oauth` coverage 74.7% → 79.0% merged toward the 90% security-critical advisory (RFC error branches).** Closes the slice-333 Q-4 gap: the slice-314 floor (`internal/api/oauth: 72`) was satisfiable by happy-path-weighted coverage while the dangerous RFC DENY branches sat untested, and an untested OAuth error branch is an auth-bypass / elevation-of-privilege class bug (the package is the OAuth 2.0 AS that mints every atlas JWT). Adds **human-written, per-RFC-section error-branch tests** that drive the deny/reject branches and assert the **specific RFC error code** in the response body, not merely the HTTP status (AC-7 / P0-422-3). **New unit suite `error_branches_test.go`** (pre-DB branches reachable without Postgres, slice-353 Q-2 pattern): RFC 8693 token-exchange refusals — missing `subject_token`, unsupported `subject_token_type`, missing/non-UUID `atlas:target_tenant_id` → `invalid_request`; **expired / wrong-issuer / wrong-audience subject_token → `invalid_token`** (the claim-validation arm, distinct from the already-covered bad-signature arm — a valid signature whose claims fail temporal/identity validation); **denied tenant-switch → `invalid_target`** with a composed no-internal-detail-leak assertion (slice 367 errleak: body carries no SQL/stack/tenant-enumeration oracle); `client_credentials` missing-credentials → `invalid_request`; malformed-form rejection across `/oauth/token` + `/oauth/introspect` + `/oauth/revoke` + `/oauth/device_authorization`; the revoke Basic-auth-no-fallthrough spoofing guard → `invalid_client`; and the endpoint-constructor fail-loud panic invariants across the token/introspect/revoke/device-auth family. **New integration tests** for the DB-state-dependent deny arms: `device_deny_integration_test.go` (RFC 8628 §3.5 **`expired_token`** + cross-client **`invalid_grant`** spoofing guard, both driven deterministically via an injected handler clock) and two introspect `{active:false}` arms in `revoke_introspect_integration_test.go` (an **expired** and a **forged-signature** token must report inactive — 200 + `{active:false}`, never `active:true`, never 401). **Coverage floor `internal/api/oauth: 72 → 77`** at `floor(79.0 − 2)` per the slice-069 monotonic ratchet (tests + floor lifted in the same PR; the 90% advisory in `$security_critical_packages` is left unchanged — this lifts the hard floor only, AC-10). No AS runtime behavior changed (P0-422-5) — tests + floor only. All fixtures use neutral synthetic values; every subject token is minted in-process by the real fsstore-backed signer (no JWT/vendor-shaped literals — slice 314 / GitGuardian). **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/422-oauth-coverage-security-critical-error-branches.md`. Builds on slice 314; lifts toward slice 350's advisory; composes with slice 367.
- **test(coverage): slice 426 — lift five low-floor surfaces (`decisions` / `policies` / `me` / `freshnessdrift` / `policy`) via the slice-353 Q-2 pure-Go fast loop.** Five packages sat far under the 70% business-logic tier target because the enrolment-only drain batches enrolled their pre-existing integration suites without adding pure-Go branch tests: `internal/api/decisions` (18), `internal/freshnessdrift` (19), `internal/policy` (35), `internal/api/policies` (39), `internal/api/me` (43). Adds a `helpers_test.go` alongside each package's integration suite exercising the **pure-Go residue** with fast `t.Parallel()` table tests — no Postgres, no `//go:build integration` tag — so the new tests run in the existing `Go · build + test` job (AC-7). Covered: validators / normalizers (`policy.validateCreate`, `validSourceAttribution`, `mapCreateError`, the pgtype↔Go converters, `decisions.splitCSV`, the wire mappers and their nullable branches), pre-transaction input guards, and the handler **DENY** branches — `policy` / `me` / `policies` assert the RLS/role/identity rejection paths (missing-context → 401, non-approver → 403, missing/invalid caller identity → error), **not happy-path-only** (slice 333 Q-4 / P0-426-3), each driven with a **nil store** so no DB call is reachable on the guard path (P0-426-4). **Floors lifted in the same PR** (merged unit+integration via the slice-279 gate, `floor(measured − 2pp)`, monotonic ratchet): `internal/api/decisions` 18 → 37 (39.52%), `internal/api/me` 43 → 50 (52.14%), `internal/api/policies` 39 → 49 (51.33%), `internal/freshnessdrift` 19 → 24 (26.44%), `internal/policy` 35 → 70 (72.75%). `freshnessdrift` gained the least because `Scheduler.Run` / `SweepOnce` / `RefreshSubscriber.Start` genuinely require NATS + Postgres and stay integration-only — the residual is documented in the test header rather than over-lifting the floor (AC-6). No handler behavior changed — tests + floor lifts only. **Type: AFK.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/426-coverage-round-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/426-coverage-round-decisions-policies-me-freshnessdrift.md`. Follows the slice 279 / 312 / 422 coverage-round precedent.
- **ci: slice 416 — pin golangci-lint version (drop `version: latest`).** The `lint-go` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` invoked `golangci/golangci-lint-action` with `version: latest`, making the linter binary a floating dependency on the **required** `Go · lint` check: an upstream golangci-lint release could change what the gate enforces (new/changed analyzers) or bust the warm cache on the next CI run with zero review — the same failure class as slice 090's `govulncheck@latest` red-wall. Replaced `version: latest` with an explicit `version: v2.12.2` (latest stable golangci-lint v2.x, compatible with the repo's Go 1.26 toolchain and the existing `version: "2"`-schema `.golangci.yml`; runs clean — 0 issues — against current `main`). Mirrored the `SQLC_VERSION`-in-`justfile` precedent (slice 109): introduced a single-source `GOLANGCI_VERSION := "v2.12.2"` pin plus a `golangci-version-check` recipe that `just lint-go` now depends on, so local lint and CI enforce the identical analyzer set (CI/local parity, AC-3). The `golangci/golangci-lint-action` `uses:` line stays SHA-pinned to `82606bf` (slice 128 `actions-pin-check` invariant unregressed; the edit touches only the `with:` block). Both pins carry a comment documenting that version bumps land in dedicated `chore(golangci):` PRs so a new analyzer set is an auditable diff, never a surprise red check on an unrelated PR. **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/416-pin-golangci-lint-version.md`.
- **test: slice 411 — contract-tier goldens for the control-detail + audit-workspace routes.** Closes the slice-409 D1 / D6 deferral of the controls-detail (`/v1/controls/*`) and audit-workspace (`/v1/audit/*`) families. Lands the golden-file contract tier (ADR-0007) on the highest-traffic routes the `/e2e/` suite hand-mocks — the three control-detail tab reads (`web/e2e/control-detail-tabs.spec.ts` route-fulfills `/api/controls/{id}/{policies,risks,history}`) and the audit-workspace period index (`web/e2e/audits-header.spec.ts` reads `/api/audits` → `GET /v1/audit-periods`). **Seams (per-route, Option A):** an unexported `controlDetailReader` interface on the controldetail `Handler` carrying JUST the three read methods the policies/risks/history paths use (the `Evidence` tenant-wide-ledger path keeps the concrete `*Store` — deferred), and an unexported list-only `periodLister` on the auditperiods `Handler` carrying JUST `List`. Both are narrow by design (slice 409 D1 / slice 410 precedent: not a god-interface over the wider Store surfaces). `New(*Store)` / `New(*period.Store)` are byte-for-byte unchanged (P0-409-2): each wires the seam field to the same store, so production behavior is identical; every other handler keeps the concrete store. **Provider recorders** (`internal/api/controldetail/handler_contract_test.go` + `internal/api/auditperiods/handler_contract_test.go`, plus the shared `contractrecord_test.go` helper copied from slice 392/409/410) run on the plain `go test ./...` unit surface — no DB, no `//go:build integration` tag (P0-409-1) — injecting fixed-row stubs via the unexported `newHandlerWithReader` / `newHandlerWithLister`. The control-detail recorder routes through a chi mux so the `{id}` path param resolves (the difference from slice 410's query-param risks recorder). Four goldens (`web/lib/contracts/control-{policies,risks,history}.golden.json` + `audit-periods.golden.json`) record `populated` + `empty` variants exercising the nullable branches: opaque `inherent_score`/`residual_score` blobs + null `link_weight`, null `scope_cell`, and the omitempty `frozen_at`/`frozen_hash`/`frozen_by` (absent on open periods). **Verbatim-passthrough consumer asserts** (`web/lib/contracts/*.contract.test.ts`): unlike slice 410's transform-aware risks BFF, all four BFFs forward the upstream body unchanged (the lib fn returns `res.json()`; the route does `NextResponse.json(body)` / re-emits the upstream text), so the asserts are `toEqual(golden)` like the slice-409 dashboard panels. **Drift proven:** renaming `link_weight` → `weight` in `control-risks.golden.json` failed both halves (provider wire-shape mismatch; consumer `link_weight: expected false to be true`); golden restored, both green. **Zero-new-gate** (AC-4): rides the existing Go-unit + vitest surfaces; auditperiods gained a first unit-surface suite (0% → 14%), controldetail's merged 90% floor is unaffected (the seam is integration-covered + the recorder adds unit coverage). **Deferred (spillover):** the controls-detail long tail (`coverage`/`effectiveness`/`state`/`attestations` — separate packages `ucfcoverage`/`controlstate`/attest) and the audit-workspace tail (`populations`/`samples`/`walkthroughs`/`notes`) → slice 412. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/411-contract-tier-controls-audit-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/411-contract-tier-controls-audit-routes.md`. Parent: slice 409.
- **test: slice 410 — contract-tier golden for the dashboard top-risks panel (`GET /v1/risks`).** Closes the slice-409 D1 deferral of the dashboard top-risks panel. Adds the golden-file contract tier (ADR-0007) to `GET /v1/risks` so the slice-210 class of silent BFF↔atlas wire-shape drift is caught at the cheapest surface (Go-unit + vitest). **Seam (list-only Option A):** an unexported `riskLister` interface on the risks `Handler` carrying JUST the `List` method the `ListRisks` path uses — deliberately narrow rather than a ~7-method interface over the wide `*risk.Store` surface (Create/List/Get/Delete/Heatmap/ThemeOrgUnitHeatmap/…) a single golden would not justify (slice 409 D1). `New(*risk.Store)` is byte-for-byte unchanged (P0-409-2): it wires `lister` to the same store, so production behavior is identical; every other handler keeps the concrete `*risk.Store`. **Provider recorder** (`internal/api/risks/handler_contract_test.go`, plus the shared `contractrecord_test.go` helper copied from slice 392/409) runs on the plain `go test ./...` unit surface — no DB, no `//go:build integration` tag (P0-409-1) — injecting a fixed-row stub via the unexported `newHandlerWithLister`. The golden (`web/lib/contracts/risks.golden.json`) records `populated` + `empty` variants of the upstream `{risks: riskWire[], count}` envelope, including the opaque `inherent_score`/`residual_score` JSON blobs, the `linked_control_ids` array (populated + empty), and the nullable `review_due_at`/`accepted_until` (present on one row, omitted on another). **Transform-aware consumer assert** (`web/lib/contracts/risks.contract.test.ts`): unlike the slice-409 passthrough panels, the dashboard/risks BFF unwraps `body.risks` and re-wraps `{risks, count: risks.length}`, so the assert checks the BFF output equals `{risks: golden.risks, count: golden.risks.length}` — NOT `toEqual(golden)`. **Drift proven:** renaming `treatment` → `treatment_kind` in the golden failed both halves (provider wire-shape mismatch; consumer `treatment: expected 'undefined' to be 'string'`); golden restored, both green. **Zero-new-gate** (AC-4): rides the existing Go-unit + vitest surfaces; risks-package unit coverage rose 4.5% → 13.8%. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/410-contract-tier-risks-panel-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/410-contract-tier-risks-panel.md`. Parent: slice 409.
- **ci: slice 408 — integration-enrolment drain batch 8 (catalog/oscal/policy/risk tail) — closes slice 390.** Eighth and **final** batch of the slice-390 enrolment-backlog drain (sequential after slice 407); merging it shrinks `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` from 5 to **0** and closes slice 390. Five packages carried a `//go:build integration` tag but were absent from the `tests-integration` job's package list (catalogued by the slice-345 enrolment guard), so their integration suites had **never run in CI**: `internal/catalog/metrics`, `internal/oscal`, `internal/policy/pdf`, `internal/policy/seed`, `internal/risk/aggrule`. Added all five (per-leaf recursive `./internal/<pkg>/...`, each a single leaf package with one `integration_test.go` plus production source and no tracked subpackages — the bare-root form from slices 403/406 does not apply) to the `go test -tags=integration` invocation, inserted before the bare `./internal/api` root, and **emptied the slice-345 `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` allowlist** (monotonic shrink 5→0; the array is now intentionally empty; the guard self-handles the empty case — self-test 12/12 green; audit reports 87 tagged / 87 enrolled / 0 waived). **No test broke** — all five suites passed unmodified, in isolation and in a forced-uncached combined serial `-p 1` run (both orderings) alongside the contaminating `internal/demoseed` neighbour; no FK-wipe contamination surfaced. `internal/oscal`'s `TestExport_FrozenPeriodProducesSignedBundle` self-skips when the optional Python compliance-trestle bridge subprocess is unavailable (decision D2 in the test file) — this is pre-existing documented optional-precondition behaviour, identical local and in CI (CI provisions no Python bridge), NOT a masked failure; the load-bearing Aggregate-side invariant-10 "refuse a non-frozen period" check runs with no bridge. The slice-386 `metrics_catalog` NULL-encoding fix (`nonNilStrings` empty-slice→`[]` not SQL NULL on the NOT-NULL `source_slices` column) is genuinely exercised — `seed_test.go` asserts `nonNilStrings(nil)`/`nonNilStrings([])` both return non-nil and the integration `SeedFromEmbedded` round-trips the real INSERT. **Coverage:** four packages already carried hard floors honest against own-suite measurements (`catalog/metrics 76` vs 94.2%, `oscal 69` vs 78.9%, `policy/seed 87` vs 89.5%, `risk/aggrule 72` vs 74.6%) — left unchanged (conservative ratchets). `internal/policy/pdf` lifted off `excludes` to a new floor `68` (`floor(70.8 − 2)`). **Latent exclude-shadow bug fixed in-place:** the `internal/policy/` excludes prefix (intended only for the root `internal/policy` handler package) silently shadowed _every_ `internal/policy/*` subpackage in `cmd/scripts/coverage-gate` (a package matching any exclude prefix is skipped), so the pre-existing `internal/policy/seed: 87` floor was dead config and the new `policy/pdf: 68` floor would have been too. Removed the blanket `internal/policy/` exclude (+ its `$exclude_justifications` entry) and gave the root its own honest floor `internal/policy: 35` (`floor(37.1 − 2)`), un-shadowing the seed/pdf subpackage floors so they are now actually enforced (verified via merged-profile gate run: all policy-family floors checked + passing, none skipped). **Final slice-390 tally** across all eight batches: real finds were slice 314 (device-flow `postForm` 404), slice 402 (a real `adminauditlog` product bug + a stale tenancy-middleware test), slice 405 (`ucfcoverage` stale-test + a genuine `TRUNCATE … CASCADE` FK-wipe contamination fix), slice 406 (stale OIDC nonce-ordering test); the benign batches were 401/403/404/407 and this one (no test broke). `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` is now **empty** — the slice-345 ratchet has reached zero and the structural enrolment gap slice 390 set out to close is closed. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/408-integration-drain-batch-8-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/408-integration-drain-batch-8-catalog-oscal-policy-risk-tail.md`. Parent: slice 390.
- **ci: slice 407 — integration-enrolment drain batch 7 (freshness drift family).** Seventh batch of the slice-390 enrolment-backlog drain (sequential after slice 406). Four packages carried a `//go:build integration` tag but were absent from the `tests-integration` job's package list (catalogued by the slice-345 enrolment guard), so their integration suites had **never run in CI**: `internal/drift`, `internal/freshness`, `internal/freshnessdrift`, `internal/exception`. Added all four (per-leaf recursive `./internal/<pkg>/...`, each a single leaf package with one `integration_test.go` plus production source and no tracked subpackages — the bare-root form from slices 403/406 does not apply) to the `go test -tags=integration` invocation, inserted before the bare `./internal/api` root, and removed all four from the slice-345 `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` allowlist (monotonic shrink 9→5; guard self-test 12/12 green; audit reports 82 enrolled / 5 waived). **No test broke** — this is the benign half of the drain (cf. slice 406): all four suites passed unmodified, in isolation and in a combined serial `-p 1` run alongside the contaminating `internal/demoseed` neighbour. **No FK-wipe contamination is structurally possible** despite this family heavily referencing `controls`/`evidence_records`/`control_evaluations` (the exact RESTRICT-FK surface that bit `ucfcoverage` in slice 405): every test scopes to a fresh random tenant (`freshTenant` → `uuid.NewString()`) and every cleanup `DELETE` is `WHERE tenant_id = $1` — there is no global un-scoped `DELETE FROM controls`, so the slice-405 cross-tenant-wipe failure mode cannot arise. **Coverage floors** added at `floor(own_suite − 2)`, all four lifted off `excludes` (+ their `$exclude_justifications` entries removed): `drift 84` (86.0%), `freshness 83` (85.2%), `exception 76` (78.7%), `freshnessdrift 19` (21.8%). `freshnessdrift`'s low own-suite is REAL not a transitive-load phantom — its `worker.go` carries a NATS JetStream `RefreshSubscriber` + scheduler loop the suite deliberately does not drive (the handler delegates to the same `RefreshTenant` the DB-path tests cover), and a slice-404/405-style phantom check confirmed **zero transitive coverage** from other enrolled suites (`[no statements]` everywhere; the only non-test caller is `cmd/atlas/main.go`), so the merged CI profile equals the package's own 21.8% and the floor is an honest ratchet documenting the NATS-subscriber gap. None of the four has unit tests (0.0% unit), so the merged profile equals the integration-only number; floors are conservative own-suite-anchored bounds. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/407-integration-drain-batch-7-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/407-integration-drain-batch-7-freshness-drift-family.md`. Parent: slice 390.
- **ci: slice 406 — integration-enrolment drain batch 6 (auth substrate + keystore).** Sixth batch of the slice-390 enrolment-backlog drain (sequential after slice 405). Four packages carried a `//go:build integration` tag but were absent from the `tests-integration` job's package list (catalogued by the slice-345 enrolment guard), so their integration suites had **never run in CI**: `internal/auth`, `internal/auth/keystore/fsstore`, `internal/mcp`, `internal/observability/otel`. Added all four to the `go test -tags=integration` invocation — `internal/auth` enrolled as the **bare root** `./internal/auth` (it holds a single `package auth_test` `integration_test.go` exercising the slice-034 users/sessions/api_keys RLS round-trips; the `./internal/auth/...` recursive form would double-enrol the oidc/jwtmw/users/oauth* subpackages that are tracked and enrolled separately, mirroring the slice-403 api-root bare-form precedent and the existing `./internal/audit` bare entry), and the other three per-leaf recursive — inserted before the bare `./internal/api` root, and removed all four from the slice-345 `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` allowlist (monotonic shrink 13→9; guard self-test 12/12 green; audit reports 78 enrolled / 9 waived). **One silently-broken test surfaced** (the never-run failure class, matching slices 314/402/405): `internal/auth`'s `TestOIDC_CallbackStateMismatchRejected` failed with `ErrNonceMismatch` instead of the expected `ErrStateMismatch`. **Stale-test fix, not a product bug**: slice 365 added an OIDC ID-token-replay guard that checks the `NonceCookie` *presence\* before the state-mismatch (CSRF) check in `HandleCallback`. The pre-365 test set State/Verifier/Idp cookies but never set the new NonceCookie, so the flow short-circuited at the nonce-presence gate before reaching the state check it is named for. The ordering is correct, intentional, and locked by slice 365's P0-365-1; the fix is to set a non-empty NonceCookie so the test exercises the state-mismatch path (mirroring the canonical slice-365 `ErrStateMismatch` test in `oidc_nonce_integration_test.go`). No test skipped or deleted. The sibling `TestOIDC_CallbackMissingCookieRejected` (no cookies at all) still correctly returns `ErrStateMismatch` at the first cookie check. **No FK-wipe contamination**: the auth suite scopes every test to a fresh random tenant UUID and cleans up via the admin (`BYPASSRLS`) pool, and `mcp`/`otel`/`fsstore` do not touch the shared DB (fake HTTP platform / in-memory exporter / `t.TempDir()` keystore respectively); a combined serial `-p 1` run alongside the contaminating `internal/demoseed` neighbour stayed green. **Coverage**: no floor changes — `internal/auth/keystore/fsstore` (74), `internal/mcp` (80), `internal/observability/otel` (92) already carry hard floors enforced against the slice-279 merged unit+integration `-coverpkg=./...` profile, and enrolment of their own integration suites can only raise the merged number; `internal/auth` (the bare path) holds **no `package auth` production code** (only the external `auth_test` integration suite), so `go test -cover` reports `[no statements]` — it needs neither a floor nor an `excludes` entry (honest per slices 396/401–405, cleaner than the `emptyset` doc.go case). **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/406-integration-drain-batch-6-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/406-integration-drain-batch-6-auth-substrate-+-keystore.md`. Parent: slice 390.
- **ci: slice 405 — integration-enrolment drain batch 5 (api domain handlers B).** Fifth batch of the slice-390 enrolment-backlog drain (sequential after slice 404). Five packages carried a `//go:build integration` tag but were absent from the `tests-integration` job's package list (catalogued by the slice-345 enrolment guard), so their integration suites had **never run in CI**: `internal/api/questionnaires`, `internal/api/ucfcoverage`, `internal/api/emptyset`, `internal/api/freshnessdrift`, `internal/audit/notes`. Added all five (per-leaf recursive) to the `go test -tags=integration` invocation, inserted before the bare `./internal/api` root, and removed them from the slice-345 `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` allowlist (monotonic shrink 18→13; guard self-test 12/12 green; audit reports 74 enrolled / 13 waived). **One silently-broken test surfaced** (the never-run failure class, matching slices 314/402): `internal/api/ucfcoverage`'s `TestControlCoverage_Slice256_InScopeRowReturnsNumeric` failed because its setup never seeded a `scope_cells` row — the control's legacy match-all `applicability_expr` resolved against an empty scope-cell universe, so the row rendered out-of-scope and coverage came back `null`. **Stale-test fix, not a product bug** (the null is the documented correct behaviour for an empty universe): added a `seedScopeCell` helper, seeded one cell in the in-scope test, and added `scope_cells` to `wipeTenantState`'s cleanup so it does not leak across tests. No test skipped or deleted. **A second `ucfcoverage` bug then surfaced in CI** — a genuine test-isolation FK-wipe bug (the whole suite failed with RESTRICT-FK violations, SQLSTATE 23503). Both wipe helpers did a global `DELETE FROM controls` without clearing the FK dependents, and under CI's serial `-p 1` run prior packages (`internal/demoseed` + the audit-sample suites) leave rows referencing controls under the shared tenant. The FK closure is deep — `sample_evidence → evidence_records → controls`, all `ON DELETE RESTRICT` — so enumerating children is whack-a-mole (an enumerate-children first attempt just advanced the error one level deeper to `sample_evidence_evidence_fk`). Fixed robustly with `TRUNCATE … CASCADE` (`TRUNCATE controls RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE`, and `TRUNCATE controls, framework_scopes, scope_cells RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE`), which clears the full transitive dependent set atomically and does not touch `scf_anchors`/`tenants` — mirroring the repo's existing TRUNCATE-CASCADE precedent (`internal/evidence/ingest`, `internal/platform/status`). Test-harness-only fix (no product change, `ucfcoverage` floor unchanged at 70). Reproduced the exact deeper CI condition locally (full `sample_evidence → evidence_records → controls` contamination chain under the shared tenant) and verified the fix resolves it in the faithful `demoseed → ucfcoverage` `-p 1` ordering, clearing the contaminating `sample_evidence`/`evidence_records` rows. **Coverage floors** added at `floor(own_suite − 2)`: `questionnaires 54` (56.6%), `ucfcoverage 70` (72.2%), `freshnessdrift 81` (83.6%), `audit/notes 83` (85.0%); `ucfcoverage`/`freshnessdrift`/`audit/notes` lifted off `excludes` (+ their `$exclude_justifications` entries removed), `questionnaires` was never gated and gains its first hard floor. `internal/api/emptyset` is a **zero-statement package** (`doc.go` only; the cross-cutting empty-set sweep lives in an external `_test` package) — it reports `[no statements]`, so it is **kept on `excludes`** rather than given a fabricated floor (honest per slices 396/401–404). Transitive coverage from the existing CI list is small in every case (≤5.6% vs own-suite 56.6–85.0%), confirming the own-suite-anchored floors are real, conservative ratchets, not transitive-load phantoms. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/405-integration-drain-batch-5-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/405-integration-drain-batch-5-api-domain-handlers-B.md`. Parent: slice 390.

The slice-396 phantom test passes in its strongest form: running the entire existing 64-package CI integration list (none of which is a target) with `-coverpkg=` pointed at each target yields **0.0% transitive** coverage, so every floor is a real, phantom-free ratchet wholly earned by the package's own suite. `policies` at 41.2% is low (its suites cover the empty-set + ack-rate paths) but honest and phantom-free — noted as a future test-coverage candidate, not a defect. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/404-integration-drain-batch-4-decisions.md`. - **ci: slice 402 — integration-enrolment drain batch 2 (admin-creds surface).** Second batch of the slice-390 enrolment-backlog drain (sequential after slice 401). Five admin-surface packages carried a `//go:build integration` tag but were absent from the `tests-integration` job's package list (catalogued by the slice-345 enrolment guard), so their integration suites had **never run in CI**: `internal/api/adminauditlog` (slice-124/135 unified audit-log + data export), `internal/api/adminauthzbundle`, `internal/api/admincreds` (slice-034 admin credentials), `internal/api/admindemo`, `internal/api/adminsso`. Added all five to the `go test -tags=integration` invocation and removed them from the slice-345 `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` allowlist (monotonic shrink 33→28; guard self-test 12/12 green). **Two suites surfaced failures** the unenrolled state had hidden. `admincreds`: two tests (`TestRevokeInvalidates`, `TestRotateGivesSuccessor`) failed because `newHandler`'s test router injected the admin credential but never ran `tenancymw.Middleware`, so the store's tenant-scoped tx hit "tenancy: no tenant in context" (500) and `Issue` returned an empty id → 400 on the follow-up — a **stale-test** gap latent since the slice-033 tenancy-middleware contract; fixed by adding `tenancymw.Middleware` to the test router (mirroring production + the sibling `adminsso`/`admindemo` suites). `adminauditlog`: see the Fixed entry below (a real product bug). **Coverage floors** lifted off `excludes` to real per-package floors at `floor(own_suite − 2)`: `adminauditlog 68` (70.8%), `admincreds 62` (64.4%), `admindemo 62` (64.9%), `adminsso 63` (65.1%); plus a new floor `adminauthzbundle 83` (85.0%, never excluded). All five floors are honest own-suite-anchored numbers — the three packages with zero unit-test files (adminauditlog/admincreds/adminsso) have merged == integration own-suite, so there is no slice-396 transitive-load phantom. Test fixtures use neutral placeholder strings. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/402-integration-drain-batch-2-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/402-integration-drain-batch-2-admin-creds-surface.md`. Parent: slice 390.
- **ci: slice 401 — integration-enrolment drain batch 1 (oidc/jwtmw/users/period).** First security-critical batch of the slice-390 enrolment-backlog drain. Four packages carried a `//go:build integration` tag but were absent from the `tests-integration` job's package list (catalogued by the slice-345 enrolment guard), so their integration suites had **never run in CI**: `internal/auth/oidc` (OIDC RP + nonce, slice 365 family), `internal/auth/jwtmw` (JWT middleware), `internal/auth/users` (user/identity store), `internal/audit/period` (audit-period freezing, constitutional invariant #10). Added all four to the `go test -tags=integration` invocation and removed them from the slice-345 `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` allowlist (monotonic shrink 37→33). All four suites ran **green on first enrolment against the real Postgres harness** — no silently-broken tests this batch (oidc 11 / jwtmw 16 / users 5 / period 10 sub-test RUN lines; zero skips, zero fails). **Coverage floors:** `internal/auth/oidc: 67` and `internal/audit/period: 70` lifted off the `excludes` list to real per-package floors at `floor(measured − 2)` against the merged unit+integration profile (oidc 69.5% own-suite = merged → pure own coverage, real floor; period 72.4% own-suite / 76.7% merged → real floor with a conservative own-suite-anchored margin). `internal/auth/jwtmw` already carried a hard floor (82; merged 93.2%) — unchanged. `internal/auth/users` **kept on `excludes`**: its own bootstrap-only integration suite covers just 20.3% of the package; the remainder is transitive coverage from admin/oauth handlers (a slice-396 transitive-load phantom), too unstable for an honest hard floor. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/401-integration-drain-batch-1-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/401-integration-drain-batch-1-security-critical.md`. Parent: slice 390.
- **test(oauth): slice 314 — lift `internal/api/oauth` coverage to 74.7% merged (enrol in CI integration job).** The slice-187+ OAuth 2.0 Authorization Server HTTP surface (921 statements; RFC 9068 JWT profile + RFC 8693 token-exchange + RFC 7636 PKCE + RFC 8628 device grant + RFC 7009 revocation + RFC 7662 introspection) sat at **15.7%** merged coverage in slice 312's round-3 audit. Two-pronged lift per the slice disposition. **(1) CI integration-enrolment.** The slice-187/188/189/190/191 integration suites carried the `//go:build integration` tag but had never been in the `tests-integration` job's package list, so they had **never run in CI**. Added `./internal/api/oauth/...` to that list and removed the package from the slice-345 `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` allowlist. **(2) New unit + integration suites for the untested branches.** New unit suites assert the pre-DB RFC-conformance gates with real RFC error-code + claim assertions (not status-code-only): `introspect_test.go` (RFC 7662 §2.1/§2.3 — content-type, missing-token, inspector-auth-failure), `revoke_test.go` (RFC 7009 §2.1/§2.2 — self-revoke-via-bearer foreign-subject + unsigned-bearer rejections, client-cred form gate), `authorize_extra_test.go` (RFC 6749 §4.1.1 + RFC 7636 — response_type/PKCE-method/required-param gates), `wellknown_test.go` (RFC 8414/8628 conditional discovery fields), `device_test.go` (RFC 8628 §3.4/§3.5 — not-configured 503, missing-params, slow_down poll tracker, approval-snapshot→JWT claim projection incl. super_admin copy-not-synthesize, approve/deny auth gates), and `helpers_test.go` (requestIP / device-code generators / login-redirect builder / nil-pool resolver default). New `user_resolver_integration_test.go` exercises the slice-192 `DBUserResolver` (`readSessionIdentity` + `queryUserRoles` + `ResolveForOAuth`) against real Postgres+RLS — the authorize integration suite used a stub resolver, leaving the real resolver at 0%. **Enrolment surfaced a latent device-flow integration-test bug** (the `postForm` helper appends `/oauth/token`, so the device suite's `postForm(srv.URL+"/oauth/device_authorization", …)` was POSTing to a doubled `…/oauth/device_authorization/oauth/token` → 404) that the unenrolled state had hidden for five slices; fixed in-suite with a dedicated `postFormTo` helper. **Coverage floor** `internal/api/oauth: 72` added at `floor(74.7 − 2)` per the slice-069 ratchet (tests + floor in the same PR). All test fixtures use neutral placeholder token strings. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/314-oauth-coverage-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/314-coverage-internal-api-oauth.md`.

- **refactor(web): slice 396 — retire the `web/lib/api.ts` barrel (slice 370 Phase 3).** Completes the slice-370 god-file refactor lineage (Phase 1 split → slice-395 Phase 2 import-site migration → this Phase 3 deletion). The barrel was a 37-line `export *` transition shim over the 18 per-domain `web/lib/api/*.ts` modules; slice 395 migrated all 177 import sites to the per-domain paths, leaving it with zero importers (verified: `grep -rnE "['\"]@/lib/api['\"]" web/` excluding `@/lib/api/<domain>` returns zero hits). **Deleted `web/lib/api.ts`**; `git mv`'d `web/lib/api.test.ts` → `web/lib/api/base.test.ts` and repointed its import from the barrel (`./api`) to `apiBaseURL`'s real home (`./base`). **Coverage-threshold reconciliation (slice-347 ratchet):** removed the `lib/api.ts` floor (file gone); `lib/api/base.ts` keeps its floor 98 (the barrel's coverage was really base.ts's `apiBaseURL` resolution). Retiring the barrel also exposed that four domain modules — `board.ts` (17%), `risk-hierarchy.ts` (8%), `exceptions.ts` (2%), `evidence.ts` (1%), all `functions:0` — had **no direct test importer**; their nonzero floors were a measurement artifact of the barrel's `export *` transitively module-loading them during the single `apiBaseURL` barrel test. With the barrel gone they measure their true 0% (no test calls their functions), so per P0-347-3 ("the ratchet starts at truth") their phantom floors were removed into the omitted-zero set — NOT a real-coverage regression, and the inverse of slice 369's authzmw case (real coverage never existed here; genuine coverage for these fetch wrappers is the slice-349/392 contract-tier rollout's job). The slice-370 `max-lines:600` eslint guard on `lib/api/**/*.ts` is KEPT (it prevents the god-file re-accreting; it is not a barrel exception). **AC-1:** barrel deleted, no broken imports. **AC-2:** `tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint`, all 1204 vitest tests across 122 files, and a full `next build` (106/106 pages) all green. **AC-3:** coverage thresholds reconciled, no real-coverage regression. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/396-web-api-barrel-retire-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/396-web-api-barrel-retire.md`.

- **test(e2e): slice 399 — fix bff-cookie prod-build spec + make prod-build CI leg blocking (closes slice 387 spillover).** Slice 387 built the `frontend-playwright-prod-build` CI harness (build:standalone → boot `node .next/standalone/web/server.js` → `ATLAS_PROD_BUILD=1`) and shipped it ADVISORY/logo-only because the companion `web/e2e/bff-cookie-production-build.spec.ts` (slice 146) carried two dev-server-shaped assumptions that had never executed until the harness existed. This slice re-shapes those two assertions, re-includes the spec in the job, and drops `continue-on-error` so the leg blocks on red. **(AC-1) Assertion 1 re-shaped RSC-aware.** Under the production build the dashboard panels are server-rendered (RSC fetches data server-side during SSR), so the browser fires zero client-side `/api/dashboard/` calls — the old `bffResponses.length > 0` was a false assertion for the prod build, NOT a product regression. The slice-146 cookie regression (NODE_ENV-coupled cookie attribute drops the auth cookie on the BFF round-trip under the standalone server's plain-HTTP transport → proxy redirect to /login → panel `JSON.parse` of login HTML → "Unexpected token '<'") is now guarded three ways, none green-washed: (1) the topbar "Sign out" control must be visible (server-rendered only for an authenticated session — proves the cookie survived the round-trip, the exact property slice 146 fixed); (2) the JSON-parse-HTML signature ("Unexpected token '<'") and the panel error Alert (`[data-testid$="-error"]`) must be absent (scoped to the regression signature, not a blanket "no panel errors"); (3) the content-type guard is retained but conditional — any observed `/api/dashboard/` response must be `application/json`, with the `length > 0` requirement dropped so it no-ops cleanly when RSC fires no client call. If the slice-146 fix is reverted the standalone server drops the cookie, the RSC render fails auth → login page → "Sign out" disappears and/or any client panel fetch surfaces "Unexpected token '<'" — the spec fails red on the regression it guards. **(AC-2) Assertion 2 cookie-domain fix.** `context.addCookies` now derives the cookie domain from the Playwright `baseURL` (the served origin) instead of `new URL(authedPage.url())` — before navigation `authedPage.url()` is `about:blank`, whose empty hostname made Playwright reject the cookie ("Cookie should have a url or a domain/path pair").
