Same baseURL-origin pattern `web/e2e/fixtures.ts` already uses for the bearer cookie. **(AC-3)** `.github/workflows/ci.yml`'s `frontend-playwright-prod-build` job now runs BOTH prod-build specs (`bff-cookie-production-build.spec.ts` + `logo-render-production-build.spec.ts`) and `continue-on-error: true` is removed — the leg is now blocking. **(AC-4)** the slice-153 logo spec is unchanged and still runs. Uses `ATLAS_JWT_COOKIE` (renamed in slice 397). Deterministic: Playwright auto-waiting web-first assertions + the `response` event listener, no fixed sleeps. The `test.skip(!ATLAS_PROD_BUILD)` guard is retained (scopes the spec to the standalone leg, prevents dev-server green-washing). `.github/branch-protection.json` NOT modified (promotion to a required check is a follow-up after green runs, per slice 387 D6). **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/399-bff-cookie-prod-build-spec-fix-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/399-bff-cookie-prod-build-spec-body-fix.md`.

- **refactor(web): slice 397 — rename `SESSION_COOKIE` → `ATLAS_JWT_COOKIE` (closes slice 328 M-3).** The BFF cookie constant exported from `web/lib/auth.ts` was named `SESSION_COOKIE` but, since slice 206, resolves to the JWT cookie value `"atlas_jwt"` — a misleading name flagged by the slice 328 code-review audit as finding M-3 ("the underlying behavior is correct, only the symbol name is misleading"). Slice 328 §D4 recommended bundling the rename into slice 370; slice 370 D2 deferred to slice 395 (Phase 2 import migration); slice 395's hard no-touch rule on `web/lib/contracts/` (slice 349/392 golden tier) forced an atomic symbol rename — which must touch the two golden-tier contract _tests_ that import the symbol — to split out here. **Pure symbol rename, zero wire/value change (P0-397-1):** the cookie value stays `"atlas_jwt"`; every `cookies().get/set/delete` and BFF `Authorization: Bearer` forward is byte-identical in behavior — only the identifier changed. **354 bare `SESSION_COOKIE` occurrences renamed across 127 files** (BFF routes, `web/proxy.ts` middleware, `web/lib/api/bff.ts`, `web/app/login/actions.ts` cookie writer, components, e2e + e2e-audit fixtures/specs, `web/scripts/capture-readme-screenshots.ts`'s standalone local const, and the two golden-tier contract tests `me.contract.test.ts` + `demo-status.contract.test.ts`) via a word-boundary-aware `perl -i 's/\bSESSION_COOKIE\b/ATLAS_JWT_COOKIE/g'` codemod. **`OIDC_SESSION_COOKIE` is a distinct cookie (`"atlas_session"`) and was NOT touched (P0-397-2):** the `\b` word boundary provably does not match the `SESSION_COOKIE` substring inside `OIDC_SESSION_COOKIE` (the preceding `_` is a word char), verified to remain at all 38 occurrences. The self-acknowledging rename-intent comment at `web/lib/auth.ts:10-14` was rewritten to reflect the completed rename. **No golden shape changed:** only the two `*.contract.test.ts` harness files' local `cookieStore.set(...)` identifier moved; no `*.golden.json` recorded contract was touched (JUDGMENT note in the slice doc confirms this narrow golden-tier-test touch is the mechanical-local-edit kind, not a contract-semantics change). **AC-1:** `web/lib/auth.ts` exports `ATLAS_JWT_COOKIE`, no `SESSION_COOKIE` export remains, `OIDC_SESSION_COOKIE` unchanged. **AC-2:** zero bare `SESSION_COOKIE` references remain (grep-clean, excluding `OIDC_SESSION_COOKIE`). **AC-3:** `tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint` (0 errors; 2 pre-existing unused-eslint-disable warnings in the screenshots script, unchanged from `main`), all 1204 vitest tests across 122 files (incl. golden-tier contracts), and a full `next build` all green; the Playwright e2e suite (requires the full atlas + Postgres + `/v1/test/issue-jwt` stack) runs in CI — the rename is compile-time-only and the e2e specs typecheck + the production build compiles every module they import. **No auth/cookie bug surfaced** — the three load-bearing auth files (`bff.ts`, `proxy.ts`, `login/actions.ts`) show identifier-only diffs. **Does NOT auto-merge — golden-tier touch needs review (P0-397-3).** **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/397-session-cookie-symbol-rename-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/397-session-cookie-symbol-rename.md`.

- **fix(web): slice 398 — fix pre-existing `tsc --noEmit` errors in three web test files (slice 395 spillover).** Slice 395's `tsc --noEmit` verification surfaced that the project's standalone `npm run typecheck` was already red on `main` with 15 strict-mode type errors across three untouched test files — none introduced by 395, and not gating CI today because the web CI job runs `next build` (which type-checks the production graph, not these test files) rather than standalone `tsc --noEmit`. All 1204 vitest tests run green at runtime; the gap is in the standalone typecheck only. This slice makes `cd web && npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 with type-correctness-only changes (P0-398-1: zero runtime-behavior change). **(1) `web/lib/auth/oauth-client.test.ts` (3 errors):** dropped two genuinely-unused `@ts-expect-error` directives (TS2578) and replaced the `window` global stub's partial-`location` suppression with an explicit `as unknown as Location` / `as Window & typeof globalThis` cast so the mock is type-complete without suppression. **(2) `web/next-config.test.ts` (1 error, TS7053):** the `config.rewrites()` return type is the `NextConfig` union (`Rewrite[] | {beforeFiles?…}`), un-indexable by `[0]`; narrowed via the same `as Array<{source; destination}>` cast the sibling test at line 115 already uses (runtime value is a plain array). **(3) `web/scripts/capture-readme-screenshots.test.ts` (11 errors, TS2345):** the test deliberately passes partial env objects to `assertCaptureSafe` to exercise the slice-132 safety gate, but the helper's param was typed `NodeJS.ProcessEnv` (requires `NODE_ENV`). Widened the production helper's signature to `Partial<NodeJS.ProcessEnv>` (sanctioned by the slice doc's "What ships" item 1) — a type-refinement, not a paper-over: the gate only reads `ATLAS_DEMO_SEED` + `ATLAS_HTTP_URL`, the `= process.env` default stays assignable, and no runtime path changes. **AC-1:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0. **AC-2:** `npm run lint` (0 errors; 2 pre-existing unused-eslint-disable warnings in the same source file, unchanged from `main`), all 1204 vitest tests across 122 files, and a full `next build` all stay green. Optional CI-wiring of `npm run typecheck` (slice doc "What ships" item 2) NOT done here — a `ci.yml` edit is out of scope this batch (slice 387 owns `ci.yml`). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/398-web-tsc-noemit-pre-existing-test-type-errors.md`.

- **refactor(web): slice 395 — migrate `@/lib/api` import sites to per-domain modules (slice 370 Phase 2).** Phase 2 of slice 370's three-phase api-client refactor. Slice 370 (Phase 1) split the `web/lib/api.ts` god-file into 18 per-domain modules under `web/lib/api/` behind a backward-compat barrel (`web/lib/api.ts` `export *`s each domain file) so the import sites kept resolving unchanged. This slice mechanically repoints every `import { … } from "@/lib/api"` to the specific per-domain path that exports each symbol (`@/lib/api/anchors`, `@/lib/api/dashboard`, etc.), so the barrel can be retired in Phase 3 (slice 396). **177 import sites migrated** across `web/app/`, `web/components/`, `web/lib/`, and the `web/e2e/board-pack-export-e2e.spec.ts` spec — 176 via a symbol→module codemod (the map was derived by parsing the 18 barrel-re-exported modules' actual `export` statements, which came out to exactly 219 symbols with zero collisions, cross-checking the 370 split table), plus one hand-migrated namespace import (`import * as api` + its `vi.mock` target retargeted from `@/lib/api` to `@/lib/api/dashboard` in `dashboard-prefetch.test.ts`). **AC-1:** zero `from "@/lib/api"` barrel imports remain (grep-clean); the barrel file itself is untouched and still works (its deletion is Phase 3 / slice 396 — P0-395-2). **AC-3 / no wire change (P0-395-1):** pure import-path relocation — the codemod rewrites only `import` specifiers, preserving `type` modifiers (top-level and inline) and aliases; no function signature, return type, or wire shape changed. `tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint` (0 errors), all 1204 vitest tests across 122 files, the slice-347 per-file coverage gate, and a full `next build` (106/106 static pages) all green. Import-path-only changes do not move v8's per-file attribution, so no coverage floor tripped. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/395-web-api-import-site-migration-decisions.md` (D1 source-derived symbol map; D2 codemod design; D3 namespace-import + `vi.mock` retarget; D4 board-spec duplicate-import consolidation; D5 sibling-module imports left untouched; D6 SESSION_COOKIE/M-3 rename DEFERRED to spillover slice 397 because an atomic rename must touch golden-tier `web/lib/contracts/`, which slice 395's brief forbids). Slice doc: `docs/issues/395-web-api-import-site-migration.md`.
- **refactor(web): slice 370 — split `web/lib/api.ts` into per-domain modules (closes slice 328 H-2).** Slice 328's code-review audit (finding H-2) flagged `web/lib/api.ts` as a 2901-LOC, 219-export god-file violating the per-domain split convention already established next door (`web/lib/api/bff.ts`, `audit-server.ts`, `metrics.ts`, the `*-export.ts` family). This is **Phase 1** of the slice doc's three-phase plan: a pure mechanical relocation of all 219 exports into 17 per-domain modules under `web/lib/api/` — `base.ts` (the public `apiBaseURL` + `APIError` primitives), `anchors.ts`, `controls-list.ts`, `vendors.ts`, `framework-scopes.ts`, `attest.ts`, `admin.ts` (credentials/features/super-admins/tenants/demo/sso, co-located as one admin domain), `control-detail.ts`, `dashboard.ts`, `risk-hierarchy.ts`, `board.ts`, `calendar.ts`, `risks.ts`, `audit-periods.ts`, `evidence.ts`, `exceptions.ts`, `policies.ts`, `me.ts` — plus an internal-only `_shared.ts` holding the two formerly-private fetch helpers (`apiFetch`, `bffControlFetch`). `web/lib/api.ts` becomes a backward-compat barrel (`export *` from each domain file, EXCEPT `_shared.ts`) so the 176 existing `@/lib/api` import sites resolve unchanged — **zero import-site churn in this PR** (the import-site migration is Phase 2, the barrel deletion Phase 3; both filed as spillover). **No wire change (P0-370-1):** the barrel's export set is verified byte-for-byte the same 219 symbols as the god-file (export-name diff: 219 → 219, none added/dropped); `tsc --noEmit` clean for all api modules; all 1204 vitest tests across 122 files green. **Coverage ratchet (slice 347 contract):** the split shifts per-file v8 accounting, so the stale `lib/api.ts` floor (16/88/15/16) is replaced by per-domain floors seeded at `floor(measured − 2pp)` for the 12 split-out files carrying non-zero statement coverage; the barrel itself measures fully-exercised (floor 98) and the four modules that measured 0% statements (`attest.ts`, `framework-scopes.ts`, `me.ts`, `vendors.ts`) are omitted under the documented `$omitted_zero_pct` methodology (their coverage lived under the god-file's 16% aggregate, no per-function signal) — no floor lowered, the aggregate bar holds. **AC-5:** new `max-lines: 600` eslint rule (hard `error`, decision D4) scoped to `lib/api/**/*.ts` (test files exempt) keeps the god-file from re-accreting; every new module is well under the cap. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/370-web-api-client-split-decisions.md` (D1 per-domain granularity; D2 SESSION_COOKIE/M-3 deferred to Phase 2; D3 one-window shim retention; D4 hard-fail eslint; D5 `_shared.ts` helper extraction; D6 anchor types co-located; D7 coverage ratchet procedure). Slice doc: `docs/issues/370-web-api-client-split.md`.

- **ci: slice 393 — wire slice-353 QA-tactical scripts into CI (closes slice 353 spillover).** Slice 353 shipped three QA-tooling scripts (`scripts/check-coverage-excludes.sh`, `scripts/assertion-density.sh`, `scripts/measure-integration-wallclock.sh`) with passing self-tests and `justfile` targets but could not wire them into `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (batch-166 reserved `ci.yml` to avoid a merge collision, mirroring the slice 369 → 391 precedent). This slice adds the three CI surfaces. **(Q-5) coverage-exclude justification lint — HARD FAIL.** New blocking `coverage-excludes-check` meta-lint job (mirrors the self-contained `actions-pin-check` / `openapi-drift-check` / `integration-enrolment-check` shape: unconditional, sub-second, pure jq, no `changes.code` gate, no stub-twin): runs the self-test then `check-coverage-excludes.sh`, failing the build when any `excludes` prefix in `cmd/scripts/coverage-thresholds.json` lacks a non-empty `$exclude_justifications` entry or an orphan justification exists. **(Q-6) assertion-density — ADVISORY (non-blocking).** New informational `QA · assertion density` job (mirrors the slice 120 `phantom-deps` pattern: `continue-on-error`, real-job/stub-sibling split on `changes.code`, sticky PR comment via a hidden HTML marker, report uploaded as an artifact): runs the self-test then `assertion-density.sh` (advisory by design — exit 0 even with warnings, P0-393-2 / slice 353 D7). **(Q-8) integration wall-clock watermark — clean-main recorder.** A `Record integration wall-clock watermark` step on the existing `Go · integration` job, gated to clean main (`github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`), feeds the job's own measured duration (the "Run integration tests" step is now bookended with `date +%s` into `ATLAS_WALLCLOCK_START`) into `measure-integration-wallclock.sh` in RECORD mode (`WALLCLOCK_SECONDS` set ⇒ no second suite run); it appends to `docs/integration-wallclock.tsv` and warns — does NOT fail — at the 20-min Phase-A/B-split trigger (recorder, not a gate, P0-393-3 / slice 353 D6). CI-wiring only — no script logic changed (P0-393-1); no auto-merge (P0-393-4). **Type: MECHANICAL.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/393-qa-tactical-ci-wiring.md`.

- **test(qa): slice 353 — QA strategy tactical round 1 (slice 333 bundle).** Addresses the medium/low tactical findings from slice 333's QA strategy audit (`docs/audits/333-qa-strategy-gap-analysis.md`) that bundled per AC-4. **Sub-theme A (docs/conventions):** `CLAUDE.md` "Testing discipline" gains a "Test-tier conventions" subsection — formalizes the pure-Go pre-DB `helpers_test.go` convention (Q-2, slice 290 pattern); decides the React component-test surface OUT of scope for v1 (Q-3, e2e is the de facto component tier); documents the integration-enrolment policy (Q-7, "every package importing `internal/db/dbx` or setting `app.current_tenant` SHOULD ship `integration_test.go`", enforced by slice 345's discovery primitive); and decides the Go integration tier carries no `-retry` (Q-16, investigate every flake). Adds a "Defect detection-tier classification" subsection (Q-13) pointing at the JUDGMENT-template fields. **Sub-theme B (coverage + wall-clock tooling):** adds a `$exclude_justifications` map to `cmd/scripts/coverage-thresholds.json` (each of the 61 excludes now has a written justification) plus `scripts/check-coverage-excludes.sh` lint + self-test (Q-5); adds `scripts/measure-integration-wallclock.sh` watermark emitter + self-test with the 20-min Phase-A/B-split trigger documented (Q-8). **Sub-theme C (defect signal):** adds `scripts/assertion-density.sh` (counts `t.Errorf`/`t.Fatalf`/`require.*`/`assert.*` per test file, warns below threshold — a cheap mutation-testing proxy) + self-test (Q-6); adds `detection_tier_actual`/`detection_tier_target` fields to the JUDGMENT decisions-log template at `Plans/prompts/04-per-slice-template.md` (Q-13); formalizes the fix-forward-rate metric at `docs/fix-forward-rate.md` (Q-14). **Sub-theme D:** ships the demoseed-convergence design doc `docs/adr/0008-demoseed-convergence.md` — scopes the `demoseed.ApplyMinimal()` path, does NOT migrate seeds (Q-17, P0-1). **Sub-theme E:** ships the ui-honesty promotion-path doc `docs/adr/0009-ui-honesty-promotion-path.md` — staged plan only, does NOT promote (Q-10, P0-2). The three new lint/measurement scripts are wired into the `justfile` and self-test locally; their CI hard-failure wiring is deferred to spillover slice (batch-166 reserves `.github/workflows/ci.yml`), mirroring the slice 369 → 391 precedent. No coverage floor lowered (P0-4); no integration-tier mocks (P0-3). **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/353-qa-strategy-tactical-round-1-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/353-qa-strategy-tactical-round-1.md`.

- **test(e2e): slice 388 — board-pack export end-to-end (closes slice 351 spillover).** Adds `web/e2e/board-pack-export-e2e.spec.ts`, the missing half of flow #12 in slice 351's e2e critical-flow coverage matrix (`docs/audits/351-e2e-critical-flow-coverage-matrix.md`): the existing `board-pack-detail.spec.ts` only exercises the UI render of a board pack, never the generate → render → download-PDF chain. Board-pack export is a v1 binary success-test flow (CLAUDE.md: "generate the next board pack from it"), flagged half-covered by both slice 333 Q-9 and the slice 351 matrix (P1, deferred there per anti-criterion P0-1). The spec models the full export chain: (1) **generate** — an in-page `fetch` POST `/api/board-packs` returns a DRAFT `BoardPack` carrying a known id (the `evidence-push-e2e.spec.ts` precedent: `page.request` is not page-route-intercepted, so the POST is driven via `page.evaluate` to flow through the page network); (2) **render** — navigate `/board-packs/{id}`, gated on the `getBoardPack` detail GET round-trip (slice 275 `waitForResponse`-before-`goto` pattern), assert the sticky `ExportBar` and its `export-pdf-link` anchor pointing at the slice-043 BFF passthrough `/api/board-packs/{id}/pdf`; (3) **download** — click the link and assert a `download` event fires with a `.pdf` suggested filename and `%PDF`-prefixed bytes. Two notable e2e mechanics, both documented inline: the PDF link carries `target="_blank"`, so the download surfaces on a popup context (`context.waitForEvent("download")`, not `page`), and every `route.fulfill` mock registers on the browser **context** rather than the page so the popup's PDF request is intercepted too. Follows the established `route.fulfill` mock convention (slice 351 P0-4) — the chromedp-backed PDF-bytes render (slices 340/341) is server-side and already pinned by the chromedp specs, so per the slice doc the spec is scoped to the BFF generate + download-trigger boundary. Mocked-payload schema-conformance follows the slice 276 lesson (the DRAFT pack satisfies the producer-side `BoardPack` type, with a section for every `BOARD_PACK_SECTION_KEYS` entry). Deterministic (AC-3): no sleeps, auto-waiting assertions, `waitForResponse`/`waitForEvent` gates; validated locally 3/3 under `--repeat-each=3` (3 parallel workers, confirming `fullyParallel` safety). ADDITIVE — does not re-implement `board-pack-detail.spec.ts`, and does not touch the board-narrative AI-assist surface (constitutional; out of scope). **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/388-e2e-board-pack-export-end-to-end.md`.

- **test(e2e): slice 389 — multi-tenant JWT harness + real-RLS cross-tenant-leak spec (closes slice 351 spillover).** Extends the env-gated test-only `POST /v1/test/issue-jwt` endpoint (`internal/api/testissuejwt.go`) to mint a genuine MULTI-tenant JWT: two new OPTIONAL request fields — `available_tenants[]` and `roles_by_tenant` — let the e2e harness mint a JWT whose `atlas:available_tenants[]` spans 2 tenants (the shape the RFC 8693 token-exchange tenant-switch requires). When `available_tenants` is omitted the minted JWT is single-tenant, byte-identical to the slice-201 shape (strictly backward compatible). The extension does NOT widen the production attack surface (P0-201-4): the endpoint is still gated by `ATLAS_TEST_MODE` at both mount time (`httpserver.go`) and request time, still signs through the single `s.jwtSigner` (no parallel test-only signer), and adds a NEW constraint — `tenant_id` MUST be a member of `available_tenants` (rejected 400 otherwise), mirroring `jwt.Validate`'s tenant-scope invariant; malformed UUIDs in the set are also 400. New Go unit tests cover the multi-tenant mint, single-tenant backward compat, the current-tenant-not-in-available 400 guard, and the malformed-UUID 400. Adds the real-Postgres-RLS spec `web/e2e/tenant-switch-rls.spec.ts` (the depth assertion slice 351's mocked `tenant-switch.spec.ts` deferred here, per slice 333 Q-9): it seeds a known "canary" risk in tenant A only (`fixtures/e2e/tenant-switch.sql` + new `seed.ts` fixture name), authenticates a multi-tenant user, asserts the canary IS visible while current_tenant = A, switches to tenant B via the REAL token-exchange (BFF → `/oauth/token`), and asserts the canary is NOT visible in the tenant-B view through genuine PostgreSQL Row-Level Security — constitutional invariant #6, the v1 binary tenant-isolation criterion, asserted through the real stack rather than a `route.fulfill` mock. The slice-351 mocked spec is unchanged (stays the fast flow-level gate). **Load-bearing judgment (decisions log D4):** the spec mints with `super_admin: true` because super_admin is an OPA-authz + token-exchange-allowlist concept ONLY — it does NOT bypass PostgreSQL RLS (jwtmw sets `app.current_tenant` from the verified claim regardless, P0-190-3), so the cross-tenant-leak assertion is exactly as strong while giving the synthetic (no `user_roles`) test user a clean authz read path; a super_admin=false synthetic JWT would 403 at OPA before RLS ran, masking the test. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/389-multi-tenant-jwt-rls-leak-spec-decisions.md` (D1 optional-fields over a second endpoint; D2 production-safety review; D3 risks-not-controls assertion target; D4 super_admin-vs-RLS reasoning; D5 tenants identity-row seed; D6 responsive duplicate-title disambiguation; D7 does-not-replace-351). Slice doc: `docs/issues/389-e2e-multi-tenant-jwt-real-rls-leak-spec.md`.

- **refactor(api): slice 369 — consolidate `writeJSON`/`writeError`/`writeServerErr` into `internal/api/httpresp` (closes slice 328 H-1).** Slice 328's code-review audit (finding H-1) surfaced 103 byte-identical response-helper declarations across 50+ `internal/api/*` packages: 40 `writeJSON`, 45 `writeError`, 18 `writeServerErr` (the slice doc's "101" headline under-counted the two `status`-rename `writeJSON` variants — decisions log D6). New package `internal/api/httpresp` (mirror of slice 367's `internal/api/httperr`) exports `WriteJSON(w, status, body)` and `WriteError(w, status, msg)`; the two packages partition the response surface with zero overlap — `httpresp` owns 2xx + 4xx (success bodies, user-input errors), `httperr` owns 5xx (generic body + `request_id` + slog, CWE-209). 1131 call sites (`writeJSON` 240, `writeError` 771, `writeServerErr` 120) rewritten via `gofmt -r` AST-exact rewrites; all 103 declarations deleted. The 18 `writeServerErr` adapters were all trivial `httperr.WriteInternal` delegates (none doing sentinel-mapping work per P0-369-3) and now call `httperr.WriteInternal` directly, closing slice 367 §D2's deferred follow-up. Parameter standardised on `status int` (net/http convention) over the legacy `code int` majority (D1). **Pure refactor — no wire change (P0-369-1):** the new `internal/api/httpresp/httpresp_test.go` pins the exact byte stream (trailing `json.Encoder` newline, `{"error": msg}` envelope, JSON escaping) at 100% statement coverage; the bodies were verified byte-identical before migration. **Regression guard:** new `cmd/scripts/duphelper-lint` analyzer (mirror of slice 367's `errleak-lint`: `golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis` + `analysistest` fixture) rejects any new package-local `writeJSON`/`writeError`/`writeServerErr` free-function declaration in `internal/api/*` (methods and the exported shared names are not flagged); wired into the `justfile` `lint` target as `lint-duphelper`. **AC-5 contract locks:** `internal/api/metrics/handlers_test.go`'s two response-shape tests now assert `httpresp.WriteJSON`/`WriteError` directly, and `internal/api/board/integration_test.go`'s 400-path test gains explicit `application/json` + `{"error": ...}` envelope assertions. `internal/api/policyacks`'s now-orphaned local `errorBody` struct removed (D7); `internal/api/evidence`'s `errorBody` kept (still used for richer 4xx envelopes via direct `httpresp.WriteJSON`). No coverage floor regresses — removing covered trivial statements never lowers a package's percentage (D9), so the slice-069 ratchet is untouched. **AC-4 partial in this PR:** the analyzer's CI hard-failure step is deferred to spillover slice 391 (`docs/issues/391-duphelper-lint-ci-wiring.md`) because batch-164 reserved `.github/workflows/ci.yml` for slice 345 this batch; the local `just lint` guard enforces it today. **Type: JUDGMENT** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/369-httpresp-shared-helper-consolidation-decisions.md` (D1 `status int`; D2 httpresp/httperr clean partition + static-500-message edge case; D3 retire-all-18 writeServerErr; D4 analyzer reject-all-from-day-one; D5 CI wiring deferred to slice 391; D6 count reconciliation; D7 errorBody disposition; D8 AC-5 test rewiring; D9 coverage). Slice doc: `docs/issues/369-httpresp-shared-helper-consolidation.md`.

- **ci: slice 345 — integration-job enrolment-discovery guard (closes slice 334 I-1).** Adds `scripts/audit-integration-enrolment.sh` plus a new blocking `integration-enrolment-check` CI job (mirrors the `actions-pin-check` / `openapi-drift-check` self-contained meta-lint shape: unconditional, sub-second, pure bash + grep, no `changes.code` gate). The guard greps `internal/` for `//go:build integration`, derives the set of package directories, and diffs it against the `./internal/<pkg>/...` entries enumerated in the integration job's "Run integration tests" `go test` invocation; it fails (naming the offending package) when a tagged package is enrolled in neither the `ci.yml` list nor the script's `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` allowlist. This closes slice 334 framework-audit finding I-1 — the gap that lets an `integration_test.go` ship and silently run no integration tests in CI (the empirical cost being the 17-slice retroactive-enrolment trail: 279, 283, 284, 287, 288, 290, 293, 294, 295, 297, 310, 313, 315, 317, 318, 319, 320). Building the guard surfaced the live state it was designed to catch: **38 packages carry the build tag but are absent from the `ci.yml` list** (e.g. `internal/api/oauth` with 28 integration test functions, `internal/exception` with 22, `internal/auth/oidc`), confirming slice 348's independent `excludes` audit. Per anti-criterion P0-345-1 (no retroactive enrolment here), the 38 are recorded in the `KNOWN_UNENROLLED` allowlist as a documented, dated, monotonic-shrink ratchet; draining them is spillover slice 390. The guard still fails on the **next** forgotten package. Companion self-test `scripts/audit-integration-enrolment_test.sh` (synthetic positive + negative fixtures, 12 assertions) runs as the first step of the CI job. The integration job's package list is **unchanged** by this slice (AC-4); no test file is modified (P0-345-2); `CLAUDE.md` / canvas untouched (P0-345-3). Documents the rule in `CONTRIBUTING.md` ("Integration-test enrolment": ship `integration_test.go`, also add to the `ci.yml` integration list) and adds a `just audit-integration-enrolment` task. **Type: AFK** — decisions log `docs/audit-log/345-ci-integration-enrolment-discovery-decisions.md` (D1 shape: standalone shell script per `audit-rls.sh` precedent; D2 the known-gaps allowlist + slice-390 spillover; D3 normalization keying on the `internal/` segment; D4 blocking-not-advisory). Slice doc: `docs/issues/345-ci-integration-job-enrolment-discovery.md`.

- **docs: slice 344 — CLAUDE.md tone-discipline expansion (closes slice 337 spillover).** Carries the three additions the slice 337 AI-writing tone audit proposed for `CLAUDE.md` but deliberately did not apply (per P0-337-6). Adds a "Repetition discipline" sub-bullet to the board-narrative tone-discipline section (vary recurring terminology in adjacent occurrences; the project's domain jargon — "load-bearing", "first-pass", "tracer-bullet", "diligence the diligence tool" — is canonical, but flag if a single term appears 3+ times in one document). Adds a "Project-specific exceptions" note documenting "harness" as a literal technical term (slice 178's UI honesty audit Playwright harness), reclaiming it from the persona's Tier 2 AI-ism list. Adds a forward-looking note to the AI-assist boundary section recording that the banned-phrase list is documented but not yet runtime-enforced — the v1 board narrative is template-only (`internal/board/narrative.go`), and slice 182's v2 LLM call site must wire the list into the system prompt when it lands. Additions only — the existing banned-phrase list is unchanged and the constitutional AI-assist boundary is untouched. JUDGMENT-type slice; decisions log at `docs/audit-log/344-claude-md-tone-discipline-expansion-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/344-claude-md-tone-discipline-expansion.md`.

- **docs(canvas): slice 342 — vision tone rewrite (banned phrase + em-dash saturation, closes slice 337 high-density finding).** Tone-only rewrite of `Plans/canvas/01-vision.md`, the project's operator-facing positioning artifact. Removes the CLAUDE.md banned phrase "best-in-class" from the SimpleRisk line (§1.2 OSS prior art), replacing it with a specific, falsifiable claim ("SimpleRisk: narrow scope, well-regarded risk register, supports import"). Triages em-dash saturation from 22 (15 per 1000 words) down to 1 (0.7 per 1000), under the persona hard max of 1 per 1000; the single retained em-dash is the §1.5 heading term-gloss ("Replacement-grade — measurable acceptance criteria"), kept as a load-bearing definition separator. No structural-argument, persona, non-goal, or anti-pattern content changed — the diff is line-for-line tone only (17 lines). JUDGMENT-type slice; decisions log at `docs/audit-log/342-vision-canvas-tone-decisions.md` records the per-em-dash triage and the banned-phrase replacement rationale. Closes the sole high-density file from the slice 337 AI-writing tone audit.

### Fixed

- **fix(web): slice 691 — refresh vendor detail after recording a review (closes 691).** After an operator recorded a vendor review, the read-only vendor detail (`/vendors/{id}`) the app redirects back to served the **cached pre-review** vendor — the derived review-status badge (`overdue` → `on time`, computed at render from `last_review_date` + `review_cadence`), the "Last review" field, and the slice-688 review-history timeline all stayed stale until a hard reload. **Root cause:** the detail, edit, and record-review surfaces share the TanStack keys `["vendor", id]` + `["vendor-reviews", id]`, the global `QueryClient` default carries `staleTime: 60_000` (`web/lib/queryClient.tsx`), and neither review-recording path invalidated after its mutation — so on nav-back within the 60s window the on-mount refetch was suppressed and the cached body painted. **Fix (frontend-only):** both review-recording surfaces now invalidate the relevant query keys on the mutation success path before `router.push(`/vendors/{id}`)`. The **edit form** (`web/app/(authed)/vendors/[id]/edit/page.tsx` `onSubmit`, the slice-424 path that sets `last_review_date`) and the dedicated **record-review form** (`web/app/(authed)/vendors/[id]/reviews/new/page.tsx`, the slice-688 ledger-append surface) both `await Promise.all([invalidate(["vendor", id]), invalidate(["vendor-reviews", id])])` (awaited so the refetch is in flight before navigation — avoids the slice-424 shared-key read-back race; no fixed timeout) and fire `["vendors"]` + `["vendors-burndown"]` prefix invalidations (the list/burndown keys carry filter params, partial-key match marks every variant stale) without blocking nav, so the list has no stale overdue row on return to `/vendors`. Recording a review updates `vendors.last_review_date` server-side (`internal/vendor/reviews.go`, the most-recent-`reviewed_at` recompute), so the badge re-derives off the refetched row — no schema change. **JUDGMENT D1 — fix BOTH surfaces:** the slice doc named only the edit-form path (filed as a slice-424 spillover, before slice 688's `reviews/new` form landed), but the slice title ("after recording a review") and the audit intent cover the dedicated record-review form too; both append a review and route back, both had the gap, so both are fixed. **AC-1/AC-2/AC-3/AC-4:** the slice-424 e2e (`web/e2e/vendor-review-workflow.spec.ts`) now asserts the post-record refresh as a guaranteed contract — the deferred derived-status flip (`overdue` → `on time`) and the updated Last-review field after the edit-form save, plus a new `slice 691: recording a review on reviews/new refreshes the detail` test that drives the record-review form end-to-end (stateful hermetic mock: `/api/vendors/{id}` flips overdue→on-time and `/api/vendors/{id}/reviews` empty→one-row after the POST) and asserts the flipped badge, updated Last-review date, and the new timeline row appear without a hard reload. **No wire/schema/backend change** (`git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- internal/ migrations/ proto/ cmd/` is empty). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/691-vendor-detail-refresh-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/691-vendor-detail-refresh-after-review.md`.
- **fix(calendar): slice 732 — exception event labels show SCF code + name, not raw UUID (closes 732).** The `/calendar` agenda + month-grid and the `/dashboard` "Upcoming" panel rendered an expiring-exception event as **"Exception on control 32e55da9-…"** — the raw control UUID (UI-audit **ATLAS-035**, 2026-06-10; spillover from slice 670, which fixed every `web/`-side copy defect but could not reach this one because the title is constructed in **backend SQL**). The desired form matches the slice-670 ATLAS-012 control-detail wording: **"Exception on AAA-01 — Access Control Policy"** (SCF code + control name). **Fix (SQL is the single source of truth — no Go string-building, parity with the audit/policy/vendor branches):** the exceptions branch of the unified-calendar query (`internal/db/queries/calendar.sql`) and the matching branch of the dashboard "Upcoming" rollup (`internal/db/queries/dashboard.sql` — the two share the slice-675 upcoming-event vocabulary, so the label must read identically on both) now `JOIN controls` on the tenant-scoped composite `(tenant_id, control_id)` FK target and resolve the SCF code. **JUDGMENT D1 — label format + two-source code resolution:** the title is `Exception on <code> — <control name>`; the code is `COALESCE(NULLIF(controls.scf_id, ''), scf_anchors.scf_id)` — the free-form code stamped on the control row, else the global catalog code reached through the structured `controls.scf_anchor_id` FK (slice 009 linkage; `scf_anchors` is the platform-global catalog with no tenant_id/RLS, so the `LEFT JOIN` leaks nothing cross-tenant — invariant #6). **D2 — graceful fallback (AC-2):** when neither code resolves, the title falls back to the control name alone (`Exception on <control name>`) — it **never** prints a bare UUID. The handler (`internal/api/calendar/handler.go`) + ICS export (`internal/api/calendar/ics.go`) pass `row.Title` through unchanged, so the ICS `SUMMARY` inherits the improved label automatically; the `web/` components render `title` verbatim and needed **no change**. **Proof:** sqlc regenerated (`internal/db/dbx/{calendar,dashboard}.sql.go`); new pure-Go ICS unit tests assert an exception `SUMMARY` carries the code+name and the no-SCF-code fallback, with no raw UUID; new real-Postgres integration tests (`internal/api/calendar/integration_test.go`, `internal/api/dashboard/integration_test.go`) seed an exception on a control with a known SCF code and assert the returned event title resolves the code+name (and the anchor-FK fallback path) and contains no control UUID. The `internal/api/calendar` package holds its coverage floor (81.3% ≥ 79). **No migration / no wire-shape change** (the `title` field stays a single `string`; only its content improves — `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- migrations/ proto/` is empty). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/732-calendar-exception-scf-label-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/732-calendar-exception-event-raw-control-uuid.md`.
- **feat(demoseed): slice 682 — anchor demo controls to SCF + seed framework requirements/STRM edges for posture (closes 682).** A seeded demo tenant's dashboard **"Framework posture"** tiles rendered **"No active framework versions yet"** because the demo seed broke the SCF-anchor coverage spine (constitutional invariant #1) in two places (UI-audit **ATLAS-037**, root-caused in slice 671 decisions log D4): (1) `writeControls` set `scf_id` (free-form TEXT) but never `scf_anchor_id` (the UUID FK `FrameworkPosture` joins on), so the `covering_control` CTE found zero controls; and (2) the demo framework version had no `framework_requirements` and no `fw_to_scf_edges` (STRM edges), so the `version_reqs`/`req_anchor` CTEs were empty. **Fix:** a new `writeFrameworkPostureSpine` (`internal/demoseed/writers.go`), run from `Seeder.Apply` after `writeAuditPeriodsAndSamples`, builds the full spine — `framework_requirements` + `fw_to_scf_edges` (STRM `equal`, `source_attribution='org_internal'`) — on a tenant-scoped demo `framework_version` (`status='current'`) and back-fills `controls.scf_anchor_id` (round-robin over the chosen anchors). **JUDGMENT D1 (AC-4) — self-contained, adopt-when-present:** the seed builds the whole spine self-contained so the dashboard renders even when the global SCF catalog (slice-006 import, `tenant_id IS NULL` anchors) is **not** loaded — the state of the CI integration DB and a fresh self-host; when the global catalog **is** present, the seed **adopts** its real `scf_anchors` (resolved by `scf_id`) for the edges rather than duplicating them, and never writes or mutates the global catalog (AC-4 / invariant #6). The synthesized anchors use **real SCF control codes** (IAC-06, CRY-05, BCD-11, TDA-09, TPM-03, MON-01, VPM-02, IRO-04) so the demo anchors to real SCF controls, never a parallel taxonomy (invariant #7); mappings go requirement → SCF anchor via STRM edge, never requirement → requirement (invariant #1, #7). **D2 —** 8 anchors with 2 deliberately left uncovered so the tile renders a realistic partial coverage (~75%, not a flat-green wall). **Idempotency (AC-5):** every spine row CASCADE-deletes off the tenant-scoped demo `framework_version` that `Teardown` removes, so a re-seed rebuilds the spine with identical cardinality. **Proof:** new integration tests (`internal/demoseed/posture_integration_test.go`) — `TestApply_FrameworkPostureRendersCoverage` runs the production `FrameworkPosture` query through the BYPASSRLS pool and asserts ≥1 row with a realistic `0 < coverage_pct < 100` (fails on the pre-682 seed: 0 anchored controls, 0 STRM edges) and `TestApply_PostureSpineIdempotent` asserts the STRM-edge count is unchanged across teardown + re-apply; pure-Go `TestDemoSCFAnchors_Invariants` pins the anchor fixture (real FAM-NN codes, no duplicates). The adopt branch is verified to resolve a real global anchor and create **no** duplicate. The existing AC-4 teardown test (global catalog count unchanged) stays green. **No migration** (`scf_anchors`, `framework_requirements`, `fw_to_scf_edges`, `controls.scf_anchor_id` all exist — `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- migrations/ proto/` is empty). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/682-demo-posture-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/682-demo-seed-posture-scf-anchors.md`.
- **fix(vendors): slice 679 — vendor UX/data: row separation, delete control, owner-email validation (closes 679).** Clusters three vendor-surface defects re-verified on `main` build `2a3805b` in the demo tenant (audit items **ATLAS-030/031/032**). **(030)** Vendor-list rows rendered name and domain with the domain as an inline `ml-2` span that read as one concatenated string ("Pinecone Bank**pinecone**bank.example") at narrow widths; the cell now stacks them `flex flex-col` — name as primary text, domain as secondary `text-muted-foreground` on its own line — each with a stable `data-testid` (`vendor-name`/`vendor-domain`). **(031)** The Edit-vendor copy promised "Delete removes the row and its cell bindings" but the page had only "Save changes" — no Delete control. **JUDGMENT D2 — ADD the control (not remove the copy):** the backend delete already existed end-to-end (`Store.Delete` RLS-scoped via `inTx`→`ApplyTenant`, CASCADE on `vendor_scope_cells`; `DELETE /v1/vendors/{id}` handler + `httpserver.go` mount + integration coverage from slice 287) — only the BFF forward, client action, and UI control were missing. A new `DeleteVendorButton` (the established slice-097 controlled `Dialog`, same pattern as `admin/demo/demo-controls.tsx`) gates the destructive mutation behind a **mandatory confirm** — the DELETE fires only from the dialog's confirm button, never the trigger (anti-criterion); cell-binding cleanup is the platform CASCADE (no orphans). The BFF `DELETE` (`web/app/api/vendors/[id]/route.ts`) keeps the bearer httpOnly and forwards to the RLS-scoped endpoint. **(032)** The "Owner (email)" field was seeded with **"Head of Security"** — a role, not an email, and unvalidated. **JUDGMENT D1 — keep email, validate, fix seed (not relabel):** the label, the `alice@example.com` placeholder, and the slice-139 export's `MaskEmail` masking all already read the field as an email, so relabelling would unwind three surfaces. A new pure `web/lib/email.ts` (`isEmail`, vitest-pinned 100% with a per-file coverage floor added same-PR) rejects a present-but-malformed owner before the network call (empty stays allowed — the field is optional); the demo seed now stamps `fictionalUserEmail(i)` (`@demo.example`) instead of the role string. **AC-3 (read-only vendor detail / review history) deferred** as larger than this slice — captured as follow-on **slice 686**. **Proof (AC-5):** vitest pins the email predicate (rejects the "Head of Security" regression + role/bare-username/trailing-@/whitespace cases); Go `TestSeededVendorOwnerIsEmail` asserts every seeded vendor owner is a valid `@demo.example` address and none is a role label; a new `TestDeleteVendor_RLSIsolatesCrossTenant` integration test proves Tenant B's delete of Tenant A's vendor is a no-op (RLS-scoped — A's row survives); Playwright `web/e2e/vendors-ux-data-679.spec.ts` (route-mocked hermetic per slice 594) asserts name/domain render as separate exact-text elements and that the DELETE fires only after confirm (`deleteCalled === false` on dialog-open, `true` after confirm). **No migration** (DELETE on existing tables) — `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- migrations/ proto/` is empty. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/679-vendor-ux-data-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/679-vendor-ux-data-fixes.md`.
- **fix(risks): slice 681 — sidebar "Risks N" badge reads as high-severity, not a total (closes 681).** The sidebar Risks badge showed a rose "10" while the register held 20+ risks (audit **ATLAS-036**, 2026-06-10). The badge already **correctly** counts only high-severity (`severity >= 15`) risks and already carried the right `aria-label` ("N high-severity risks"), but **nothing visual or on-hover** conveyed that, so a sighted operator read it as a total count. **Fix (presentation only — the threshold + count logic are unchanged, anti-criterion):** the badge now renders a small leading **"▲" marker glyph** (reads as "elevated / attention", not "tally") plus a `title` (hover) attribute matching the `aria-label`, both reading "N high-severity risk(s)" with correct singular/plural — so the disambiguation is reachable by sighted-hover **and** assistive tech. A pure `highSeverityBadgeLabel` helper (`web/components/shell/sidebar-counts.tsx`) holds the wording, vitest-pinned. **Count liveness (the "is it live?" question):** the badge IS live — `RisksCountBadge` polls via TanStack Query `refetchInterval: 60_000` (slice 214), so adding a high-severity risk surfaces within one 60s refresh tick; the audit's "didn't update after a 21st risk" is consistent with that cadence (the 21st risk was not high-severity, or the look was inside the 60s window). The 60s cadence is documented (decisions log D3) and unchanged. **AC-3** the badge affordance reads as high-severity; **AC-4** vitest asserts the label always says "high-severity" (never "total") with correct singular/plural, and the hermetic Playwright spec asserts the rendered badge's `aria-label` + `title`. **Anti-criterion honored:** does NOT change the high-severity threshold that drives the badge — only its presentation. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/681-risk-register-ux-decisions.md`.
- **fix(metrics): slice 677 — metrics correctness (freshness consistency, count labeling, neutral no-target badge) (closes 677).** Three metric-display correctness defects re-verified on `main` build `2a3805b` in the demo tenant, bundled because they share the metrics surface. **(ATLAS-020, high)** The dashboard freshness widget read **100% within window** while the Metrics view read **Evidence freshness = 0.0%** for the same tenant. **Root cause:** both surfaces ultimately derive from the same `evidence_freshness` read model (one row per (tenant, control)), but the dashboard read it **live** (`GET /v1/evidence/freshness`) while the metrics view rendered the **latest stored metric observation** — a point-in-time 0.0% snapshot captured by the metrics scheduler **before** slice 671 wired seed-evaluation to populate the read model. The fix removes the second read path: a new single-source-of-truth helper `web/lib/api/freshness-consistency.ts` (`freshnessPctFromReport`) is the **one** definition both surfaces use — the dashboard subtitle's `computeFreshnessPct` delegates to it, and the metrics board KPI card (`web/components/dashboards-metrics/board-metric-card.tsx`) reads the **live** `FreshnessReport` (sharing the dashboard's TanStack query key) for its headline value + badge, so the two surfaces **cannot** disagree by construction. The stored-observation series still backs the trend sparkline. **(ATLAS-021, medium)** The dashboard freshness widget counted over **50** ("0 of 50") while the evidence ledger reported **200** ("Showing 50 of 200"), reading as a 4× contradiction. They count genuinely different populations — the freshness widget counts **controls** (latest-per-control verdicts), the ledger counts all append-only evidence **records** (invariant #2). This is a **labeling** fix: the freshness panel (`web/components/dashboard/evidence-freshness-panel.tsx`) now labels its unit as "controls" ("{n} of {total} controls have their latest evidence past its freshness window. (Controls, not ledger records — the evidence ledger counts every append-only record.)") so the two no longer read as a discrepancy. **(ATLAS-023, medium)** A metric with value 0.0% / 0 and "no target set" rendered a green **"on target"** pill — actively misleading on a board surface read at face value. **JUDGMENT D1:** `thresholdBadgeColor` (`web/lib/api/metrics.ts`) now returns **`neutral`** (not green) when there is no target row or no `target_value`; green is reserved exclusively for value-meets-target. **D2:** the two neutral cases are disambiguated by copy via a new pure `neutralBadgeLabel` helper — no observation → "no data", value-but-no-target → "no target" — both the same muted badge, never green. **AC-4 proof:** vitest pins the badge branches (`web/lib/api/metrics.test.ts`, no-target → neutral; zero-value → neutral; `neutralBadgeLabel` cases) and the shared freshness definition (`web/lib/api/freshness-consistency.test.ts`, including a `test.each` asserting the dashboard `computeFreshnessPct` equals `freshnessPctFromReport` for every input — the consistency contract), with a per-file coverage floor added same-PR; Playwright `web/e2e/metrics-correctness-consistency.spec.ts` (hermetic, route-mocked BFF per slice 594 — no seed-harness dependency) serves one fixed `FreshnessReport` to both surfaces and asserts the freshness % is **identical** on the dashboard widget and the metrics KPI, the freshness panel labels its population as "controls", and a no-target board metric renders a **neutral** badge ("no target"), never green. **Anti-criteria honored:** does **not** change the evidence ledger or the freshness-window definition (display + consistency + badge semantics only — `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- internal/ migrations/ proto/ cmd/` is empty), and never shows green for a genuinely-unmet or no-target metric. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/677-metrics-correctness-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/677-metrics-correctness-pass.md`.
- **fix(dashboard): slice 674 — render active tenant name in H1 + breadcrumb after switch (closes 674).** After switching tenants, the dashboard Program **H1** tenant chip (`web/components/dashboard/dashboard-header-subtitle.tsx`, slice 229) and the topbar **breadcrumb** (`web/components/shell/breadcrumb.tsx`, slice 223) kept rendering the **origin** tenant's name (e.g. "Default Tenant") while the tenant-switcher and Settings already showed the active tenant ("Demo Demo") — UI-audit **ATLAS-027** (2026-06-10), reachable only since slice #1245 made tenant-switching usable. **Root cause (not a wrong-source bug):** both surfaces already read the correct source (`/api/me/tenants` + the `current` flag, slice 192) but fetched **only on mount**. An in-tab switch rotates the `atlas_jwt` cookie and calls `router.refresh()`, which re-renders server components but does **not** re-run a mounted client component's mount effect — so the H1/breadcrumb held the origin name, while the `<TenantSwitcher>` updated because it alone re-fetches on the slice-199 `tenant-switched` broadcast. **Fix:** a shared `useCurrentTenantName` hook (`web/lib/auth/use-current-tenant-name.ts`) that fetches on mount **and** re-fetches on that same `tenant-switched` broadcast; the H1 (`TenantContext`) and `Breadcrumb` both route through it, so every tenant-name surface flips together with the switcher — no hard reload. The pure resolution logic (`pickCurrentTenantName` / `parseTenantsResponse`) is consolidated in `web/lib/auth/current-tenant.ts` (vitest-pinned 100%, per-file coverage floor added same-PR; `breadcrumb.tsx` re-exports `pickCurrentTenantName` for its existing test). **AC-1/AC-2** the H1 + breadcrumb render the active tenant's name, resolved through the same `/api/me/tenants` `current`-flag source the switcher uses; **AC-3** audited the other tenant-name surfaces — the Settings `TenantSection` uses a TanStack Query so it re-resolves on its own navigation (not on-screen during a switch), and the `<TenantSwitcher>` already re-fetches on the broadcast, so the two mount-only chrome surfaces were the consistent fix target; **AC-4** Playwright `web/e2e/tenant-name-after-switch.spec.ts` (multi-tenant, route-mocked hermetic per slice 594) switches A→B and asserts the H1 chip **and** breadcrumb segment update from tenant A's name to tenant B's. **Anti-criterion honored:** name display only — zero change to tenant-switch semantics, the RFC 8693 token-exchange flow, or RLS scoping (`git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- internal/ migrations/ proto/ cmd/` is empty; the broadcast carries only an opaque nudge, re-validated by re-fetching the RLS-backed endpoint per P0-199-2). **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/674-dashboard-tenant-name-after-switch.md`.
- **fix(risks): slice 680 — data-quality + scoring clarity: audit labels, residual/severity headers, new-risk pending (closes 680).** Clusters three low-severity clarity/data findings re-verified on `main` build `2a3805b` (audit items **ATLAS-033**, **ATLAS-038**, **ATLAS-029**). **(033)** The demo seed labelled each audit period by loop index (`"SOC 2 <year> Q{i%4+1}"`), decoupling the quarter label from the actual date range — a "Q3" period could span 2026-02→05, and the rows ordered oddly. The label is now **derived from the period's own start date** via a pure `auditPeriodName(start)` helper (`internal/demoseed/fixtures.go`: Q1 = Jan-Mar … Q4 = Oct-Dec, start year), so the label and range can never contradict; periods emit oldest-first so order matches the labels. The `/audits` **"Framework version"** column rendered `framework_version_id.slice(0, 8)…` — a truncated UUID that reads as an opaque content hash (the auditor saw `e443f4b1…`). The LIST path now resolves a **readable label** ("SCF 2025.2"): a new `ListAuditPeriodsWithFrameworkByTenant` sqlc query LEFT JOINs `frameworks` + `framework_versions` (catalog tables under the existing `tenant_or_catalog` RLS policy — no schema change, the version string was already present), the period domain carries a `FrameworkLabel`, the wire gains `framework_label` (`omitempty`), and the cell renders it — **falling back** to the truncated UUID in mono when the label is absent (unresolved framework version). **(038)** Across seeded risks the same residual mapped to different severities, reading as a scoring inconsistency. **JUDGMENT — confirmed NOT a bug:** per canvas §6.2, **inherent severity** (likelihood × impact, before controls) and **residual** (inherent × (1 − control_effectiveness), after controls) are **independent axes** — two risks with the same inherent severity legitimately carry different residuals and vice-versa. The fix is **copy only**: the `/risks` headers now read **"Inherent severity"** and **"Residual (after controls)"**, each with a native `title` tooltip spelling out the relationship; the scoring model is unchanged (anti-criterion). **(029)** A freshly-created risk stored an empty `{}` residual and omitted `review_due_at` until the evaluator backfills them, so both cells rendered a bare **"—"** that reads as broken. A new `residualState` / `reviewDuePending` classifier (`web/app/(authed)/risks/filters.ts`) drives an explicit **"Pending evaluation"** affordance (with tooltip) instead — distinguishing not-yet-evaluated from a real score. **Proof (AC-4):** pure-Go `auditPeriodName` table tests + a property test pin the label↔range quarter correctness (`internal/demoseed/audit_period_name_test.go`), and the demoseed integration test now reads every seeded period back and asserts its label suffix matches the calendar quarter of its `period_start`; a pure-Go `frameworkLabel` test covers the join-label composition + fallbacks; vitest pins `frameworkVersionLabel`, `residualState`, and `reviewDuePending`; the contract golden gains `framework_label`; two hermetic Playwright specs (route-mocked BFF per slice 594) assert the new-risk "Pending evaluation" cells, the clarified headers, and the readable-vs-fallback framework-version rendering. **Anti-criteria honored:** does NOT change the risk scoring model (copy/headers only), does NOT change evaluator timing (only the pending affordance — slice 671 owns the demo eval-run), and does NOT touch the risk-methodology-default open question (column-label copy only). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/680-data-quality-scoring-clarity-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/680-data-quality-scoring-clarity.md`.
- **fix(risks): slice 684 — risks list header/footer count consistency (closes 684).** The `/risks` header read **"Showing {visible} of {rows} risks"** while the table footer read **"Showing 1–50 of 53"** with Previous/Next pagination — the identical contradiction slice 666 fixed on `/controls`, surfaced during that slice and captured as a follow-up: both strings shared the verb **"Showing"** but meant different things (header = filtered subset of the register total; footer = the current page's row range of the filtered set), so read together the header implied all rows were on screen while the footer paginated only the first page. The totals are correct — this is a **copy/semantics** fix, not a data bug. **JUDGMENT D1 — page-local module, not a shared generic:** mirroring slice 666's deliberate page-local seam (and this page's existing `filters.ts` per-page convention), the label lives at `web/app/(authed)/risks/count-label.ts` (`risksCountLabel`) rather than a shared `web/components/list/count-label.ts` — the noun differs per page ("risks" vs "SCF anchors") so a shared generic would be a thin noun-parameter wrapper, and option (b) would churn the just-merged /controls page for no functional gain; two consumers is the under-threshold "rule of three" case (extract on the third). **D2 — the header drops the verb "Showing" and becomes a plain COUNT** of the filtered register: `53 risks` unfiltered, `42 of 53 risks` filtered; the shared `<ListPagination>` footer keeps sole ownership of the "Showing M–N of TOTAL" page-range phrasing and is **left untouched** (anti-criterion). With the verb removed the header reads as a count and the footer as a range, so the two no longer collide; the filtered total drives the header (**AC-3**) and is the same number the footer paginates over, so they are mutually consistent. **D3 —** when nothing is filtered (`filtered === total`) the header collapses to the plain total ("53 risks") rather than the misleading "53 of 53", removing the "all of them are here" implication (**AC-2**). **Proof:** vitest `web/app/(authed)/risks/count-label.test.ts` (9 cases — pins that the header never emits "Showing", the unfiltered plain-total form, the "N of M" filtered form with the filtered count first, the 0-of-M and empty-register cases, and defensive clamping so the label can never read "60 of 53"), with a per-file coverage floor added in the same PR; Playwright `web/e2e/risks-count-consistency.spec.ts` (route-mocked hermetic per slice 594 — the `/api/risks` GET is fulfilled with 53 risks, no shared-seed dependency) asserts the header reads "53 risks" and **not** "Showing" while the footer reads "Showing 1–50 of 53" — the two non-contradictory. **Anti-criterion honored:** zero change to pagination page size or underlying counts — copy/semantics only; the shared `web/components/list/pagination.tsx` footer is untouched and no backend/wire/schema file changes (`git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- internal/ migrations/ proto/ cmd/` is empty). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/684-risks-count-semantics-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/684-risks-list-count-consistency.md`.
- **fix(dashboard): slice 667 — remove inert recent-activity filter chips + duplicated dev note (closes 667).** The All / Evidence / Controls / Approvals chips on the dashboard "Recent activity" card were **non-interactive** `<span>` elements (no handler) and each carried a developer-facing placeholder note — _"Filter chips activate once the activity endpoint widens beyond the evidence branch"_ — **duplicated 4× in the DOM** via `title` attributes (UI-audit **ATLAS-013**). An inert control with a "coming soon" tooltip is exactly the HONESTY-GAP anti-pattern the `web/e2e-audit/` harness exists to catch. **JUDGMENT D1 — HIDE, not WIRE:** the dashboard activity feed binds to `GET /v1/activity`, whose handler (`internal/api/dashboard/handler.go::Activity`) reads only `cursor` + `limit` and whose store (`store.go::ActivityFeed`) reads **only** the evidence branch (`ListEvidenceActivity`) — there is no `kind`/`source` filter param, so the "Controls"/"Approvals" chips had nothing to bind to and would always render an empty feed. Slice 669 (merged) defaulted the **standalone** `/activity` view to business events but did **not** touch the dashboard endpoint, its store, or its source table — wiring the dashboard chips still requires backend work. Per the slice's default lean ("don't ship inert controls") and the anti-criterion, the chips and their placeholder `title` are **removed**; the wire-it-later path (widen the endpoint + add a `?kind=` param, keeping the chip taxonomy consistent with slice 669) is captured as follow-up slice 685. **AC-2** no internal "activate once…" text remains in `title`/DOM (was 4×, now 0×); **AC-3** hidden path — no empty chip row renders, and the empty-state + populated-list paths are unchanged. **Proof:** the dashboard Playwright spec (`web/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts`) flips its `activity-filter-chip` assertion from `toHaveCount(4)` to a new `toHaveCount(0)` + `activity-feed-filters` count 0 + no-"activate once" body guard; the `web/e2e-audit/mockup-spec.json` manifest drops the now-removed `activity-feed-filters` / `activity-filter-chip` expected testids so the honesty harness stays accurate. **No wire/schema/backend change:** `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- proto/ migrations/ internal/` is empty (frontend-only). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/667-dashboard-activity-chips-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/667-dashboard-activity-chips-inert-dup-note.md`.
- **fix(risks): slice 663 — risk-create fresh-tenant mitigate dead-end (closes 663).** The Add-risk form (`/risks/new`) opened on **Treatment = "mitigate"**, which makes **Linked controls** required (canvas §6.1: mitigate ⇒ ≥1 linked control). On a **fresh tenant with zero instantiated controls** there is nothing to link ("No active controls in this tenant yet"), so the primary create flow **dead-ended for a brand-new operator** — a mitigate-treatment risk could not be created at all (UI-audit **ATLAS-006**). **JUDGMENT D1 — AC-2 option (a):** the form's _opening default_ treatment now lands on **`avoid`** instead of `mitigate`. `avoid` is the only treatment with **zero required satellite fields** (canvas §6.1: `accept` needs accepter + accepted_until; `transfer` needs instrument_reference; `mitigate` needs a linked control; `avoid` is status-only), and it matches the **server's own omitted-treatment safe-default** (`internal/api/risks/handlers.go` falls back to `RiskTreatmentAvoid`), so the default is consistent client-to-server with **no backend change**. Defaulting to `accept` was rejected because the form omits the accept satellite inputs, so it would just move the dead-end (server rejects `accept` without accepted_until + accepter); option (b) — relaxing the mitigate rule when zero controls exist — was rejected because it would diverge client validation from the server's still-strict `ValidateTreatment` (submit-then-500) and weaken a canvas §6.1 invariant for a problem a default solves. **AC-3 preserved:** the mitigate-requires-control rule is UNCHANGED — an operator who selects `mitigate` in any tenant still must link ≥1 control (client `validateRiskForm` + server `ValidateTreatment`), and the existing empty-state affordance in `control-multi-select.tsx` still guides a fresh operator to switch treatment. The opening default is pinned as an exported, documented `DEFAULT_TREATMENT` constant (`web/app/(authed)/risks/new/validate.ts`) so it is unit-testable without the React tree. **Proof:** vitest `web/app/(authed)/risks/new/validate.test.ts` pins that the default is not `mitigate`, is `avoid`, that an empty-tenant default-flow submit (zero linked controls) is valid (AC-1), and that switching to mitigate still requires a control (AC-3); Playwright `web/e2e/risks-create.spec.ts` adds an AC-1 regression guard — a fresh tenant (zero controls, `/api/controls-list` mocked empty) creates a risk through the **untouched** default flow (the control picker is not even rendered), posting `treatment=avoid` and routing back with the new row; the existing `risks-create-control-link.spec.ts` specs are updated to select `mitigate` explicitly now that it is no longer the opening default. **No wire/schema/backend change:** `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- proto/ migrations/ internal/` is empty (client-default fix only). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/663-risk-create-dead-end-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/663-risk-create-fresh-tenant-mitigate-dead-end.md`.
- **fix(controls): slice 666 — controls list header/footer count consistency (closes 666).** The `/controls` header read **"Showing 53 of 53 SCF anchors"** while the table footer read **"Showing 1–50 of 53"** with Previous/Next pagination — a contradiction (UI-audit **ATLAS-007**): both strings shared the verb **"Showing"** but meant different things (header = filtered subset of the catalog total; footer = the current page's row range of the filtered set), so read together the header implied all 53 were on screen while the footer paginated only the first 50. The total of 53 is correct — this is a **copy/semantics** fix, not a data bug. **JUDGMENT D1 — the footer keeps sole ownership of the "Showing M–N of TOTAL" page-range phrasing** (it is the shared `<ListPagination>` primitive used by /risks, /controls, /policies and is left untouched); **the header drops the verb "Showing" entirely and becomes a plain COUNT** of the filtered catalog: `53 SCF anchors` unfiltered, `42 of 53 SCF anchors` filtered. With the verb removed the header reads as a count and the footer reads as a range, so the two no longer collide; the filtered total drives the header (**AC-3**) and is the same number the footer paginates over, so they are mutually consistent. **D2 —** when nothing is filtered (`filtered === total`) the header collapses to the plain total ("53 SCF anchors") rather than the misleading "53 of 53", removing the "all of them are here" implication (**AC-2**). The wording + the filtered/total branch live in a new pure module `web/app/(authed)/controls/count-label.ts` (`controlsCountLabel`, mirroring the page's existing filters.ts/selection.ts pure-logic seams), vitest-covered to 100% with a per-file coverage floor added in the same PR. **Proof:** vitest `count-label.test.ts` (9 cases — pins that the header never emits "Showing", the unfiltered plain-total form, the "N of M" filtered form with the filtered count first, the 0-of-M and empty-catalog cases, and defensive clamping so the label can never read "60 of 53"); Playwright `e2e/controls-count-consistency.spec.ts` (route-mocked hermetic — the `/api/controls` GET is fulfilled with 53 anchors, no shared-seed dependency) asserts the header reads "53 SCF anchors" and **not** "Showing" while the footer reads "Showing 1–50 of 53" — the two non-contradictory. **Anti-criterion honored:** zero change to pagination page size or underlying counts — copy/semantics only; `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- internal/ migrations/ proto/ cmd/` is empty and the shared `web/components/list/pagination.tsx` footer is untouched. The sibling `/risks` page carries the identical header/footer contradiction; out of scope here and captured as a follow-up slice. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/666-controls-count-semantics-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/666-controls-list-count-consistency.md`.
- **fix(calendar): slice 675 — align calendar agenda event sources with the dashboard (closes 675).** The dashboard "Upcoming" widget listed audit periods + vendor reviews + exceptions, but the compliance calendar agenda (all filters checked) showed ONLY the exceptions, and the calendar legend had no "Vendor reviews" entry (UI-audit **ATLAS-034**). **Root cause:** the calendar's event-source `UNION ALL` (`internal/db/queries/calendar.sql` `ListCalendarEvents`) was authored under slice 094 with four branches (audit / exception / policy `next_review_at` / periodic control review) and **no vendor branch at all** — vendor reviews, a first-class item on the dashboard's `ListUpcomingItems` rollup (slice 066), were structurally absent from the calendar. The two surfaces answer the same "what's coming up?" question from two independently-authored queries that drifted. **Fix:** add a `vendor` `UNION ALL` branch to `ListCalendarEvents` that mirrors the dashboard's vendor cadence math (`last_review_date + review_cadence interval`), windowed like the calendar's other branches; thread `vendor` through the closed event-type vocabulary everywhere it lives — the handler's `validEventTypes` / `normalizeTypeFilter`, the frontend `CalendarEventType` union, the legend (`type-filter.tsx`, now "Vendor reviews"), the agenda + month-grid color/label maps, and the `linkFor` exhaustiveness guard (vendor events link to the real `/vendors/[id]` detail page). The `/v1/calendar.ics` feed inherits the vendor branch automatically (it renders directly off `ListCalendarEvents` rows), so the in-app agenda and the ICS feed stay consistent (**AC-4**). **DRY (AC-3):** the two queries are NOT merged — they intentionally differ on policy-event semantics (dashboard = policy-ack renewal; calendar = `next_review_at`) and on control reviews (calendar-only), and a literal "one query" would regress the dashboard (anti-criterion: do not change what the dashboard shows). Instead the shared **vocabulary** (the set of entity types each surface sources) is documented as a contract: [ADR-0015](docs/adr/0015-calendar-dashboard-upcoming-event-source-parity.md) plus cross-reference comments in both `.sql` files, so a future event-type change to one surface is a deliberate, reviewed decision rather than a silent drift. **Proof:** Go integration (`internal/api/calendar/integration_test.go`, real Postgres + RLS as `atlas_app`) — `TestCalendar_SurfacesVendorReviews` asserts a seeded vendor with `last_review_date` + cadence appears in the agenda with `type=vendor` linking to its vendor entity; `TestCalendar_AgendaSourcesAllDashboardTypes` (**AC-5**) seeds an audit period + a vendor review + an exception and asserts ALL THREE appear (was: exceptions only); the existing RLS-isolation, cadence-math, truncation, and ICS guards stay green. Go unit covers the vendor type-filter (`TestNormalizeTypeFilter_AcceptsVendor`); vitest covers the `linkFor` vendor branch; the quarantined Playwright calendar spec gains an `AC-675-1` legend + agenda contract. **No new wire/schema/migration:** the vendor branch reads the existing `vendors` table (RLS-enabled, slice 024) over the existing `/v1/calendar` route; `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- migrations/ proto/` is empty. **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/675-calendar-agenda-missing-events.md`.
- **fix(vendors): slice 664 — vendor review-burndown zero-vendor empty state (closes 664).** With zero vendors the Vendors "Review burndown" widget rendered **"100% ON-TIME / 0 vendors"** (UI-audit ATLAS-009) — an empty population is not 100% compliant. **Root cause:** the platform's on-time fraction is on-time / total with `total == 0` short-circuiting to `1.0` (`internal/vendor/store.go` `onTime()`), so the 0/0 read surfaced through the frontend's `Math.round(fraction * 100)` as a misleading 100%. **Fix (frontend-only, display guard):** a new pure formatter `formatOnTimeRate(total, fraction)` (`web/lib/api/vendor-burndown-format.ts`) returns the empty token **`—`** when the population is empty (`total <= 0`) and the rounded percent otherwise — it guards on the **population size**, NOT the fraction value, so a genuine 100%-on-time _populated_ tenant still renders "100%" (the slice anti-criterion: no change to the computation for populated tenants). The `BurndownCard` headline on-time stat AND every per-criticality band stat now route through the formatter, so a zero-vendor band in an otherwise-populated tenant is also honest. The "0 vendors" sub-count is unchanged and stays accurate. Framing matches the board-pack vendor-burndown empty-state honesty established by slices 273/662 (an empty burndown reads as empty, never "100%"). **Proof:** vitest `web/lib/api/vendor-burndown-format.test.ts` pins the zero-population empty token (not "100%"), the negative/non-finite guards, the _genuine_ populated 100% case (guard must not blank it), and the rounding behaviour; Playwright `web/e2e/vendors-burndown-empty-state.spec.ts` (route-mocked hermetic — both the `/api/vendors` list and `/api/vendors/burndown` GETs are fulfilled by the mock, no shared-seed dependency) asserts a zero-vendor tenant renders the `—` rate (never "100%") with an accurate "0 vendors" count, and a populated tenant still renders "75%" (no regression). A stable `data-testid` (`vendor-stat-<label>`) is added to each burndown `Stat` value as the spec's contract point. **No wire/schema/backend change:** `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- proto/ migrations/ internal/` is empty (the backend `onTime()` is intentionally left as-is, out of slice scope). **Type: AFK.** Slice doc: `docs/issues/664-vendor-burndown-empty-state-zero-vendors.md`.
- **feat(features): slice 660 — gate OSCAL + board-reporting nav and routes by feature flag (closes 660; resolves 659 exposure).** Admin → Features showed `oscal.export` = OFF and `board.reporting` = OFF ("Default off pending GA") and the page copy claimed **"Disabling a module hides its routes"**, yet the sidebar rendered **Vendor Claims** (`/oscal/component-definitions`) + **Board Packs** (`/board-packs`) UNCONDITIONALLY and the routes ignored the flags — a false contract that also exposed the edge-broken OSCAL page (UI-audit ATLAS-008; pairs with ATLAS-001/659/683). **JUDGMENT D1 — GATE, not re-copy:** the flags now actually gate both nav AND routes, so the stated contract becomes true and the pre-GA surfaces stop being exposed. **What ships:** (1) a NON-admin, tenant-scoped enabled-modules read `GET /v1/features/enabled` (`internal/api/features` `Enabled` handler) returning `{"modules":{"oscal.export":bool,"board.reporting":bool}}` for the caller's own tenant via `featureflag.Enabled` under RLS — the admin toggle surface `GET/PATCH /v1/admin/features` stays admin-only (no write path, no audit metadata leaked to non-admins); the canonical gating set lives in new `featureflag.GatingKeys`; (2) server-side NAV gating — `getAuthedNav()` resolves the enabled-modules read (BFF `/api/features/enabled`) and drops the gated nav entry for ALL users via a single pure `gateNavItems` helper (one source of truth for desktop sidebar + mobile drawer; fail-closed — a missing key hides the entry); (3) load-bearing ROUTE gating — the whole OSCAL module (list/get/accept/reject/needs-info/scf-anchor) and the whole board module (briefs + packs) are wrapped in the existing `featureflag.Gate` middleware, returning a clean **404 + {"error":"feature disabled"}** + `X-Feature-Disabled` header when off (JUDGMENT D3 — 404, indistinguishable from a non-existent route, NO internal-error leak per slice 367), so the API is genuinely UNREACHABLE not just nav-hidden; (4) the gated FE pages render a clean "module not enabled" panel (`FeatureDisabledState`) on a direct-nav 404 instead of a raw error; (5) the Features-page copy is now accurate (AC-4). **Proof:** Go integration (`internal/api/features/gate_integration_test.go`, real Postgres) — with the flag OFF (Seed default) the gated route returns **404 (not 200)**, the downstream sentinel never runs, the body is exactly `{"error":"feature disabled"}` (single key, no leak); with the flag toggled ON the route serves; **RLS isolation** — tenant A toggling `oscal.export` ON does not make the route reachable for tenant B (invariant #6); Go unit covers the non-admin `/v1/features/enabled` read (correct booleans, 401 without a credential, admin also permitted) + `GatingKeys` (all seeded, all default OFF); vitest covers `gateNavItems` (hides the right items, fail-closed) + `isFeatureDisabledError` + the `/api/features/enabled` BFF proxy (fail-closed on upstream error, bearer forwarded); Playwright (`web/e2e/feature-flag-gating.spec.ts`, route-mocked hermetic) asserts the default sidebar omits Vendor Claims + Board Packs and a direct-nav to a gated route shows the clean disabled panel (no raw error). **No wire/schema change:** flag DEFAULTS stay OFF pending GA (nothing auto-enabled); `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- proto/ migrations/` is empty (the new endpoint is reflected in `internal/api/openapi/routes.go` + the regenerated `docs/openapi.yaml`). **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/660-feature-flag-gating-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/660-feature-flag-route-nav-gating.md`.

- **feat(web): slice 662 — render board-pack vendor-burndown section + human-label publish blocker (closes 662; fixes 673).** On `/board-packs/{id}` the rendered sections jumped **§04 → §06** — **§05 (Vendor risk burndown) never rendered** — and the "Not ready to publish" blocker showed the **raw internal key `vendor_burndown`** among human labels (UI-audit ATLAS-005). **Root cause (twofold):** (1) **FE** — `SectionStructured` had no `case "vendor_burndown"` (fell through to `default: return null`, a blank §05 visual), the publish-gate built blocker labels with `section.title || key` (the raw key when the title was empty or the section missing), and the section loop did `if (!section) return null` (silently dropped a slot); (2) **data (AC-4)** — the slice-205 demo seed wrote `board_packs.content.sections` as a **JSON array** of five untyped objects (no `key` field), not the keyed `map[string]Section` shape `board.Pack` marshals to, so `json.Unmarshal` in `storedPackFromRow` errored and **500'd** the list + detail endpoints on the seeded tenant — **the same root cause as slice 673** (`GET /api/board-packs` 500). **What ships:** a minimal `VendorBurndownPanel` (`web/components/board-pack/vendor-burndown-panel.tsx`) — total high-criticality vendors, reviews on time (`onTime/total` + on-time %), past due — consuming the already-on-wire `vendor_burndown_total`/`_on_time`/`_past_due`/`_on_time_pct` fields (no wire/proto/migration change; honest `—`/zeros for an empty tenant, no fabrication); a FE `SECTION_TITLES` label map + `sectionLabel(key, section)` resolver (`web/lib/api/board.ts`) mirroring backend `sectionTitles`, resolving `SECTION_TITLES[key] ?? (section?.title || key)` so a **human label always renders** even for a missing or empty-title section (AC-2) — applied to the publish blocker and the section header; a `MissingSection` graceful "not generated" state for any absent fixed section (AC-3 — keeps numbering contiguous, treats a missing section as unapproved by its human label, never crashes on `section.approved`/`section.title`); and a **demo-seed fix** (`internal/demoseed/fixtures.go` `demoBoardPackContent`) writing the exact `board.Pack` envelope — a key→section **map** carrying all eight `SectionKeys` with titles, every section approved (the demo pack ships published), §05 vendor_burndown data populated — within slice-205 demo conventions (BYPASSRLS writer, correct `tenant_id`, `demo_seed_v` mark, fictional content). **Slice 673: fixed-by-662** — verified against a real Postgres: the new `TestApply_BoardPackSectionShape` integration test seeds the demo pack and asserts its `content` deserializes into `board.Pack` with all 8 sections + non-empty titles (the exact `storedPackFromRow` operation the list endpoint performs), and the `internal/board` board-pack integration suite (`List`/`GetByID`) is green. **Proof:** vitest `web/lib/api/board.test.ts` (12 cases — every key maps to a label; `sectionLabel` returns the human label for a missing/empty-title `vendor_burndown`, never the raw key; the blocker label logic never emits a raw snake_case key); Playwright `web/e2e/board-pack-vendor-burndown.spec.ts` (route-mocked hermetic — a complete pack renders §05 with title + visual and contiguous numbering with no raw key on the page; a pack missing §05 renders the graceful state and the blocker shows the human label, never the raw key); the new Go integration test. `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD -- proto/ migrations/ schemaregistry/` is empty. **Type: JUDGMENT.** Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/662-board-vendor-burndown-decisions.md`. Slice doc: `docs/issues/662-board-pack-vendor-burndown-section-missing.md`.
